The CIR panel found it safe when formulated to be non-sensitizing, but it can be irritating, especially near eyes, in high concentrations, and on sensitive or broken skin.

sources.label: CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Mentha piperita (Peppermint) Oil
PubMed: Final report on the safety assessment of Mentha Piperita Oil, Leaf Extract, Leaf, and Leaf Water

ingredients.name: Mentha Viridis (Spearmint) Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: The essential oil steam-distilled from spearmint leaves, composed mainly of carvone. It provides a fresh minty scent and mild cooling sensation, but as an essential oil it can irritate or sensitize sensitive skin, especially around the eyes or on broken skin.

sources.label: PMC — Medicinal Uses, Phytochemistry, Pharmacology, and Toxicology of Mentha spicata
CIR — Amended Safety Assessment of Mentha piperita-Derived Ingredients (related mint assessment)

ingredients.name: Meradimate
ingredients.note: Weak UVA anthranilate filter that the FDA has not found GRASE and that the EU SCCS recommended against using over safety data gaps. Considered an older, poorly characterized filter.

ingredients.name: Methenamine
ingredients.note: Slow formaldehyde releaser and contact allergen; EU-permitted up to 0.15% as a preservative. Concern stems from formaldehyde liberation and sensitization.

ingredients.name: Methyl 2-Octynoate
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a green violet-leaf odor; a potent sensitizer that is strongly IFRA-restricted. Relevant mainly for fragrance-reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Methylchloroisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Potent contact sensitizer, almost always used as the MCI/MI blend (Kathon CG). EU permits the 3:1 MCI/MI mixture only in rinse-off products at max 15 ppm.

ingredients.name: Methyleugenol
ingredients.note: Fragrance constituent found in essential oils that is both a contact sensitizer and a genotoxic carcinogen of regulatory concern, hence strictly IFRA-limited. Uncertain/mixed profile warrants a precautionary stance.

ingredients.name: Methylisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Major contact allergen named Contact Allergen of the Year (2013). Banned in EU leave-on cosmetics and capped at 15 ppm in rinse-off products.

ingredients.name: Methylparaben
ingredients.note: Weak estrogen-mimicking preservative. Usually labeled, so easy to avoid. Risk is cumulative; reversible on discontinuation.

ingredients.name: Michelia Alba Flower Oil
ingredients.note: White champaca essential oil very high in linalool (plus geraniol); oxidized linalool is a known skin sensitizer and an EU-listed fragrance allergen.

ingredients.name: Musk Ketone
ingredients.note: Nitromusk that is persistent and bioaccumulative; permitted only under tight EU concentration limits and largely phased out. Rated precautionary due to bioaccumulation and toxicity concerns.

ingredients.name: Musk Xylene
ingredients.note: Nitromusk that is persistent, bioaccumulative and an SVHC of very high concern; banned/restricted in EU cosmetics, so it should not appear in compliant products. Precautionary flag is false because it is effectively prohibited.

ingredients.name: Myristica Fragrans (Nutmeg) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Spice essential oil containing myristicin, which is toxic at high doses; potential for skin sensitivity at higher concentrations. Treated cautiously.

ingredients.name: Nickel
ingredients.note: The most common contact allergen worldwide, present as a trace contaminant in pigments and eye cosmetics; even low levels can trigger allergic contact dermatitis in sensitized users.

ingredients.name: Nonapeptide-1
ingredients.note: Synthetic biomimetic peptide acting as an alpha-MSH antagonist at MC1R to inhibit melanogenesis (skin lightening). Receptor-active depigmenting mechanism warrants precaution.

sources.label: PubMed: Enhanced skin benefits of EGCG loaded in nonapeptide-1-conjugated mesoporous silica nanoparticles to reverse skin photoaging
PMC: Alpha-Melanocyte Stimulating Hormone: An Emerging Anti-Inflammatory Antimicrobial Peptide

ingredients.name: o-Aminophenol
ingredients.note: Oxidative hair-dye coupler and skin sensitizer; SCCS-restricted in permanent hair-dye formulations.

ingredients.name: Ocimum Basilicum (Basil) Flower/Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Basil-derived material can contain estragole/methyl chavicol, a flagged sensitizer with toxicity concerns at high exposure. Treated cautiously as a potential sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Ocimum Basilicum (Basil) Oil
ingredients.note: Basil essential oil contains estragole (methyl chavicol), a genotoxic carcinogen flag, plus linalool/eugenol sensitizers; IFRA restricts high-estragole chemotypes. Treated as a precautionary fragrance allergen.

ingredients.name: Octinoxate
ingredients.note: UV filter shown to have estrogenic / weak anti-androgenic activity. Regulators still permit it up to ~10%, but for daily leave-on use a mineral filter avoids the question.

ingredients.name: Octocrylene
ingredients.note: Common photostabilizer and UVB/short-UVA filter that can degrade over time into benzophenone, a suspected endocrine disruptor and carcinogen. Detectable in plasma above FDA thresholds and a known contact/photo allergen.

ingredients.name: Octylisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Isothiazolinone contact allergen used as an in-can/material preservative. Not an approved cosmetic preservative in the EU and a recognized sensitization concern.

ingredients.name: Oligopeptide-1
ingredients.note: Recombinant human EGF used cosmetically to promote cell renewal. Treated cautiously because long-term safety and skin penetration of a proliferation-signaling growth factor are not well established.

ingredients.name: Origanum Majorana Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Marjoram leaf material can carry fragrance allergens (linalool, limonene, geraniol) and is reported as a dermal sensitizer/irritant. Treated as a precautionary sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Origanum Vulgare Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Oregano leaf extract rich in phenols carvacrol and thymol, with antimicrobial/antioxidant activity but known potential to irritate or sensitize skin, especially when concentrated. Treated as a precautionary sensitizer botanical.

ingredients.name: Oxybenzone
ingredients.note: Chemical sunscreen filter with endocrine activity in lab/animal studies and notable skin-absorption. Mineral filters (zinc oxide, titanium dioxide) are the clean swap.

ingredients.name: p-Aminophenol
ingredients.note: Oxidative hair-dye intermediate and known skin sensitizer; SCCS-restricted, with mutagenicity concerns at high exposure.

ingredients.name: p-Phenylenediamine
ingredients.note: Permanent hair-dye agent; potent allergen capable of severe reactions. Mainly hair, not face.

ingredients.name: Padimate O
ingredients.note: PABA-derivative UVB filter that has fallen out of favor over allergy, photo-allergenicity and potential to form nitrosamines or generate free radicals. Largely replaced by newer filters.

ingredients.name: Papain
ingredients.note: Proteolytic enzyme used for exfoliation. A recognized skin and respiratory sensitizer; can induce allergic sensitization through skin even when enzymatically inactivated.

ingredients.name: PEG-100 Stearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated stearate emulsifier (usually paired with glyceryl stearate). Safe as used, but PEG/ethoxylated ingredients can carry trace 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide residues, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: PEG-12 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Water-dispersible silicone surfactant; the linear silicone backbone is low concern, but the PEG (ethoxylated) portion can carry trace 1,4-dioxane unless stripped. Precautionary for the byproduct contaminant.

ingredients.name: PEG-40 Hydrogenated Castor Oil
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated castor oil used to solubilize fragrances and oils. Effective and broadly safe, but as an ethoxylate it may carry trace 1,4-dioxane.

ingredients.name: PEG-40 Stearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated emulsifier/solubilizer. Generally safe, but ethoxylation can leave trace 1,4-dioxane/ethylene oxide, warranting precaution.

ingredients.name: PEG-7 Glyceryl Cocoate
ingredients.note: Mild emollient surfactant; the PEG ester is well tolerated, but as an ethoxylated ingredient it may contain trace 1,4-dioxane (anticipated human carcinogen) unless stripped. Precautionary for the contaminant only.

ingredients.name: PEG-8
ingredients.note: Low-molecular-weight humectant/solvent that is itself low-hazard, but as an ethoxylated material it may carry trace 1,4-dioxane (an anticipated human carcinogen) unless purified. Tiered precautionary for the potential contaminant, not the PEG.

ingredients.name: PEG-8 Stearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated emulsifier that is itself low concern but can carry trace 1,4-dioxane from the ethoxylation process. The carcinogenic byproduct, removable by purification, is the reason for the precautionary tier.

ingredients.name: PEG-80 Sorbitan Laurate
ingredients.note: Mild PEG-based nonionic surfactant common in gentle and baby cleansers; being ethoxylated it can carry trace 1,4-dioxane unless purified. The surfactant itself is low-irritation.

ingredients.name: PEI-10
ingredients.note: A low-molecular-weight form of polyethylenimine polymer used in cosmetics (mainly hair products) as a film-former and conditioning agent. Higher-molecular-weight PEI forms are cytotoxic in cell studies; the shorter PEI-10 is considered much less toxic, but research specifically on PEI-10 dermal safety is limited — use cautiously.

sources.label: Identification of possible cytotoxicity mechanism of polyethylenimine – PubMed
Cytotoxic impacts of linear and branched PEI nanostructures – PubMed

ingredients.name: Pelargonium Graveolens Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Geranium essential oil used as fragrance/skin-conditioner. EFSA classifies it a skin and respiratory sensitizer; contains geraniol/citronellol EU fragrance allergens.

ingredients.name: Peppermint Oil
ingredients.note: Can cause contact allergy and perioral/hairline dermatitis; menthol and limonene are irritant and sensitizing in susceptible users.

ingredients.name: Peru Balsam
ingredients.note: Classic marker allergen on patch testing; rich in cinnamates and benzoates, so it is largely restricted in modern leave-on cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Phenethyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Aromatic alcohol used as preservative-booster, solvent and rose-scented fragrance. CIR safe to ~1%, but it is a fragrance ingredient that can sensitize fragrance-allergic individuals.

sources.label: CIR: Phenethyl alcohol safety assessment page
PubMed: RIFM fragrance ingredient safety assessment, phenethyl alcohol

ingredients.name: Phytonadione
ingredients.note: Vitamin K1 is used in cosmetics to help reduce the appearance of dark circles and bruising by supporting healthy blood coagulation in skin tissue. Rare cases of allergic contact dermatitis from phytonadione epoxide (an oxidation product) have been reported, especially from eye-area cosmetics — patch-test if sensitive.

sources.label: Emerging epidemic of allergic contact dermatitis to phytonadione epoxide – PubMed

ingredients.name: Piper Methysticum Leaf/Root/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Kava plant extract used as a skin conditioner; CIR found the data insufficient to support safety and topical use has been linked to skin sensitization and dermopathy. Flagged precautionary for uncertain/mixed evidence.

ingredients.name: Pogostemon Cablin Oil
ingredients.note: Patchouli essential oil used as fragrance/skin-conditioner. Considered an irritant and respiratory sensitizer; can irritate skin above recommended 0.5-2%.

ingredients.name: Pollen Extract
ingredients.note: Bee-collected pollen used as an antioxidant/soothing extract. A recognized cause of hypersensitivity reactions in bee/pollen-allergic individuals.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylamide
ingredients.note: Acrylamide-based polymer used as a thickener/stabilizer. The polymer is inert, but residual acrylamide monomer is a recognized neurotoxin/probable carcinogen, so SCCS limits residuals and it is flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Polyaminopropyl Biguanide
ingredients.note: A synthetic antimicrobial preservative used in cosmetics at up to 0.1%, which CIR considers safe at this concentration. However, the EU Scientific Committee on Consumer Safety (SCCS) flagged unresolved genotoxicity concerns and advises against its use in sprays; it is classified CMR 2 (possible carcinogen) under EU regulation, so caution is warranted—especially in aerosol or inhalable products.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Polyaminopropyl Biguanide as Used in Cosmetics (Final)
EU Health – Revision of the opinion on the safety of PHMB in cosmetic products
PubMed – SCCS 2nd Revision on the safety of PHMB in cosmetic products

ingredients.name: Polyethylene
ingredients.note: Solid polyethylene microbeads are an environmental microplastic concern and are being phased out under EU REACH restrictions. Restricted rather than a direct skin-safety hazard.

ingredients.name: Polysilicone-2
ingredients.note: A silicone polymer used as a hair conditioner and anti-foaming agent found in over 500 cosmetic products, but the CIR Expert Panel issued an 'insufficient data' notice for this specific ingredient because safety and toxicology studies have not been submitted. Until more data is available, its safety cannot be fully confirmed.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polymerized Tetramethylcyclotetrasiloxanes (includes Polysilicone-2)

ingredients.name: Potassium Carbonate
ingredients.note: This is an alkaline salt used in cosmetics as a pH adjuster and buffering agent. While it is a common inorganic ingredient, high concentrations are irritating to skin and eyes; at low concentrations used for pH adjustment it is considered safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Carbonate Salts as Used in Cosmetics (Final 2016)

ingredients.name: Potassium Persulfate
ingredients.note: Persulfate oxidizer in lightening powders; a respiratory sensitizer linked to asthma and a known contact allergen.

ingredients.name: PPG-26-Buteth-26
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic polyether glycol used as an emollient, solubilizer, and conditioning agent in cosmetics. The CIR Expert Panel determined in 2016 that available data were insufficient to fully support its safety, which means it carries a moderate level of uncertainty.

ingredients.name: Propolis
ingredients.note: Resinous bee-collected plant exudate and a well-known contact allergen, originally in beekeepers; increasingly seen with cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Propyl Gallate
ingredients.note: Gallate-ester antioxidant used to prevent rancidity of oils. Documented skin sensitizer; CIR limits it to 0.1% in cosmetics, hence cautionary.

ingredients.name: Propylene Glycol
ingredients.note: Common humectant and solvent; a weak but recognized contact allergen (ACDS Allergen of the Year 2018), so worth reducing for sensitive or eczema-prone skin. Safe for most users at typical levels.

ingredients.name: Propylparaben
ingredients.note: Endocrine-disruptor concern; among the more estrogenic parabens.

ingredients.name: Protease
ingredients.note: An enzyme that breaks down proteins in dead skin cells, acting as a gentle chemical exfoliant to reveal smoother skin. While generally well tolerated, proteases can cause irritation or allergic reactions in sensitive individuals, and enzyme activity in commercial products can vary widely.

sources.label: Wiley – An overview of the use of proteolytic enzymes as exfoliating agents (2022)
PubMed 36279484 – Proteolytic activity of cosmetic enzyme peel products

ingredients.name: Pueraria Lobata Root Extract
ingredients.note: Kudzu root extract rich in isoflavones (puerarin, daidzein) with documented phytoestrogenic activity, raising an endocrine concern. Legume-related allergy possible.

ingredients.name: Pulsatilla Koreana Extract
ingredients.note: Korean pasque flower extract used mainly for antimicrobial/natural-preservative activity (often paired with Usnea barbata and Zanthoxylum). Documented cases of irritant contact dermatitis warrant caution.

sources.label: PMC: Pulsatilla koreana Nakai Extract Attenuates Atopic Dermatitis-like Symptoms by Regulating Skin Barrier Factors and Inhibiting the JAK/STAT Pathway
PubMed: Anti-inflammatory effects of methanol extract from Pulsatilla koreana in lipopolysaccharide-exposed rats
PubChem: Cosmetic composition with whitening effect comprising extract of pulsatilla radix (patent)

ingredients.name: Quaternium-15
ingredients.note: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative and a frequent contact-allergy trigger.

ingredients.name: Red 30 Lake (CI 73360)
ingredients.note: Insoluble thioindigoid lake pigment, FDA-approved for cosmetics including lips but not for the eye area; subject to FDA specifications. Possible dye sensitivity.

ingredients.name: Red 33 (CI 17200)
ingredients.note: Water-soluble azo dye, FDA color additive permitted in cosmetics with limits (lip products up to 3% color), not for eye area. Some individuals sensitive to azo dyes.

ingredients.name: Red 7 (CI 15850)
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo lake pigment (lithol rubine) FDA-certified for cosmetics including lips, but not permitted in eye-area products. Batch certification required; possible dye sensitivity.

sources.label: PubChem: D&C Red No. 6 / Lithol Rubine compound record (CID 135487652)
PubMed: Identification and quantification of decarboxylated analogue of Pigments Red 57 and 57:1 in D&C Red No. 6, D&C Red No. 7, and their lakes

ingredients.name: Resorcinol
ingredients.note: Hair-dye ingredient with thyroid-disruption and skin-sensitization concerns.

ingredients.name: Retinal
ingredients.note: Retinaldehyde, the aldehyde form of vitamin A and a potent retinoid one step from retinoic acid, used as an anti-aging/anti-acne active. Can cause irritation and photosensitivity and is a vitamin-A-derivative endocrine/retinoid concern, so treated as precautionary.

ingredients.name: Retinyl Linoleate
ingredients.note: Retinyl ester (vitamin A) retinoid; SCCS restricts vitamin A esters and advises caution in leave-on products and during pregnancy. Treated as endocrine/teratogen-adjacent concern.

ingredients.name: Retinyl palmitate
ingredients.note: Mild cosmetic retinoid. Precautionary Tier 2 only because of debated photo-co-carcinogenicity data and pregnancy caution around high vitamin-A; routine low-level cosmetic use is generally considered fine.

ingredients.name: Rheum Palmatum Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the root of Chinese rhubarb, containing anthraquinones (including emodin) and tannins with antioxidant and astringent properties. At low cosmetic concentrations it can soothe irritated skin, but emodin is restricted in food supplements in the EU due to genotoxicity concerns, so high-concentration cosmetic uses warrant caution.

sources.label: PMC — The Health Benefits of Emodin from Rhubarb — Summary Update

ingredients.name: Rosa Damascena Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Damask rose essential oil used as fragrance/skin-conditioner. Contains EU fragrance allergens citronellol, geraniol, linalool and farnesol; can sensitize/irritate.

ingredients.name: Rosa Damascena Flower Water
ingredients.note: Damask rose hydrosol (rose water). Soothing, hydrating aromatic water; gentle, though it can carry trace rose-fragrance allergens.

ingredients.name: Rose Extract
ingredients.note: Rose flower extract used for fragrance and conditioning; naturally contains fragrance allergens (citronellol, geraniol, eugenol, linalool) that can sensitize, especially when oxidized.

ingredients.name: Rosemary Oil
ingredients.note: Aromatic oil containing camphor, limonene and pinenes that can provoke contact allergy; distinct from low-allergen rosemary leaf extract used as antioxidant.

ingredients.name: Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Rosemary essential oil contains monoterpenes and linalool that can cause contact irritation/sensitization in susceptible individuals. Treated as a potential sensitizer (Tier 2, precautionary).

ingredients.name: Royal Jelly Extract
ingredients.note: Bee-derived skin-conditioning extract; major royal jelly proteins (MRJP1/MRJP2) are documented allergens that can trigger reactions in bee-sensitive users.

sources.label: PMC: Bee Products in Dermatology and Skin Care
PMC: Royal Jelly Protects against Epidermal Stress through Upregulation of NQO1 Expression
PMC: Effect of Protease-Treated Royal Jelly Extract on Facial Wrinkles: A Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind, Parallel-Group Study

ingredients.name: Salvia Sclarea (Clary) Oil
ingredients.note: Clary sage essential oil rich in linalool and linalyl acetate (linalool is an established fragrance allergen). Used as a natural fragrance/skin tonic; irritation/allergy is the chief concern.

ingredients.name: Salvia Sclarea Oil
ingredients.note: An essential oil distilled from clary sage flowers, with a herbal, floral scent and antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Like most essential oils, it can irritate sensitive skin or trigger reactions in those with fragrance allergies; it should be avoided if you're pregnant.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety and efficacy of Salvia sclarea essential oil (EFSA assessment) (2024)
PubMed – Clary sage oil role in wound healing (2025)

ingredients.name: Sandalwood Oil
ingredients.note: A recognized fragrance contact allergen; can cause allergic contact dermatitis and photoallergy in sensitized individuals.

ingredients.name: Saponins
ingredients.note: Plant glycosides used as natural surfactants/cleansers; saponins are gastrointestinal/mucosal irritants and can be hemolytic, so precautionary.

ingredients.name: sh-Polypeptide-1
ingredients.note: Recombinant human basic Fibroblast Growth Factor (bFGF/FGF2) analog. Bioactive growth factor with debated topical safety; precautionary.

sources.label: PubMed: Topical application of sh-oligopeptide-1 and clinical trials with cosmetic preparations: risk or fraud?
PMC: Skin rejuvenation using cosmetic products containing growth factors, cytokines, and matrikines: a review

ingredients.name: sh-Polypeptide-22
ingredients.note: Recombinant human Transforming Growth Factor beta-1 (TGFbeta-1) analog. Bioactive growth factor whose topical safety is debated; precautionary.

sources.label: PMC: Skin rejuvenation using cosmetic products containing growth factors, cytokines, and matrikines: a review (includes TGF-beta polypeptides)

ingredients.name: sh-Polypeptide-3
ingredients.note: Recombinant human Keratinocyte Growth Factor (KGF) analog. Bioactive growth factor with debated topical safety; precautionary.

sources.label: PMC: Skin rejuvenation using cosmetic products containing growth factors, cytokines, and matrikines: a review

ingredients.name: SH-Polypeptide-9
ingredients.note: SH-Polypeptide-9 is a lab-made copy of VEGF, a human growth factor that promotes new blood vessel formation — in skincare it's used to improve microcirculation and support hair follicle health. Because it stimulates cell growth (mitogenic activity), some researchers flag it as something to watch, though current evidence at cosmetic-use levels does not show cancer risk.

sources.label: PubMed – Effects of the biomimetic peptide Sh-Polypeptide 9 (CG-VEGF) on hair follicle dermal papilla cells

ingredients.name: Silica
ingredients.note: Amorphous (cosmetic) silica is inert and safe in creams and pressed products. Loose powders pose an inhalation caution; crystalline silica is the hazardous form and should not be present in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cetearyl Sulfate
ingredients.note: This is a sulfate surfactant (cleansing agent) derived from fatty alcohols, used to create lather and help remove dirt and oil from skin and hair. Like other sulfate surfactants it can be mildly drying or irritating with repeated use, especially on sensitive skin.

sources.label: CIR – Sodium cetearyl sulfate ingredient page

ingredients.name: Sodium Dodecylbenzenesulfonate
ingredients.note: Sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate is a strong anionic (negatively charged) surfactant and detergent that cleans effectively but can strip moisture from skin and cause irritation or dryness, especially at higher concentrations or with repeated use. It is typically used at low levels in rinse-off products.

sources.label: Amended safety assessment of dodecylbenzenesulfonate – PubMed
Sodium Dodecyl Benzene Sulfonate in atopic dermatitis – PMC

ingredients.name: Sodium Glycolate
ingredients.note: Sodium glycolate is the sodium salt of glycolic acid (an alpha-hydroxy acid), which can gently exfoliate skin by loosening dead surface cells. Because AHAs increase skin's sensitivity to UV light and can irritate if overused, always wear sunscreen when using products with this ingredient and follow usage directions carefully.

ingredients.name: Sodium hydroxymethylglycinate
ingredients.note: Often marketed as a 'natural' preservative but releases formaldehyde. Precautionary Tier 2 due to the green-washing framing.

ingredients.name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
ingredients.note: Milder than SLS but ethoxylated, so it can carry trace 1,4-dioxane (a probable carcinogen) unless vacuum-stripped during manufacture. The dioxane is a manufacturing contaminant, not an inherent property; reputable suppliers reduce it to negligible levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Metabisulfite
ingredients.note: Sulfite preservative and antioxidant that can trigger contact allergy and, in sensitive individuals, respiratory or urticarial reactions. EU permits it within Annex V limits (up to 0.2% as free SO2).

ingredients.name: Sodium Myreth Sulfate
ingredients.note: This is an ethoxylated sulfate surfactant used as a gentle cleansing agent in shampoos and body washes; it is generally milder than sodium lauryl sulfate but can still be drying for very sensitive skin at high concentrations. It is considered safe for rinse-off cosmetics at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Persulfate
ingredients.note: Persulfate bleach accelerator; associated with occupational asthma and contact dermatitis among salon workers and clients.

ingredients.name: Sodium Salicylate
ingredients.note: The sodium salt of salicylic acid, used in cosmetics as a preservative and denaturant at up to about 2%. The CIR found it safe as used when formulated to be non-irritating and non-sensitizing; it should not be used in products for children under 3 years (except rinse-off shampoos) and may cause mild exfoliation at higher concentrations.

sources.label: PubMed – Amended Safety Assessment of Salicylic Acid and Salicylates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Silicate
ingredients.note: Sodium silicate is a highly alkaline inorganic salt used in small amounts in rinse-off cosmetics like cleansers and hair products as a pH adjuster and texture agent. At high concentrations it is corrosive to skin, so the CIR and EU regulations restrict it to specific product types and concentrations — in properly formulated, pH-balanced products it is safe.

sources.label: CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Silica and Silicates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Sulfite
ingredients.note: Sodium sulfite is used in cosmetics as a preservative and antioxidant to prevent browning and oxidation. It belongs to the sulfite family, which can trigger contact dermatitis or, rarely, asthma-like reactions in people who are sulfite-sensitive.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Sulfites as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Sesquioleate
ingredients.note: Common nonionic sorbitan-ester emulsifier, but a documented and emerging contact allergen used in patch-test series. Flagged precautionary for sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Steareth-2
ingredients.note: Low-mole ethoxylated stearyl alcohol used as a W/O co-emulsifier. Ethoxylated process can leave trace 1,4-dioxane; flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Steareth-20
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated ("-eth-") emulsifier with the same trace 1,4-dioxane caveat as other ethoxylated surfactants. The PEG chain is well tolerated; the byproduct contaminant is the basis for precaution.

ingredients.name: Steareth-21
ingredients.note: Higher-mole ethoxylated stearyl alcohol emulsifier. Safe in use but ethoxylated, hence trace 1,4-dioxane precaution.

ingredients.name: Sweet Orange Oil
ingredients.note: High-limonene citrus oil; mildly phototoxic and a fragrance allergen, with limonene oxidation products driving sensitization.

ingredients.name: Symphytum Officinale (Comfrey) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Comfrey can contain hepatotoxic/genotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids; oral use is FDA-banned and EMA restricts topical use, though dermal absorption of lycopsamine is low. Treated as a sensitizer/toxicant concern.

ingredients.name: Symphytum Officinale Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from comfrey leaves that has anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing benefits, but comfrey naturally contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs) which can be liver-toxic; the leaf contains lower PA levels than the root. Regulators recommend using only on intact skin and for limited durations; it is not for internal use.

sources.label: PMC – LC-MS/MS Evaluation of Pyrrolizidine Alkaloids in Comfrey Leaves
PubMed – Penetration of lycopsamine from comfrey ointment through human epidermis

ingredients.name: Symphytum Officinale Root Extract
ingredients.note: Comfrey root extract contains allantoin and rosmarinic acid which have proven anti-inflammatory and healing benefits for skin; however, it also naturally contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs) which are hepatotoxic and potentially carcinogenic, so the EU limits its topical use to short durations (up to 10 days) and requires PA-depleted extracts in cosmetics. Look for PA-depleted versions when choosing products with this ingredient.

sources.label: EU CosIng – Symphytum Officinale Root Extract (entry 84281)
PubMed: Influence of pyrrolizidine alkaloid depletion on biological activity of Symphytum officinale extracts
PMC: A Symphytum officinale Root Extract Exerts Anti-inflammatory Properties

ingredients.name: Talc
ingredients.note: Talc is mined near asbestos and can be contaminated with it; the FDA still must establish standardized asbestos testing under MoCRA. Creams and pressed products are generally fine, but loose face/body powders carry an inhalation and contamination caution.

ingredients.name: Tanacetum Annuum Flower Oil
ingredients.note: The essential oil distilled from blue tansy flowers, naturally rich in chamazulene (which gives it a vivid blue color) and known for anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. It is an essential oil and can sensitize some people; unlike common tansy (Tanacetum vulgare), it is very low in toxic thujone, but always use it at low, recommended concentrations.

sources.label: PMC — Chemical Characterization and Antifungal Activity of Blue Tansy (Tanacetum annuum) Essential Oil

ingredients.name: Tea Tree Oil
ingredients.note: Common contact allergen; oxidized oil is markedly more sensitizing, with ascaridole, terpinene and limonene as key haptens. Sensitization is cumulative and reversible.

ingredients.name: Tetradecyl Aminobutyroylvalylaminobutyric Urea Trifluoroacetate
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic peptide (marketed as SYN-HYCAN) designed to boost the skin's own hyaluronic acid production for plumping and firming effects; as a peptide active it is generally well tolerated at recommended concentrations. However, it contains a trifluoroacetate salt, which places it in the PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) category; the FDA has flagged insufficient safety data for PFAS in cosmetics, warranting precaution.

sources.label: PubChem CID 137528195 – Tetradecyl aminobutyroylvalylaminobutyric urea trifluoroacetate
FDA: Report on the Use of PFAS in Cosmetic Products and Associated Risks
FDA: FDA Finds Insufficient Data to Determine Safety of PFAS in Cosmetic Products

ingredients.name: Thymus Vulgaris (Thyme) Extract
ingredients.note: Thyme extract whose key constituents (thymol, carvacrol) are recognized skin sensitizers/allergens; restricted to low levels in leave-on products. Treated as a Tier 2 sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Thymus Vulgaris Flower/Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: An essential oil distilled from thyme flowers and leaves, used for its antimicrobial properties and herbal scent. It contains thymol and carvacrol which are potent actives and potential contact allergens, so it should be used at low concentrations in rinse-off products or well diluted.

sources.label: PubMed 25870697 – Thymus vulgaris essential oil: chemical composition and antimicrobial activity
PubMed 11260240 – Occupational airborne contact dermatitis caused by thyme

ingredients.name: Toluene
ingredients.note: Neurotoxic solvent, mostly in nail polish. Inhalation/repeated exposure concern; part of the nail 'toxic trio.'

ingredients.name: Toluene-2,5-Diamine
ingredients.note: Primary oxidative hair-dye intermediate and strong skin sensitizer closely related to PPD; SCCS-restricted with maximum-use limits.

ingredients.name: Tonalide
ingredients.note: Polycyclic synthetic musk that is persistent and bioaccumulative with suspected endocrine-disrupting effects. Flagged precautionary primarily for environmental and bioaccumulation concerns rather than acute skin risk.

ingredients.name: Tosylamide/Formaldehyde Resin
ingredients.note: Nail-lacquer film-former and well-known cause of allergic contact dermatitis (including ectopic eyelid/face) due to residual formaldehyde reactivity.

ingredients.name: Triclocarban
ingredients.note: Antibacterial relative of triclosan; same endocrine-disruptor concern.

ingredients.name: Triclosan
ingredients.note: Antibacterial in some cleansers/soaps; endocrine-disruptor and antibiotic-resistance concerns. Largely removed from US hand soaps but still appears in other products.

ingredients.name: Tussilago Farfara (Coltsfoot) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Coltsfoot is an Asteraceae plant containing pyrrolizidine alkaloids (hepatotoxic) and is a potential Compositae sensitizer; flagged precautionary despite topical cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Usnea Barbata (Lichen) Extract
ingredients.note: Lichen extract containing usnic acid, used as a co-preservative/antimicrobial; noted sensitization potential and usnic acid is hepatotoxic if ingested. Marked precautionary for sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Vanilla flower botanical extract; vanilla-derived aromatics include vanillin, an EU-flagged fragrance allergen, so treated precautionarily despite generally mild profile.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Vanilla fruit extract is largely non-sensitizing per CIR but contains vanillin, an EU-listed declarable fragrance allergen, so handled precautionarily.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Vanilla-derived oil containing vanillin, which the EU now lists as a declarable fragrance allergen (Reg 2023/1545); generally mild but flagged for sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Vanilla fruit water (aqueous fraction). Mild aromatic botanical; can contribute trace fragrance compounds.

ingredients.name: Vanillin
ingredients.note: Aromatic aldehyde fragrance/flavor agent now individually labelled as a fragrance allergen under EU Regulation 2023/1545; secondary sensitizer linked to vanilla/isoeugenol cross-reactivity.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Vanilla-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Vetiveria Zizanoides Root Oil
ingredients.note: An essential oil distilled from vetiver grass roots with a deep, earthy, woody scent widely used in perfumes and cosmetics. As a complex essential oil it is a potential sensitizer in people with fragrance allergies; the EU's SCCS has assessed it as safe at recommended cosmetic concentrations.

sources.label: PubMed – SCCS opinion on safety of Acetylated Vetiver Oil fragrance ingredient (2019)
PMC – Vetiver Essential Oil in Cosmetics: What Is New? (2017)

ingredients.name: Vitex Agnus-Castus Extract
ingredients.note: Chasteberry extract with documented estrogen-receptor-beta-selective phytoconstituents (apigenin) and dopaminergic/hormonal activity; endocrine concern.

ingredients.name: Witch Hazel
ingredients.note: Astringent extract considered safe when non-irritating; alcohol-based distillates and high levels can dry or irritate sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Yellow 5 (CI 19140)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved synthetic azo dye that causes the most allergic/intolerance reactions of the azo dyes, especially in aspirin-sensitive or asthmatic individuals.

sources.label: PubChem: FD&C Yellow No. 5 compound record (CID 16013)
PMC: Toxicological Effects of Tartrazine Exposure: A Review of In Vitro and Animal Studies with Human Health Implications
FDA: How Safe are Color Additives?

ingredients.name: Yellow 6 (CI 15985)
ingredients.note: Petroleum-derived azo dye (Sunset Yellow), FDA-certified color additive. Animal studies show no carcinogenicity, but it can trigger allergic-type reactions in sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Ylang Ylang Oil
ingredients.note: Floral oil rich in linalool, benzyl benzoate and isoeugenol; one of the higher-risk essential oils for allergic contact dermatitis.

ingredients.name: Zanthoxylum Piperitum Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese pepper (sansho) extract containing tingling sanshool alkylamides plus limonene/terpene fragrance components; can sensitize/irritate, conservative dosing advised.

ingredients.name: Zinc Pyrithione
ingredients.note: Anti-dandruff antimicrobial reclassified as reprotoxic (CMR) in the EU, leading to its prohibition as a cosmetic preservative as of 2022. Can also irritate skin and eyes.

ingredients.name: Zingiber Officinale (Ginger) Root Oil
ingredients.note: Steam-distilled ginger essential oil; can cause skin irritation or sensitization in susceptible individuals, patch testing advised.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Zingiber officinale (Ginger)-Derived Ingredients (main report)
CIR: Safety Assessment of Zingiber officinale (Ginger)-Derived Ingredients (supporting literature review)
PMC: Safety and efficacy of essential oil, oleoresin and tincture from Zingiber officinale Roscoe as sensory additives in feed

ingredients.name: 1,2-Hexanediol
ingredients.note: Multifunctional glycol acting as solvent, humectant and preservative-booster; CIR concluded 1,2-glycols safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: 1-Methylhydantoin-2-Imide
ingredients.note: This is a hydantoin-derived compound used as an antimicrobial preservative in cosmetics. It has a well-characterized structure and is considered safe at typical use concentrations.

ingredients.name: 2,3-Butanediol
ingredients.note: Small diol functioning as humectant, solvent and skin conditioner; standard cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety evaluation of some humectants and moisturizers used in cosmetic formulations

ingredients.name: 2-Mercaptonicotinoyl Glycine
ingredients.note: A synthetic compound that combines a nicotinic acid-derived thiol with the amino acid glycine, used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning active. It is a niche ingredient with limited published safety data, but no known hazards at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: 2-Oleamido-1,3-Octadecanediol
ingredients.note: A synthetic ceramide-like molecule derived from oleic acid and octadecanediol that mimics the skin's natural lipids to help strengthen the moisture barrier. It is generally considered safe and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: 3-O-Ethyl ascorbic acid
ingredients.note: Stable vitamin C derivative; antioxidant/brightening, safe.

ingredients.name: 4-T-Butylcyclohexanol
ingredients.note: A soothing active (trade name SymSitive) that calms the skin's nerve receptors to reduce stinging, itching, and redness — often used in sensitive-skin formulas. It is considered safe for cosmetic use at typical concentrations.

sources.label: PubChem Patent WO-2018137854-A1 — Trans-4-t-butylcyclohexanol in cosmetics

ingredients.name: 4-Terpineol
ingredients.note: Monoterpene alcohol (main active of tea tree oil) used as a fragrance/antimicrobial agent. RIFM evaluated it as safe at current fragrance levels with markedly lower sensitization risk than whole tea tree oil.

ingredients.name: 7-Dehydrocholesterol
ingredients.note: The cholesterol-pathway precursor of vitamin D3, used as a skin-conditioning/barrier-support lipid. Used at very low levels and considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Acacia Decurrens Flower Wax
ingredients.note: Floral plant wax from Acacia decurrens used as an emollient, structuring and film-forming agent. Standard botanical wax with low irritation potential.

sources.label: PubChem: Acacia decurrens flower compound record
CIR: Safety Assessment of Acacia Senegal Gum and related Acacia-derived ingredients

ingredients.name: Acacia Senegal Gum
ingredients.note: Plant polysaccharide gum used as film-former, binder and emulsion stabilizer; CIR concluded safe with low toxicity.

ingredients.name: Acacia Seyal Gum
ingredients.note: Acacia tree polysaccharide gum used as a film-former, thickener and texture agent. Benign; closely related to CIR-reviewed acacia gums.

ingredients.name: Acer Saccharum (Sugar Maple) Extract
ingredients.note: Sugar maple extract (a natural AHA source) used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Acer Saccharum Extract
ingredients.note: Sugar maple sap extract containing mild naturally-occurring AHAs (malic, tartaric) and polyphenols, used for gentle skin conditioning/antioxidant benefit. Weak exfoliant; benign cosmetic botanical.

sources.label: PubMed: Nutrients, Antioxidant Capacity and Safety of Hot Water Extract from Sugar Maple (Acer saccharum) and Red Maple Bark
PMC: Chemical Compositional, Biological, and Safety Studies of a Novel Maple Syrup Derived Extract for Nutraceutical Applications
PubMed: Phenolic glycosides from sugar maple (Acer saccharum) bark

ingredients.name: Acetone
ingredients.note: Standard nail-polish remover solvent of low systemic toxicity; drying and defatting to skin/nails, an inhalation irritant, and highly flammable.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Dipeptide-1 Cetyl Ester
ingredients.note: Synthetic lipo-dipeptide signal peptide marketed for firming and skin-calming; no significant safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Glucosamine
ingredients.note: Amino sugar used as a skin-conditioning agent supporting hydration and exfoliation. CIR concluded glucosamine ingredients are safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Glutamine
ingredients.note: Acetyl Glutamine is a stabilized form of the amino acid glutamine that helps reinforce the skin's moisture barrier and boost hydration. It's considered very safe with no significant irritation concerns.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of α-Amino Acids as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Acetyl Hexapeptide-1
ingredients.note: Synthetic alpha-MSH biomimetic peptide that stimulates melanocortin-1 receptor for skin/hair pigmentation. Used at low levels; low irritation/sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Acetyl hexapeptide-8
ingredients.note: Cosmetic peptide; safe.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-11
ingredients.note: A synthetic peptide that supports skin-cell energy production and may help with anti-aging and extracellular matrix repair. It is considered safe at cosmetic concentrations and is not associated with significant safety concerns.

sources.label: Peptides in Skin Senescence Review – PMC

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-5
ingredients.note: Synthetic anti-edema tetrapeptide used in eye products to reduce puffiness and dark circles. Considered safe and non-toxic topically.

sources.label: PMC: Usage of Synthetic Peptides in Cosmetics for Sensitive Skin
PubMed: Protective and Anti-Aging Effects of 5 Cosmeceutical Peptides

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-9
ingredients.note: Synthetic matrikine peptide (Dermican, Ac-Gln-Asp-Val-His) that mimics lumican to support collagen synthesis. Well-tolerated cosmetic peptide active.

sources.label: PMC: Usage of Synthetic Peptides in Cosmetics for Sensitive Skin
PubMed: Regulatory role of the tetrapeptide AcSDKP in skin and hair physiology

ingredients.name: Acetylarginyltryptophyl Diphenylglycine
ingredients.note: A synthetic peptide (CID 72942020 on PubChem) used in anti-aging serums to help reduce the appearance of expression lines. No significant safety concerns have been identified at cosmetic use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Acetylated Glycol Stearate
ingredients.note: A waxy ester made from glycol stearate and acetic acid, used to give creams a pearlescent shimmer and smooth texture. It is considered safe for cosmetic use based on the low toxicity profile of related glycol stearate ingredients.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Glycol Stearate and Glycol Stearate SE

ingredients.name: Acrylamide/Sodium Acryloyldimethyltaurate Copolymer
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic polymer used to thicken gels and stabilize emulsions in skincare. The CIR Expert Panel reviewed it and found it safe as used — it has very low skin penetration due to its large molecular size and is not a sensitizer at normal cosmetic levels.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Acryloyldimethyltaurate Polymers (Final Report 2017)
CIR Safety Assessment of Acrylamide/Acrylate Copolymers (2022)

ingredients.name: Acrylates Copolymer
ingredients.note: Fully polymerized acrylic copolymer used as a film former and rheology modifier. Inert as used; only unreacted acrylamide/acrylate monomers are a concern, and finished polymer grades are tightly controlled.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ammonium Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer functioning as a film former, fixative and viscosity/emulsion stabilizer. High-molecular-weight polymer with low toxicity and minimal residual monomer per CIR.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Beheneth-25 Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic polymer used to thicken and stabilize cosmetic formulas (like lotions and gels). CIR concluded it is safe in cosmetics when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers (2018)

ingredients.name: Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Carbomer-family acrylic copolymer that thickens and helps emulsify oils. Considered safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Dimethicone Copolymer
ingredients.note: Hybrid acrylic-silicone polymer giving water- and transfer-resistant films; inert and generally recognized as safe.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ethylhexyl Acrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylic copolymer used as a film former in sunscreens, foundations and styling products. Large inert polymer; standard formulation aid.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ethylhexyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic polymer used to thicken and stabilize cosmetic formulas, creating a smooth gel-like texture. The CIR Expert Panel found it safe as used when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR – Acrylates/Ethylhexyl Acrylate Crosspolymer ingredient page
CIR – Final Safety Assessment: Crosslinked Alkyl Acrylates (PDF)

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ethylhexyl Acrylate/Dimethicone Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Silicone-acrylate copolymer used as a film-former and texture/wear agent in color and sun care products. Standard low-concern polymer.

sources.label: PubChem: Acrylic acid/dimethicone methacrylate/ethylhexyl acrylate copolymer compound record
CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Palmeth-25 Acrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer used as a viscosity/thickening and texture agent. Judged safe by CIR for the acrylate polymer family.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Polytrimethylsiloxymethacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight silicone-acrylate film former used in color, sun and hair care. CIR reviewed acrylates copolymers as safe; large inert polymer with little toxicity.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Steareth-20 Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic polymer used to thicken and stabilize lotions and creams. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in cosmetics when formulated to be non-irritating; it showed no mutagenicity or sensitization in standard tests.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Stearyl Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: A film-forming polymer used to thicken, stabilize, or add texture to creams and cosmetics. It has a good safety record and is not expected to cause skin problems at normal use levels.

sources.label: CIR – Amended Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics (2018)

ingredients.name: Actinidia Chinensis (Kiwi) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Kiwi seed oil high in linoleic and alpha-linolenic acids, used as an emollient and antioxidant skin-conditioning oil. Benign.

ingredients.name: Adansonia Digitata Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Baobab fruit extract used as an antioxidant (vitamin C) and skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Adansonia Digitata Pulp Extract
ingredients.note: Baobab fruit pulp extract, rich in vitamin C and polysaccharides, used as an antioxidant and humectant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign.

ingredients.name: Adansonia Digitata Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Baobab seed oil rich in oleic/linoleic/palmitic acids and vitamin E; non-comedogenic emollient.

sources.label: PubChem: Adansonia digitata seed oil compound record
CIR: Adansonia digitata seed oil ingredient page
PMC: Impact of Different Oil Extraction Techniques on the Physicochemical Properties of Adansonia digitata Seed

ingredients.name: Adenosine
ingredients.note: Signaling ingredient for smoothing; safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Adipic Acid/Neopentyl Glycol Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic polymer formed from adipic acid and neopentyl glycol that gives creams and serums a silky, smooth texture. It is not absorbed through the skin and is considered safe in cosmetic use.

sources.label: PubChem CID 168488 – Adipic acid, isophthalic acid, neopentyl glycol resin
CIR Safety Assessment of Trimellitic Anhydride Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Aesculus Hippocastanum (Horse Chestnut) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from horse chestnut seeds that contains aescin, a compound known to help reduce puffiness and support circulation; the whole plant parts are technically toxic if eaten but topical cosmetic use of the standardized extract is considered safe at normal concentrations.

sources.label: PMC: Beneficial antioxidant effects of A. hippocastanum seed extract (PubMed 20219666)
PMC: Horse chestnut seed extract for chronic venous insufficiency

ingredients.name: Aframomum Angustifolium Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Ginger-family (Zingiberaceae) seed extract used as an antioxidant, anti-aging skin-conditioning botanical; reported suitable for sensitive skin. Benign.

ingredients.name: Aframomum Melegueta Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the seeds of the West African spice plant (related to ginger), used in skincare for antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits. Generally well-tolerated; very high concentrations may cause a mild tingling warmth on sensitive skin.

sources.label: PMC — Antioxidant potentials of Aframomum melegueta seed extract
PubMed — A. melegueta seed extract clinical study

ingredients.name: Agar
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide gel from red algae used as a gelling, thickening and stabilizing agent in creams, gels and emulsions.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Plant Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics (includes Agar)
CIR: Safety Assessment of Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics (final report)

ingredients.name: Agave Tequilana Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Agave leaf extract used as a humectant/skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Agave Tequilana Stem Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the stem of the blue agave plant (used to make tequila) that is added to cosmetics for its moisturizing and skin-soothing properties. No safety concerns have been identified at the concentrations typically used in skincare products.

sources.label: EU CosIng – AGAVE TEQUILANA LEAF EXTRACT (related listing)

ingredients.name: Agropyron Repens Root Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from couch grass (a common plant) root, used in cosmetics for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is a traditional botanical with a good safety profile at cosmetic concentrations.

sources.label: PMC – Metabolic Profile of Agropyron repens Rhizome

ingredients.name: Ahnfeltia Concinna Extract
ingredients.note: This extract from a red marine alga is rich in polysaccharides that help hydrate the skin and support its natural barrier. It's a gentle, well-tolerated ingredient with antioxidant and soothing properties.

sources.label: PMC – Beneficial Effects of Marine Algae-Derived Carbohydrates for Skin Health
PubMed – Polysaccharides of the red algae

ingredients.name: Alanine
ingredients.note: Amino acid and NMF constituent used as a skin-conditioning humectant. CIR concluded alpha-amino acids are safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Alcaligenes Polysaccharides
ingredients.note: Bacterial-fermentation polysaccharide gum used as a high-capacity humectant, emulsion stabilizer and viscosity agent. CIR microbial-gum assessment supports safety.

ingredients.name: Alchemilla Vulgaris Extract
ingredients.note: Lady's mantle extract, a tannin-rich astringent and antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Standard, low-hazard plant extract.

sources.label: PMC: Harnessing the Power of Alchemilla: A Natural Solution for Skin Health and Dermatological Disorders
PubChem: Alchemilla vulgaris, extract compound record
PubMed: Protective effect of dietary Alchemilla mollis on UVB-irradiated premature skin aging

ingredients.name: Alcohol Denat.
ingredients.note: Denatured ethanol used as a fast-evaporating solvent; low systemic concern but can be drying and irritating on compromised or sensitive skin at high concentration, and is flammable. Precautionary mainly for its drying/barrier-disrupting potential.

ingredients.name: Algae Extract
ingredients.note: Generic marine algae/seaweed extract used as a skin-conditioning and humectant agent. Generally well-tolerated; possible trace arsenic controlled by GMP.

ingredients.name: Algin
ingredients.note: Brown-seaweed-derived polysaccharide (sodium alginate) used as a thickener/gelling and film-forming agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Alginic Acid
ingredients.note: Brown-algae polysaccharide used as a thickening/gelling and binding agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Allantoin
ingredients.note: Soothing, skin-protectant; safe.

ingredients.name: Allium Cepa (Onion) Bulb Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from onion bulbs, rich in quercetin and sulphur compounds known for antioxidant and soothing properties; commonly used to improve the appearance of scars. Generally safe for skin, though people with onion allergies should patch-test first.

sources.label: Inhibitory effects of onion extract – PubMed

ingredients.name: Allium Sativum (Garlic) Bulb Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from garlic bulbs, included in cosmetics for its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. It is generally considered safe at typical cosmetic use levels, though the strong odor and potential for irritation in high concentrations are worth noting.

sources.label: ScienceDirect: Garlic peel extracts – food additive review

ingredients.name: Aloe Arborescens Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from a succulent plant related to common aloe vera, used in cosmetics for its antioxidant and soothing properties. The CIR notes that available data are currently insufficient to fully confirm safety for this specific species (as opposed to Aloe vera), so look for products with low concentrations.

sources.label: CIR – Aloe Arborescens Leaf Extract ingredient page
PMC – Chemical Profile, Antioxidant and Antibacterial Activities of Aloe arborescens

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis (Aloe Vera) Leaf Juice
ingredients.note: Aloe vera leaf juice used as a soothing/moisturizing botanical. CIR deems it safe when anthraquinone (aloin) levels stay below 50 ppm.

sources.label: PubChem: Aloe barbadensis extract compound record (CID 5712222)
PMC: Aloe vera: A review of toxicity and adverse clinical effects
PMC: Toxicologic Assessment of a Commercial Decolorized Whole Leaf Aloe Vera Juice

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Aloe flower botanical used for skin conditioning; CIR supports safety when anthraquinone content is limited, and anthraquinones can be phototoxic, so precautionary.

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Water
ingredients.note: Aloe vera hydrosol — the watery distillate/juice fraction of aloe leaf. Soothing, hydrating humectant; very widely tolerated.

sources.label: PubChem: Aloe barbadensis extract compound record (CID 5712222)
PMC: Aloe vera: A review of toxicity and adverse clinical effects
PMC: Toxicologic Assessment of a Commercial Decolorized Whole Leaf Aloe Vera Juice

ingredients.name: Aloe Ferox Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Soothing aloe-derived leaf extract used as a skin-conditioning agent. Considered safe for topical use.

ingredients.name: Aloe Vera
ingredients.note: Hydrating, soothing leaf gel that is very well tolerated; rare allergy occurs but it is benign for most users.

ingredients.name: Alpha-Glucan
ingredients.note: Glucose-based polysaccharide used as a humectant, prebiotic and barrier-supporting skin-conditioning agent. Gentle and non-irritating, suitable for sensitive skin.

sources.label: PMC: Prebiotic Oligosaccharides in Skin Health: Benefits, Mechanisms, and Cosmetic Applications
PubMed: Skin health promotion effects of natural beta-glucan derived from cereals and microorganisms

ingredients.name: Alteromonas Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: Marine-bacteria fermentation extract (exopolysaccharide) used for skin conditioning and hydration; considered well tolerated and low risk.

sources.label: Anti-aging cosmetic with Alteromonas ferment extract - PMC clinical study
Marine versus Non-Marine Bacterial Exopolysaccharides and Skincare - PMC
Application of Marine Microbial Natural Products in Cosmetics - PMC

ingredients.name: Alteromonas Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: A liquid filtrate obtained by fermenting Alteromonas macleodii, a deep-sea bacterium; it is used in skincare products for its reported skin-calming and moisturizing benefits. The ingredient has a good tolerability profile and is generally considered safe at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: PMC – Galactomyces Ferment Filtrate transcriptomic analysis (related ferment filtrate science)

ingredients.name: Althaea Officinalis (Marshmallow) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Marshmallow root is rich in mucilage (slippery polysaccharides) that soothes and moisturizes skin, making it ideal for sensitive or irritated complexions. It is widely considered safe, though as with any plant extract, those with plant allergies should patch-test.

sources.label: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidative effects of Althaea officinalis root extract – PMC

ingredients.name: Althaea Rosea Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Hollyhock flower extract used as a soothing, mucilage-rich skin-conditioning botanical. Standard low-hazard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Alumina
ingredients.note: Inert aluminum oxide used as an abrasive, bulking and opacifying agent and pigment substrate. Insoluble mineral considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Dimyristate
ingredients.note: Aluminum salt of myristic acid used as a thickener, gellant and viscosity/texture agent (often to gel oils). Inert metallic soap regarded as safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Distearate
ingredients.note: A metal soap made from aluminum and stearic acid used to thicken and stabilize cosmetic formulas, especially lipsticks and foundations. It stays on the surface of the skin and is not considered a significant absorption risk.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Hydroxide
ingredients.note: Inert substrate for lake colorants and opacifying/pigment-coating agent; CIR found alumina/aluminum hydroxide safe with negligible systemic aluminum.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Starch Octenylsuccinate
ingredients.note: Modified starch powder used to absorb oil and impart a soft, dry feel; mild and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Stearate
ingredients.note: Aluminum salt of stearic acid used as a thickener, suspending and emulsion-stabilizing agent; CIR concluded it is safe with low dermal toxicity. Not a toxic-metal contaminant concern at cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Aluminum/Magnesium Hydroxide Stearate
ingredients.note: A mineral salt used to thicken and stabilize cosmetic products, helping oil and water stay blended; well-tolerated by most skin types and not considered a safety concern at typical cosmetic levels.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alumina and Aluminum Hydroxide as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Amaranthus Caudatus Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Derived from love-lies-bleeding (amaranth) seeds, this extract is loaded with squalene, vitamin E, and fatty acids that hydrate the skin and protect it from environmental damage. It's considered safe with no known concerns.

ingredients.name: Amber Powder
ingredients.note: Fossilized pine resin (amber) milled into powder, used as a mild physical exfoliant and deodorizer. Benign.

ingredients.name: Amethyst Powder
ingredients.note: Finely milled amethyst (a quartz/silica mineral) used as a bulking, abrasive or marketing 'gemstone' powder. Inert and low-hazard topically.

sources.label: Comment on potential carcinogens in makeup cosmetics (crystalline silica context) - PMC

ingredients.name: Aminobutyric Acid
ingredients.note: Non-protein amino acid (GABA) used as a skin-conditioning/anti-wrinkle active claiming muscle-relaxing and moisturizing effects. Skin-identical compound considered safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Aminomethyl Propanediol
ingredients.note: Aliphatic amino-alcohol used as pH adjuster/neutralizer. CIR concluded safe at concentrations up to 2%; buffered in finished products.

ingredients.name: Aminomethyl Propanol
ingredients.note: Amino-alcohol base used to neutralize acrylic thickeners. Considered safe at low neutralizing levels and a common TEA alternative.

ingredients.name: Aminopropyl Ascorbyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: Stable water-soluble vitamin C derivative used as an antioxidant and brightening agent. Parent ascorbic acid/ascorbates judged safe by CIR.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/Beheneth-25 Methacrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A crosslinked polymer used as a gel-forming emulsion stabilizer to give creams and gels a smooth, lightweight texture. It is well tolerated; the CIR reviewed acryloyldimethyltaurate polymers as a class and found them safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/Carboxyethyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Pre-neutralized crosslinked synthetic polymer used as a thickener/stabilizer and suspending agent over a wide pH range. CIR-reviewed acrylate polymer regarded as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer
ingredients.note: Pre-neutralized acrylic copolymer that thickens and stabilizes emulsions at a wide pH. Considered safe and non-sensitizing as used.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Lauryl Sulfate
ingredients.note: Sulfate cleanser similar to SLS; a moderate skin and eye irritant that can be drying. Not ethoxylated, so no 1,4-dioxane concern, and not a toxin or endocrine disruptor.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Polyacrylate
ingredients.note: Ammonium salt of polyacrylic acid used as a thickener/emulsion stabilizer and film former; high-molecular-weight polymer reviewed by CIR as safe with negligible skin penetration.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Synthetic acryloyldimethyltaurate polymer used as a thickener/rheology modifier and emulsion stabilizer; CIR-assessed safe.

ingredients.name: Amodimethicone
ingredients.note: Amino-functionalized polydimethylsiloxane used mainly as a hair-conditioning silicone that films onto the hair shaft. CIR/FDA consider it safe; not irritating or sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Amorphophallus Konjac Root Powder
ingredients.note: Glucomannan-rich milled konjac tuber used as a hydrating, mild exfoliating bulking/texture agent. Generally well tolerated.

sources.label: Amorphophallus konjac: traditional uses, bioactive potential, emerging health applications - PMC
Effect of Konjac Glucomannan on dermal environment against UVB-induced condition - PMC
Beneficial health characteristics of native and hydrolysed konjac glucomannan - PubMed

ingredients.name: Amylopectin
ingredients.note: Amylopectin is a natural, highly branched form of starch (found in corn, rice, and potato) used in cosmetics as a thickener and texture agent. It is food-grade and very well tolerated by skin.

ingredients.name: Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Pineapple fruit extract containing bromelain enzyme, AHAs and vitamin C; used for gentle enzymatic exfoliation and brightening. Safe at typical cosmetic levels.

sources.label: Ananas comosus peels extract as new natural cosmetic ingredient, topical nano cream - PubMed
Properties and therapeutic application of bromelain (pineapple enzyme) - PMC
Applications of bromelain from pineapple waste towards acne - PMC

ingredients.name: Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Pineapple fruit water (aqueous fraction). Mild hydrating botanical; the same fruit also yields enzymes (bromelain) used for gentle exfoliation.

sources.label: Ananas comosus peels extract as new natural cosmetic ingredient, topical nano cream - PubMed
Properties and therapeutic application of bromelain (pineapple enzyme) - PMC
Applications of bromelain from pineapple waste towards acne - PMC

ingredients.name: Anastatica Hierochuntica Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of the desert resurrection plant (Rose of Jericho) used as a hydrating, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Angelica Acutiloba Root Extract
ingredients.note: Root extract of Japanese angelica used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Standard cosmetic botanical; note that Apiaceae roots can contain trace furocoumarins, but extract is treated as benign at use levels.

sources.label: Anti-photoaging effects of Angelica acutiloba root ethanol extract in human dermal fibroblasts - PubMed
Anti-wrinkle properties of Angelica gigas root extracts - PMC

ingredients.name: Angelica Keiskei Extract
ingredients.note: Ashitaba extract (chalcones, flavonoids) used as antioxidant, astringent, emollient and skin-protecting agent.

sources.label: Evaluation of acute skin irritation and phototoxicity of Angelica keiskei (cosmetic safety) - PubMed
Angelica keiskei (Ashitaba) powder and its functional components - PubMed
Ashitaba chalcones suppress melanomagenesis via BRAF and PI3-K - PMC

ingredients.name: Anhydroxylitol
ingredients.note: Cyclic sugar derived from xylitol used as a hygroscopic humectant and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found it non-irritating, non-sensitizing and safe as used.

ingredients.name: Annatto (CI 75120)
ingredients.note: Natural carotenoid colorant from Bixa orellana seeds; FDA-listed color additive exempt from certification, approved in US and EU.

sources.label: Safety of annatto and exposure to bixin/norbixin (E 160b) as food additive - PMC
Biosynthesis of the food and cosmetic plant pigment bixin (annatto) - PubMed
FDA Summary of Color Additives for Use in Foods, Drugs, Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Anthemis Nobilis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Roman chamomile flower extract used as a soothing botanical. Benign; rare Asteraceae contact allergy possible.

ingredients.name: Anthemis Nobilis Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Roman chamomile essential oil used as fragrance and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found Anthemis nobilis ingredients not irritating or sensitizing as used; Asteraceae-allergic users should be cautious.

ingredients.name: Anthyllis Vulneraria Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Kidney vetch flower extract used as a skin-conditioning/brightening botanical; standard plant extract.

sources.label: Radical scavenging and antioxidant activity of Anthyllis vulneraria leaves and flowers - PMC
Phenolic profile, EPR determination, and antiproliferative activity of Anthyllis vulneraria extracts - PMC
Comprehensive phytochemical characterization of kidney vetch (Anthyllis vulneraria L.) - PubMed

ingredients.name: Apium Graveolens (Celery) Extract
ingredients.note: An antioxidant-rich plant extract from celery used in skincare for soothing and conditioning. Research suggests antimicrobial benefits; generally considered safe for topical use.

sources.label: Celery as potential antibacterial agent in skin wounds – PMC
Celery antibacterial skin wound study – PubMed

ingredients.name: Arachidic Acid
ingredients.note: Saturated C20 fatty acid used as an emollient and opacifying/texture agent. Standard low-hazard fatty acid.

ingredients.name: Arachidyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: A C20 fatty alcohol used as an emollient, emulsion stabilizer and thickener; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Arachidyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: An alkyl glucoside (fatty alcohol + glucose) used as a mild O/W emulsifier, typically paired with arachidyl/behenyl alcohol. Benign cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Arctium Lappa Root Extract
ingredients.note: Burdock root extract (inulin, arctiin, polyacetylenes) used as skin-conditioning, soothing and astringent agent; minor allergy risk.

sources.label: Natural Arctium lappa fruit extract improves clinical signs of aging skin - PubMed
Review of pharmacological effects of Arctium lappa (burdock) - PubMed
Aqueous extract of Arctium lappa root enhances skin biology - PMC

ingredients.name: Argan Oil
ingredients.note: Nutrient-rich emollient oil; benign and well-tolerated on skin and hair.

ingredients.name: Argania Spinosa (Argan) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Argan kernel oil rich in unsaturated fatty acids and vitamin E, used as an emollient and antioxidant. CAS 223747-87-3, listed in EU CosIng.

sources.label: Argania spinosa kernel oil - Cosmetic Ingredient Review (CIR)
Argan oil chemical composition, extraction process, quality control - PMC
Oxidative stability of cosmetic argan oil: a one-year study - PubMed

ingredients.name: Arginine
ingredients.note: Amino acid used as humectant and pH adjuster; benign endogenous compound.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Annua Extract
ingredients.note: Sweet wormwood extract with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory phytochemicals. Used as a soothing botanical; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Capillaris Extract
ingredients.note: A plant extract from a traditional East Asian herb studied for anti-inflammatory and skin-calming effects, including in psoriasis and eczema models. It is generally well-tolerated in cream formulations at typical cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: PubMed – Artemisia capillaris extract cream in psoriasis models
PubMed – Inhibitory effects of Artemisia capillaris essential oil on melanogenesis

ingredients.name: Artemisia Capillaris Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Flower extract of capillary wormwood used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical. Asteraceae-family origin means rare contact sensitivity is possible, but treated as benign at use levels.

sources.label: Artemisia capillaris inhibits atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions - PMC
Therapeutic efficacies of Artemisia capillaris extract cream in psoriasis models - PMC
Inhibitory effects of Artemisia capillaris essential oil on melanogenesis - PMC

ingredients.name: Artemisia Princeps Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from Japanese mugwort used in skincare for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects; research shows it can help strengthen the skin barrier and soothe dry or irritated skin. It is generally well tolerated, though those with ragweed/daisy-family allergies may wish to patch test.

sources.label: PMC: Antioxidant Artemisia princeps Extract Enhances Filaggrin and Loricrin Expression
PMC: Artemisia Species as Potential Cosmetic Raw Materials

ingredients.name: Artemisia Princeps Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Mugwort leaf extract used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical. Generally benign topically; Artemisia pollen-allergic individuals may rarely react.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Scoparia Extract
ingredients.note: Whole-plant Artemisia scoparia extract used as a skin-conditioning, soothing antioxidant botanical. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Ascophyllum Nodosum Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed (algae) extract used for skin conditioning/antioxidant and moisturizing effects; standard low-risk botanical marine ingredient.

ingredients.name: Ascorbic acid
ingredients.note: Antioxidant and safe brightener. Can mildly irritate at high strength but no safety concern.

ingredients.name: Ascorbyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Glucose-bound vitamin C derivative converted to ascorbic acid in skin for brightening. Stable and well tolerated; safe.

ingredients.name: Ascorbyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: Lipid-soluble vitamin C ester used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. CIR reviewed ascorbic acid derivatives as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Ascorbyl Propyl Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: This is a hybrid molecule that combines vitamin C (ascorbic acid) with hyaluronic acid, designed to deliver both brightening and hydration benefits in one ingredient. It is a newer ingredient with a good safety profile based on its parent compounds, though specific long-term safety data are still limited.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Ethers and Esters of Ascorbic Acid as Used in Cosmetics – SAGE Journals
Persulfated Ascorbic Acid Glycoside as a Safe and Stable Derivative – PMC

ingredients.name: Asiatic Acid
ingredients.note: Centella asiatica triterpene aglycone with antioxidant and wound-healing activity; CIR found Centella-derived ingredients safe.

ingredients.name: Asiaticoside
ingredients.note: Centella asiatica triterpenoid glycoside that promotes collagen synthesis and calms inflammation; CIR found Centella-derived ingredients safe.

ingredients.name: Asparagopsis Armata Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from a red seaweed with antibacterial and antioxidant properties; early research suggests it may help with acne-prone skin. It appears well tolerated in cosmetic patch tests, but large-scale human safety data is still limited.

sources.label: Therapeutical Potential of Asparagopsis armata Biomass for Acne Vulgaris – MDPI Marine Drugs
Marine Ingredients for Sensitive Skin: Market Overview – MDPI Marine Drugs
Asparagopsis Genus: Biological Activities and Chemical Composition – MDPI Molecules

ingredients.name: Aspartic Acid
ingredients.note: Endogenous amino acid used as a skin/hair conditioning humectant. Covered by the CIR alpha-amino acids safety assessment as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Aspergillus Ferment
ingredients.note: This is a fermentation product from Aspergillus mold, used in skincare to brighten skin, improve moisture, and smooth texture — similar to some K-beauty ferment ingredients. It is generally considered safe; people with very sensitive skin should patch-test first.

sources.label: Study on the Skincare Effects of Red Rice Fermented by Aspergillus oryzae In Vitro (PMC11085780)

ingredients.name: Astaxanthin
ingredients.note: Potent red carotenoid antioxidant from microalgae used for photo-protection and anti-aging. Safe; may impart a faint color to formulas.

ingredients.name: Astragalus Membranaceus Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the root of the astragalus (milk vetch) plant, long used in traditional Chinese medicine. It contains antioxidants and polysaccharides that may help with skin hydration and anti-aging; patch tests at concentrations up to 10% showed no irritation or allergic reactions.

sources.label: PubMed — Astragalus membranaceus Extract for Pigmentary and Anti-Aging Skincare (Systematic Review)
MDPI Cosmetics — Natural Plant-Based Compositions: Astragalus membranaceus and Centella asiatica for Skin Health

ingredients.name: Astrocaryum Murumuru Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Amazonian palm seed butter rich in lauric acid, used as occlusive emollient for skin and hair. CIR ruled it safe in current cosmetic use.

sources.label: Astrocaryum murumuru seed butter - PubChem NIH
Comprehensive analysis of Amazonian murumuru fat (Astrocaryum murumuru) - PubMed
Comprehensive characterization of oils and fats from Colombian Amazon region - PMC

ingredients.name: Astrocaryum Tucumã Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Amazonian tucuma seed butter used as an emollient/skin-conditioning fat; benign cosmetic plant butter.

ingredients.name: Astrocaryum Vulgare (Tucumã) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Amazonian palm kernel oil rich in lauric/oleic acids used as an emollient. Benign plant oil.

ingredients.name: Aureobasidium Pullulans Ferment
ingredients.note: A fermented ingredient from a naturally occurring yeast-like fungus that produces moisturizing and antioxidant compounds. Research shows it can help reduce wrinkles and brighten skin with a good safety profile.

sources.label: PubMed 25682620 – Anti-skin-aging benefits of exopolymers from Aureobasidium pullulans SM2001
PubMed 32197310 – Pullulan for advanced sustainable skin-contact applications

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Bran Extract
ingredients.note: Oat bran extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Extract
ingredients.note: Oat kernel extract rich in beta-glucans and avenanthramides; soothing, anti-inflammatory and hydrating, supports barrier. Generally safe, non-comedogenic.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients - PubMed (doi.org)

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Flour
ingredients.note: Ground oat kernel flour acting as absorbent, bulking and soothing agent; rich in beta-glucan. Well tolerated and non-comedogenic.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients - PubMed

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Emollient oil from oat kernels rich in lipids, valued for soothing and barrier support. Benign and widely used in sensitive-skin products.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients - PubMed

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Protein
