ingredients.note: Mixture of fatty acids from coconut oil used to make soaps/surfactants and as an emulsifier. CIR reviewed coconut-derived fatty acids as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Coconut Alkanes
ingredients.note: Plant-derived hydrocarbon emollient used as a light, volatile silicone/dimethicone alternative. No notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients.note: Occlusive emollient oil; safe and benign, though comedogenic for some acne-prone facial skin.

ingredients.name: Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Derived from the flesh of the coconut, this extract provides hydrating fatty acids, vitamins, and proteins that soften and nourish the skin. The CIR Expert Panel has confirmed it's safe in cosmetics as currently used.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Cocos nucifera-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed – Final report on safety of Cocos nucifera oil and related ingredients

ingredients.name: Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Coconut water (aqueous fruit fraction). Lightweight hydrating humectant; distinct from heavier coconut oil.

ingredients.name: Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil
ingredients.note: Plant-derived emollient and occlusive oil widely used in skin and hair care. CIR reviewed plant oils including coconut oil as safe; comedogenic for some users.

ingredients.name: Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Water
ingredients.note: The natural liquid from inside coconuts, used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent. The CIR Expert Panel found coconut-derived ingredients, including coconut water, safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Cocos nucifera (Coconut)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Cocoyl Hydrolyzed Collagen
ingredients.note: Coconut-acid-acylated hydrolyzed collagen used as a mild cleansing/conditioning surfactant; CIR-reviewed, low-hazard.

ingredients.name: Codium Fragile Extract
ingredients.note: Green seaweed extract used as a skin-conditioning/moisturizing agent. A standard, low-hazard marine botanical.

ingredients.name: Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Leaf/Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Coffee leaf/seed extract used as a botanical antioxidant/skin-conditioning agent; standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Coffee seed extract used as an antioxidant (caffeine, chlorogenic acids) and skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A lightweight emollient oil cold-pressed from coffee beans, rich in fatty acids and antioxidants like tocopherols; it absorbs well and helps moisturize and protect skin. It is well-tolerated and considered safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC11858793 – Coffea arabica: An Emerging Active Ingredient in Dermato-Cosmetic Applications

ingredients.name: Coix Lacryma-Jobi Ma-Yuen Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Job's tears (adlay) seed extract rich in amino acids and polysaccharides used as a hydrating, soothing, brightening botanical. Patch test advised for grain-allergic individuals.

ingredients.name: Coix Lacryma-Jobi Ma-Yuen Seed Powder
ingredients.note: Ground powder from adlay (Job's tears) seeds, used in traditional Asian skincare and medicine; it contains compounds that may help brighten skin and reduce inflammation. It has a long history of safe use.

sources.label: PMC – Coixol from adlay on tyrosinase: future of cosmetics (2022)
PubMed – Adlay Seed ethanolic extract and anti-cancer/cosmetic research (2022)

ingredients.name: Collagen Amino Acid
ingredients.note: Amino acids derived from hydrolyzed collagen used as a skin-identical humectant/moisturizer. Benign cosmetic ingredient (CAS 9015-54-7).

sources.label: PubMed: Safety assessment of animal- and plant-derived amino acids as used in cosmetics
CIR: Safety Assessment of Skin and Connective Tissue-Derived Proteins and Peptides
CIR: Safety Assessment of Tissue-Derived Proteins and Peptides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Collagen Amino Acids
ingredients.note: Amino-acid mixture from hydrolyzed collagen used as a humectant and skin/hair conditioning agent. CIR found amino acids safe as used.

ingredients.name: Collagen Extract
ingredients.note: Animal-derived structural protein used as a film-forming humectant and conditioning agent. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Colloidal Oatmeal
ingredients.note: One of the most universally tolerated actives; beta-glucans and avenanthramides calm, hydrate and reinforce the barrier (FDA skin-protectant).

ingredients.name: Combretum Fruticosum Flower Nectar
ingredients.note: Flower nectar used as a masking/fragrance agent at trace levels. Not on EWG restricted lists; benign as used.

sources.label: PMC: Bioactivities of the Genus Combretum (Combretaceae): A Review

ingredients.name: Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Cera
ingredients.note: Natural palm-leaf wax used as a film former and structuring/texturizing agent in lip and color cosmetics. CIR concluded it is safe; low comedogenicity.

ingredients.name: Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Wax
ingredients.note: Hard plant wax from carnauba palm leaves used as a thickener, film-former and structuring agent in sticks/balms. CIR concluded safe; non-comedogenic, low sensitization.

ingredients.name: Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Wax Extract
ingredients.note: A hard plant wax from carnauba palm leaves used as a thickener/structuring and film-forming agent; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic wax.

ingredients.name: Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Wax/Cera Carnauba/Cire de Carnauba
ingredients.note: Plant wax from carnauba palm used as a thickener/film-former/structuring agent; CIR-reviewed waxes are safe as used.

ingredients.name: Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Wax/Copernicia Cerifera Cera
ingredients.note: Hard plant wax from carnauba palm leaves used as a structurant, film former and emollient in sticks/mascara. CAS 8015-86-9, reviewed by CIR.

ingredients.name: Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Wax/Copernicia Cerifera Cera/Cire de Carnauba
ingredients.note: Plant wax from carnauba palm used as a thickener/film-former/structuring agent; CIR-reviewed waxes are safe as used.

ingredients.name: Copper Gluconate
ingredients.note: Copper salt of gluconic acid used as a chelating/skin-conditioning agent at low levels; CIR concluded it is safe as used in cosmetics (2024).

ingredients.name: Copper Lysinate/Prolinate
ingredients.note: This is a copper complex with two amino acids (lysine and proline) used in anti-aging products to support collagen synthesis and skin repair, similar to well-studied copper peptides. Copper is an essential trace element and this form is considered safe for topical use.

sources.label: PubMed – Biological activities of peptides: skin penetration of copper complexes
PMC – Human skin penetration of a copper tripeptide in vitro

ingredients.name: Copper Palmitoyl Heptapeptide-14
ingredients.note: A synthetic copper-peptide complex that aims to boost collagen and elastin production in skin for an anti-aging effect; used at very low concentrations (<0.001%) and considered safe for topical use.

sources.label: PMC: A framework for the safety evaluation of peptides in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Copper Tripeptide-1
ingredients.note: Copper-bound signal peptide used at low cosmetic levels for repair and firming. Well tolerated; can be unstable when combined with strong acids or vitamin C.

ingredients.name: Coptis Chinensis Root Extract
ingredients.note: Chinese goldthread root extract (berberine-rich) used as a soothing/antimicrobial antioxidant botanical. Generally tolerated; patch test advised for sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed: Fractionated Coptis chinensis Extract Suppresses P. acnes-Stimulated Inflammation in Human Keratinocytes
PMC: Effect of Rhizoma Coptidis and Coptis chinensis aqueous extract on radiation-induced skin injury
PMC: Integrated transcriptomic and metabolomic analysis of the antibacterial mechanism of Rhizoma Coptidis extract

ingredients.name: Coptis Japonica Root Extract
ingredients.note: Alkaloid-rich (berberine, palmatine) root extract used as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial botanical. Low topical risk.

sources.label: PubMed: Coptis japonica root extract induces apoptosis in SNU-668 human gastric cancer cells
PubMed: Fractionated Coptis chinensis Extract and Its Bioactive Component (berberine) Suppress P. acnes-Stimulated Inflammation

ingredients.name: Corallina Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Red coralline algae extract used as a mineral-rich conditioning and soothing marine botanical. Standard well-tolerated extract.

ingredients.name: Corchorus Olitorius Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Jute (molokhia) leaf extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign standard plant ingredient.

ingredients.name: Cordyceps Militaris Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of the Cordyceps militaris fungus used as a skin-conditioning/antioxidant agent. Benign mushroom-derived cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Crambe Abyssinica Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Lightweight plant seed oil rich in erucic acid used as an emollient, occlusive and film-former with a silicone-like feel. Topically well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Crataegus Cuneata Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Hawthorn fruit extract rich in flavonoids used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign cosmetic plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed: Comprehensive strategy integrating metabolomics for discovery of active markers in hawthorn (Crataegus cuneata) fruits
PubMed: Polyphenolic content and bioactivities of Crataegus species

ingredients.name: Creatine
ingredients.note: An endogenous amino-acid derivative used topically as a cell-energizing, antioxidant and skin-conditioning active. Not formally assessed by CIR but widely used and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Crithmum Maritimum Extract
ingredients.note: Aerial-part extract of sea fennel rich in phenolics and vitamins, used for soothing and antioxidant skin conditioning. Low irritation risk; rare allergy possible in Apiaceae-sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Cryptomeria Japonica Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese cedar leaf extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning and protecting agent; standard botanical.

sources.label: PubMed: Cryptomeria japonica essential oil inhibits the growth of drug-resistant skin pathogens
PubMed: Biological Activities of Extracts from Different Parts of Cryptomeria japonica
PubMed: Whitening and antioxidant activities of bornyl acetate from Cryptomeria japonica essential oil

ingredients.name: Cucumis Melo (Melon) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Melon fruit extract used as a hydrating, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Cucumis Melo (Melon) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Melon seed extract used as a conditioning/antioxidant botanical. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Cucumis Melo Cantalupensis Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Cantaloupe melon fruit extract used as a botanical skin-conditioning/antioxidant agent; benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Cucumis Sativus (Cucumber) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Soothing cucumber fruit extract used as emollient and skin conditioner; CIR found cucumber-derived ingredients safe as used.

ingredients.name: Cucumis Sativus (Cucumber) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Light cucumber seed oil used as a skin-conditioning emollient. Standard benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Cucurbita Pepo (Pumpkin) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Pumpkin seed extract used as a skin-conditioning antioxidant botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Cucurbita Pepo (Pumpkin) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Pumpkin seed oil rich in fatty acids and tocopherols, used as a conditioning emollient. Benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Cupressus Sempervirens Leaf/Nut/Stem Oil
ingredients.note: Essential oil distilled from the leaves, cones, and stems of Mediterranean cypress; used in perfumery and cosmetics for its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Safety testing in zebrafish embryos supports a good safety profile at cosmetic levels.

sources.label: Cupressus sempervirens Essential Oil: antibacterial, toxicity, and safety assessment – PMC
Biological Activity of Cupressus sempervirens Essential Oil – PMC

ingredients.name: Curcuma Longa (Turmeric) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the leaves of the turmeric plant that contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds; it is used in cosmetics to help brighten skin and soothe irritation. Some people with turmeric allergies may react to it, but it is generally safe.

sources.label: PMC – Curcuma Longa: from traditional applications to modern plant medicine research

ingredients.name: Curcuma Longa (Turmeric) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Turmeric root extract used as an antioxidant, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Cutibacterium Granulosum Ferment Extract Filtrate
ingredients.note: Skin-microbiome bacterial ferment filtrate used as an emollient/skin-conditioning active with reported antioxidant and barrier-support effects. Standard, low-concern microbiome ingredient.

sources.label: PubMed: Propionibacterium (Cutibacterium) granulosum – characteristics and ferment properties
PMC: Transcriptomic Analysis of Human Keratinocytes Treated with Galactomyces Ferment Filtrate

ingredients.name: Cyamopsis Tetragonoloba (Guar) Gum
ingredients.note: Plant-derived galactomannan polysaccharide used as a thickener/stabilizer. CIR galactomannan review found no sensitization signal; safe as used.

ingredients.name: Cyanocobalamin
ingredients.note: Vitamin B12, a cobalt-containing antioxidant/anti-inflammatory used in skin and hair products; imparts a pink hue. Considered safe topically.

ingredients.name: Cyclodextrin
ingredients.note: Cyclodextrins are donut-shaped sugar molecules used in cosmetics to encapsulate fragrance or active ingredients, improving stability and controlled release. Toxicology reviews confirm good skin tolerability when used in topical products.

sources.label: PubMed – Effect of hydroxypropyl-β-cyclodextrin in semi-solid emulsions on skin parameters

ingredients.name: Cyclopentasiloxane
ingredients.note: Volatile silicone giving a light, dry feel; safe in cosmetics, with environmental rather than skin-safety considerations.

ingredients.name: Cynanchum Atratum Extract
ingredients.note: Asian plant extract used as antioxidant, soothing and barrier-strengthening botanical for sensitive/dry skin. No serious adverse reactions reported.

sources.label: PubMed: Cynanchum atratum inhibits the development of atopic dermatitis in DNCB-induced mice
PMC: Cynanchi atrati and Its Phenolic Constituent Sinapic Acid Target RCAN1 to Control Skin Inflammation
PMC: Anti-Melanogenic Effects of Fractioned Cynanchum atratum by Regulation of cAMP/MITF Pathway

ingredients.name: Cynara Scolymus (Artichoke) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Antioxidant, anti-inflammatory botanical skin-conditioning extract; rare irritation in those allergic to artichoke.

ingredients.name: Cyperus Rotundus Root Extract
ingredients.note: Botanical root extract used as a skin-conditioning and anti-inflammatory agent at low concentrations. Generally well tolerated; patch test advised for botanical-sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed: Preclinical study of the topical anti-inflammatory activity of Cyperus rotundus L. extract in models of skin inflammation
PubMed: Topical Cyperus rotundus essential oil for treatment of axillary hyperpigmentation: a randomized, double-blind study
PubMed: The extract of Cyperus rotundus rhizome promotes hair growth and modulates hair cycle

ingredients.name: Cysteine
ingredients.note: Sulfur-containing amino acid used as an antioxidant, hair-waving and conditioning agent. Considered safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Cystoseira Tamariscifolia Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from a brown seaweed species rich in phenolic antioxidants; it is used in cosmetics for its antioxidant and preservative properties. Studies show low toxicity and strong free-radical-scavenging activity.

sources.label: PubMed – Methanol extracts from Cystoseira tamariscifolia and C. nodicaulis

ingredients.name: Daucus Carota Sativa (Carrot) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Carrot root extract (carotenoid source) used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign food-derived extract.

ingredients.name: Daucus Carota Sativa (Carrot) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Carrot seed oil used as an emollient/skin-conditioning botanical; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Daucus Carota Sativa Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from cultivated carrot roots, rich in beta-carotene and antioxidant phenolics, used in cosmetics for skin-conditioning and anti-aging effects. It has a well-established safety profile in topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC – The Wild Carrot (Daucus carota): A Phytochemical and Pharmacological Review

ingredients.name: Daucus Carota Sativa Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from cultivated carrot roots, rich in beta-carotene (a vitamin A precursor) and antioxidants, used to condition skin and give a healthy glow. It is well-tolerated and widely recognized as safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC – Wild carrot (Daucus carota): a phytochemical and pharmacological review

ingredients.name: Davidsonia Jerseyana Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Davidson's plum is an Australian rainforest fruit extraordinarily rich in anthocyanin antioxidants, used in skincare for anti-aging and brightening benefits. It has no known toxicity concerns and has a good safety record in Australian cosmetic use.

sources.label: Untargeted and Targeted Metabolomic Profiling of Australian Indigenous Fruits (incl. Davidsonia jerseyana) — PMC

ingredients.name: DC Black 2 (CI 77266)
ingredients.note: A very fine black pigment (carbon black) used in eye makeup like mascaras and eyeliners to create deep dark color. It is considered safe on intact skin at up to 10%, but in nano-particle form and in sprays there is an unresolved concern about inhalation, so precaution applies to aerosol or powder formats.

sources.label: SCCS – Opinion on Carbon Black CI 77266 (nano-form)
EC Health – Revision of Opinion on Carbon Black (nano-form)

ingredients.name: Decyl Cocoate
ingredients.note: A lightweight ester made from decyl alcohol and coconut fatty acids, used to give skin a smooth, non-greasy feel. It is reviewed under the CIR alkyl esters assessment and is considered safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Decyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Gentle sugar-derived nonionic surfactant common in sensitive-skin and sulfate-free formulas. Low irritancy; rare allergy reports are uncommon.

ingredients.name: Decyl Isostearate
ingredients.note: A lightweight ester made from decyl alcohol and isostearic acid that spreads easily on skin to provide a smooth, non-greasy feel and moisture. It is considered safe and non-irritating in standard cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Decylene Glycol
ingredients.note: A mild preservative and antimicrobial ingredient (a 10-carbon diol) that also has skin-conditioning properties; it helps keep products from growing bacteria and mold. Generally regarded as well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Dehydroacetic Acid
ingredients.note: Pyranone-based preservative authorized in the EU up to 0.6% (not permitted in aerosol sprays). Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Denatonium Benzoate
ingredients.note: Extremely bitter denaturant/aversive agent (e.g., anti-nail-biting); generally low toxicity but rare contact urticaria reported, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Desamido Collagen
ingredients.note: A chemically modified form of collagen where some of the amino groups are removed, making it more water-soluble; it is used as a conditioning and film-forming agent in skincare and hair care. Generally considered safe.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Tissue-Derived Proteins and Peptides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Dextran
ingredients.note: Microbial glucose polysaccharide used as a moisturizing, smoothing and stabilizing agent. CIR found microbial polysaccharide gums safe as used; GRAS as a food additive.

ingredients.name: Dextrin
ingredients.note: Starch-derived polysaccharide used as a binder/viscosity and absorbent agent; benign.

ingredients.name: Dextrin Isostearate
ingredients.note: Dextrin fatty-acid ester used as an oil-phase gelling/thickening agent; related dextrin esters showed no ocular irritation.

ingredients.name: Dextrin Palmitate
ingredients.note: Esterified starch (dextrin + palmitic acid) used as an oil-gelling/thickening agent, emulsifier and anticaking agent. Standard texture ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Diammonium Citrate
ingredients.note: A salt formed from citric acid and ammonia that acts as a pH adjuster and chelating agent in cosmetic formulas, helping to stabilize the product and improve the performance of other ingredients. It is considered safe at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Diamond Powder
ingredients.note: Finely milled crystalline carbon used as a physical exfoliant/abrasive agent. CAS 7782-40-3, EINECS 231-953-2; inert and non-toxic.

sources.label: Biocompatibility and safety of diamond nanoparticles on reconstructed human epidermis (PMC)

ingredients.name: Dibutyl Adipate
ingredients.note: Adipic acid diester used as a lightweight emollient and solvent. CIR reviewed dibutyl adipate as safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Dibutyl Ethylhexanoyl Glutamide
ingredients.note: An amino-acid-based oil gellant used with dibutyl lauroyl glutamide to structure anhydrous and oil-rich formulas. Standard, low-hazard texture agent.

ingredients.name: Dibutyl Lauroyl Glutamide
ingredients.note: An amino-acid-derived oil gelling agent that self-assembles to thicken anhydrous oils in lipsticks and gels. Benign, low-molecular-weight texture agent.

sources.label: PubChem compound summary – N-Lauroyl-L-glutamic acid di-n-butylamide (CID 113401)
PubChem compound summary – Lauroyl glutamic dibutylamide (CID 12049931)

ingredients.name: Dicalcium Phosphate
ingredients.note: An inorganic calcium phosphate salt used as an abrasive, bulking and pH/texture agent (common in toothpaste). Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Dicaprylyl Carbonate
ingredients.note: Lightweight dialkyl carbonate ester emollient that improves spreadability. Reviewed as a safe, non-irritating cosmetic emollient.

ingredients.name: Dicaprylyl Ether
ingredients.note: Light, fast-spreading fatty ether emollient; CIR (Fatty Ethers) assessed as safe, non-sensitizing and non-comedogenic.

ingredients.name: Diethoxyethyl Succinate
ingredients.note: Synthetic succinate diester used as an emollient/solvent. Standard cosmetic ingredient with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Diethylamino Hydroxybenzoyl Hexyl Benzoate
ingredients.note: Photostable UVA filter (Uvinul A Plus) reaffirmed safe by the EU SCCS in 2025, with low skin penetration. Often paired with UVB filters for stable broad-spectrum coverage.

ingredients.name: Diethylhexyl Butamido Triazone
ingredients.note: Photostable organic UVB/UVA-II filter approved in the EU up to 10%; negligible skin penetration with no genotoxic, irritant, or sensitizing concern.

ingredients.name: Diethylhexyl Carbonate
ingredients.note: Synthetic dialkyl carbonate ester used as a light emollient; judged safe by CIR when formulated to be non-irritating. Benign cosmetic emollient.

ingredients.name: Diethylhexyl Sodium Sulfosuccinate
ingredients.note: This is a mild sulfosuccinate surfactant (wetting and cleansing agent) used in small amounts in cosmetics to help ingredients spread or foam gently. Sulfosuccinates are generally considered milder than harsh detergents like SLS.

ingredients.name: Diethylhexyl Succinate
ingredients.note: A lightweight synthetic emollient ester that gives products a silky feel without a greasy finish. It is considered safe and non-irritating at cosmetic use concentrations.

sources.label: Sagepub – CIR Safety Assessment of Dicarboxylic Acids, Salts, and Esters (2012)

ingredients.name: Diethylhexyl Syringylidenemalonate
ingredients.note: A synthetic antioxidant and photostabilizer used in sunscreens to boost UV protection and stabilize unstable UV filters like avobenzone. In vitro studies indicate no significant cytotoxicity at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: Ultraviolet Filters for Cosmetic Applications – MDPI Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Diglucosyl Gallic Acid
ingredients.note: Biotech-derived glucoside of gallic acid used as a skin-brightening antioxidant that inhibits melanin synthesis. No notable safety concerns reported at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Diglycerin
ingredients.note: A dimer of glycerin used as a humectant and skin conditioning agent. Benign, low-irritation moisturizing ingredient.

ingredients.name: Diheptyl Succinate
ingredients.note: Succinic-acid diester used as lightweight silicone-alternative emollient, often paired with capryloyl glycerin/sebacic acid copolymer. Biodegradable, EU-permitted.

sources.label: PMC – Di-heptyl succinate as non-phthalate plasticizer, toxicology study

ingredients.name: Diisooctyl Succinate
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic ester used as a lightweight, non-greasy skin emollient in lotions and creams. It is listed in the EU CosIng database with no restrictions and has a low hazard profile.

ingredients.name: Diisopropyl Adipate
ingredients.note: Diisopropyl adipate is a synthetic ester used in cosmetics as a lightweight emollient and skin-softening agent that gives products a silky, non-greasy feel. The CIR Expert Panel has confirmed it is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Diisopropyl Sebacate
ingredients.note: Synthetic diester of isopropyl alcohol and sebacic acid used as a lightweight emollient/solvent; PubChem CID 24096, minimally irritating.

ingredients.name: Diisostearoyl Polyglyceryl-3 Dimer Dilinoleate
ingredients.note: A complex ester emulsifier derived from vegetable-based dimer acids and polyglycerin, used to give cosmetics a smooth, velvety texture and to help stabilize water-in-oil formulas. It is considered safe in cosmetics in the present practices of use and concentration.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Diisostearyl Malate
ingredients.note: Malic-acid diester emollient providing gloss and slip, common in lip products; well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Dilauryl Thiodipropionate
ingredients.note: Thiodipropionic acid diester used as an antioxidant/stabilizer; CIR found it safe when formulated to be non-irritating and it is FDA GRAS. Non-sensitizing in studies.

ingredients.name: Dimer Dilinoleyl Dimer Dilinoleate
ingredients.note: Ester of dimer dilinoleyl alcohol and dimer dilinoleic acid used as a thickening emollient/structuring agent in lip and color products. Benign.

sources.label: CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Dialkyl Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed – Amended Safety Assessment of Dialkyl Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Inert silicone that smooths skin and hair and reduces water loss; safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Dimethiconol
ingredients.note: Silicone conditioning agent for slip and shine; inert and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Dimethyl Sulfone
ingredients.note: Organic sulfur compound (MSM) used as an anti-inflammatory, soothing and barrier-supporting agent. GRAS for topical use and generally non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Dimethylmethoxy Chromanyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: Marketed as Chromabright™, this synthetic ingredient is a powerful skin brightener that works by blocking melanin production — studies show it is more effective than kojic acid at reducing dark spots while being much gentler than hydroquinone. It has been tested and found non-irritating and non-cytotoxic at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: Development of a new cosmetic active for safe skin brightening – Wiley International Journal of Cosmetic Science

ingredients.name: Dimethylsilanol Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Silicon-grafted hyaluronic acid derivative used as a humectant and skin-conditioning moisture binder. Widely regarded as safe and well tolerated, including on sensitive skin.

sources.label: CIR Final Report: Safety Assessment of Hyaluronates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Dioscorea Japonica Root Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese yam root extract used as a soothing, moisturizing skin-conditioning botanical. Standard low-hazard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Diospyros Kaki Calyx Extract
ingredients.note: Persimmon calyx extract containing sugars, vitamins, tannins and flavonoids, used as an antioxidant/humectant/skin-conditioning botanical. Rare reports of mild irritation.

sources.label: PubMed – Suppressive effect of Diospyros kaki calyx on atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions
PubMed – Diospyros kaki calyx inhibits immediate-type hypersensitivity via reduction of mast cell activation
PMC – Topical cleanser with Diospyros kaki folium reduces skin oil content and pore size

ingredients.name: Diospyros Kaki Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Persimmon leaf extract rich in tannins, flavonoids and vitamin C; antioxidant, astringent and skin-protecting agent.

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Hexa C5-9 Acid Esters
ingredients.note: Synthetic hexaester used as a non-greasy emollient and texture enhancer. Oxidation-resistant; standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Hexahydroxystearate
ingredients.note: Hexaester of pentaerythritol dimer and stearic acid used as an emollient/viscosity agent and lanolin alternative. No known safety or irritancy concerns at typical use levels.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Pentaerythrityl Tetraesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Hexahydroxystearate/Hexastearate/Hexarosinate
ingredients.note: Complex dipentaerythritol ester blend used as a thickening emollient/film former, common in color cosmetics. Standard benign ester.

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Tetrahydroxystearate/Tetraisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyol fatty-acid ester used as a heavy emollient, binder and pigment-wetting agent in color cosmetics. Standard inert ester material.

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Tri-Polyhydroxystearate
ingredients.note: Polyhydroxystearic acid ester of dipentaerythritol used as a water-resistant emollient, pigment dispersant and W/O emulsion stabilizer. CAS 855310-95-1.

ingredients.name: Dipeptide-2
ingredients.note: Small synthetic peptide (valyl-tryptophan) marketed for reducing puffiness and improving lymphatic drainage. Low hazard cosmetic peptide.

ingredients.name: Diphenyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Phenyl-substituted silicone used as an emollient and conditioning agent giving slip and shine. Silicones are reviewed as safe and non-irritating in cosmetic use by CIR.

ingredients.name: Diphenyl Dimethicone/Vinyl Diphenyl Dimethicone/Silsesquioxane Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Crosslinked silicone resin (silsesquioxane crosspolymer) used as a film-former and texture/long-wear agent. Standard, low-concern silicone polymer.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Dimethicone Crosspolymers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Diphenylsiloxy Phenyl Trimethicone
ingredients.note: Phenyl silicone fluid used as a conditioning, film-forming and shine-enhancing agent; generally considered safe and non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR Final Report: Safety Assessment of Linear Phenyl-Substituted Methicones as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Dipropylene Glycol
ingredients.note: Low-irritation solvent and viscosity agent considered low concern in cosmetic concentrations. Generally well tolerated with minimal sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Disiloxane
ingredients.note: Volatile light silicone fluid used as solvent/carrier giving silky spreadability; evaporates rather than absorbs.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alkoxy Polysiloxanes as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Disodium Cocoamphodiacetate
ingredients.note: Very mild amphoteric surfactant common in baby shampoos and sensitive-skin cleansers. Low irritation and good ocular tolerance.

ingredients.name: Disodium Cocoyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Disodium salt of cocoyl glutamate offering mild cleansing and foam at higher pH. Gentle and well tolerated on skin.

ingredients.name: Disodium Cocoyl Glycinate
ingredients.note: A gentle, amino acid-based surfactant derived from coconut oil and glycine that cleanses without stripping the skin's natural oils. It is considered a mild, skin-compatible cleanser suitable for sensitive skin formulas.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Amino Acid Alkyl Amides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Disodium EDTA
ingredients.note: Chelating agent that binds metal ions to boost preservative efficacy and stability. Used at low levels and considered safe; poorly biodegradable is an environmental, not skin, concern.

ingredients.name: Disodium Lauroamphodiacetate
ingredients.note: A mild, amphoteric (both positive and negative charge) cleanser derived from lauric acid that is gentler on skin than many other detergents; it is commonly used in baby washes and sensitive-skin cleansers. It is considered safe and is less irritating than sodium lauryl sulfate.

ingredients.name: Disodium Lauryl Sulfosuccinate
ingredients.note: Mild non-ethoxylated sulfosuccinate surfactant used to soften harsher cleansers and boost foam. Low irritation and no 1,4-dioxane concern.

ingredients.name: Disodium Phosphate
ingredients.note: Inorganic salt used as a buffering/pH-adjusting agent. Standard benign cosmetic excipient.

ingredients.name: Disodium Stearoyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Amino-acid-derived (glutamate) gentle emulsifier/dispersant. Recognized as safe and very mild, suitable for sensitive skin; rare isolated irritation reports.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Amino Acid Alkyl Amides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Distarch Phosphate
ingredients.note: This is a chemically modified starch used as a thickener, binder, and anti-caking agent in cosmetics such as powders. CIR concluded it is safe in cosmetics at current practices of use and concentration.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Starch Phosphates as Used in Cosmetics (Final 2022)

ingredients.name: Disteardimonium Hectorite
ingredients.note: Modified clay used as a non-surfactant suspending/gelling agent; CIR found alkonium/ammonium hectorites safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Distearyldimonium Chloride
ingredients.note: Cationic quaternary-ammonium conditioning agent used for antistatic and hair-conditioning effects; concentrated forms can irritate but it is safe as used in rinse-off products.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alkonium Clays (including disteardimonium hectorite) as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Dodecane
ingredients.note: Volatile C12 hydrocarbon used as fast-evaporating solvent/carrier and lightweight emollient in long-wear makeup and sunscreens. Generally safe; mild irritation possible.

ingredients.name: Drometrizole Trisiloxane
ingredients.note: Photostable broad-spectrum filter (L'Oreal Mexoryl XL) with a large silicone-bearing molecule that limits skin penetration. Good safety and tolerability profile; long used in the EU.

ingredients.name: Dunaliella Salina Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from Dunaliella salina, a salt-water microalgae that is one of the richest natural sources of beta-carotene and other carotenoids; it is used in anti-aging skincare for antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits. Clinical studies show it is well tolerated and can help reduce visible signs of skin aging.

sources.label: PMC – A Dunaliella salina Extract Counteracts Skin Aging under Intense Solar Irradiation
PubMed – In vivo antioxidant activity of carotenoids from Dunaliella salina

ingredients.name: Ecamsule
ingredients.note: Water-soluble, photostable UVA filter (Mexoryl SX) with very low skin absorption due to its size and polarity. Strong safety record and FDA-approved by NDA route in select products.

ingredients.name: Echinacea Angustifolia Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the root of narrow-leaf coneflower, used in cosmetics for its antioxidant and soothing properties. It is generally well tolerated on skin; research shows the key compound echinacoside has antioxidant activity and supports wound healing without cytotoxicity at cosmetic levels.

sources.label: PubMed — Phytochemical Composition and Wound Healing Properties of Echinacea angustifolia Root Extract
PMC — Phytochemical and Wound Healing Study (full text)

ingredients.name: Echinacea Purpurea Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from echinacea flowers and roots known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties; it can help calm irritated skin and may improve hydration. Rarely, people allergic to plants in the daisy family may react to it.

sources.label: PubMed – Skin improvement and stability of Echinacea purpurea formulations (2010)
PubMed – Echinacea purpurea alkylamides anti-inflammatory in atopic eczema (2017)

ingredients.name: Echinacea Purpurea Root Extract
ingredients.note: An herbal extract from the purple coneflower root with soothing, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant benefits for skin. It is generally well tolerated, though people with ragweed allergies (same plant family) should use caution.

sources.label: PubMed 30538520 – Echinacea purpurea extract in treatment of pruritus
PubMed 20384903 – Skin improvement and stability of Echinacea purpurea dermatological formulations

ingredients.name: Echium Plantagineum Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A plant-based oil unusually rich in omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids (including stearidonic acid and gamma-linolenic acid) that support the skin barrier and help reduce inflammation. Safe for topical use; great option for dry or inflamed skin.

sources.label: Chemical characterization of Echium plantagineum seed oil – PubMed
Alternative oil extraction methods from Echium plantagineum seeds – PubMed

ingredients.name: Ecklonia Cava Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed extract rich in phlorotannins used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning agent; covered by CIR's brown-algae safety assessment. Benign as used.

ingredients.name: Eclipta Prostrata Extract
ingredients.note: Ayurvedic herb extract (bhringraj) used mainly in hair-care for follicle support; a benign, well-tolerated botanical.

sources.label: PubMed – Potent antioxidative and UVB protective effect of water extract of Eclipta prostrata L
PMC – Eclipta prostrata: Ethnomedicinal uses, chemical constituents, and biological activities
PMC – Anti-Inflammatory Potential of Eclipta prostrata Leaf-Derived Ointment in Atopic Dermatitis

ingredients.name: Eclipta Prostrata Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Eclipta prostrata (false daisy/bhringraj) leaf extract used as a conditioning/soothing botanical, common in hair care. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Ectoin
ingredients.note: Bacteria-derived osmolyte that hydrates, protects, and soothes the skin barrier. Very well tolerated with minimal irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Elaeis Guineensis (Palm) Oil
ingredients.note: Occlusive emollient plant oil; CIR concluded safe as used, advising cosmetic grades keep polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon contaminants minimal.

ingredients.name: Elaeis Guineensis Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Oil pressed from the seeds of the African oil palm, rich in lauric acid and used in cosmetics as an emollient and emulsifier. The CIR has reviewed plant-derived fatty acid oils, including palm kernel oil, and found them safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR – Elaeis guineensis (palm) kernel oil ingredient page
CIR – Safety Assessment of Plant-Derived Fatty Acid Oils (supplement manuscript)

ingredients.name: Elastin
ingredients.note: Connective-tissue structural protein used as a film-forming skin-conditioning agent; precautionary due to animal/bovine sourcing considerations in CIR tissue-derived protein review.

ingredients.name: Elettaria Cardamomum Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Cardamom essential oil used as a fragrance/conditioning botanical; generally well tolerated with mild irritation possible at high levels. Benign at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: PubMed – Elettaria cardamomum (L.) Maton Essential Oil
PMC – Antioxidant Activity and GC-MS Profile of Cardamom Essential Oil
PMC – Recent advances in extraction, chemical composition, and delivery of cardamom phytochemicals

ingredients.name: Ensulizole
ingredients.note: Water-soluble UVB filter favored in light lotions for its non-greasy feel and minimal skin penetration. Generally well tolerated, though it can generate reactive oxygen species under UV, so antioxidant pairing is sensible.

ingredients.name: Epigallocatechin Gallate
ingredients.note: Green tea polyphenol with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Safe at cosmetic levels; antioxidant, safe.

ingredients.name: Epigallocatechin Gallatyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: This is a stabilized, water-soluble form of EGCG (a potent green tea antioxidant), made by attaching a glucose molecule. It penetrates skin well and has been shown in clinical trials to reduce dark spots and brighten skin tone without known safety concerns.

sources.label: PMC – EGCG enzymatic glucosylation potentiates skin-lightening activity

ingredients.name: Epilobium Fleischeri Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from alpine willowherb, used in skincare for its 5-alpha-reductase inhibiting and anti-inflammatory properties, making it helpful for oily or acne-prone skin. It is generally considered safe for topical use and is approved by ECOCERT/COSMOS for natural cosmetics.

sources.label: PubMed: 5-α reductase inhibition by Epilobium fleischeri extract modulates facial microbiota
PMC: 5-α reductase inhibition by Epilobium fleischeri extract modulates facial microbiota structure

ingredients.name: Equisetum Arvense Extract
ingredients.note: Equisetum arvense (common horsetail) is a plant rich in silica and antioxidants used in skincare for its anti-inflammatory and collagen-supporting properties. It is generally well tolerated topically; however, internal use of concentrated forms is not recommended due to possible kidney effects.

sources.label: Preparation and evaluation of anti-wrinkle cream with Equisetum arvense – PMC
Phytochemical investigation of Equisetum arvense and anti-inflammatory potential in keratinocytes – PMC
Equisetum arvense modulates inflammatory immunocompetent cells – PMC

ingredients.name: Ergothioneine
ingredients.note: Naturally occurring amino-acid-derived antioxidant that accumulates in skin cells and scavenges free radicals. Very well tolerated; antioxidant, safe.

ingredients.name: Eruca Sativa Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Arugula/rocket leaf extract (Brassicaceae) used as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory skin conditioner. CAS 90028-42-5.

sources.label: PubMed – Eruca sativa and its flavonoid components improve skin barrier function via PPAR-α activation
PubMed – Eruca sativa seed extract: A novel natural product
PMC – Quantitative Analysis and Molecular Docking of Flavonols from Eruca sativa and Their Effect on Skin Barrier Function

ingredients.name: Eryngium Maritimum Extract
ingredients.note: Sea holly (Apiaceae) extract used as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory skin-conditioning botanical rich in polyphenols and flavonoids.

ingredients.name: Erythritol
ingredients.note: Sugar-alcohol humectant; benign, non-irritating and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Ethoxydiglycol
ingredients.note: Glycol ether solvent and penetration enhancer. CIR concluded safe; note as a penetration enhancer it can increase uptake of co-ingredients.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Acetate
ingredients.note: Widely used nail polish and remover solvent; relatively benign but an eye/respiratory irritant and highly flammable.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Ferulate
ingredients.note: Ethyl ferulate is a fat-soluble ester of ferulic acid (a potent plant antioxidant), which gives it excellent UV-protective and anti-inflammatory properties in creams and serums. It has been shown in multiple studies to be safe, non-irritating, and effective as a sunscreen booster.

sources.label: Ethyl Ferulate, a Component with Anti-Inflammatory Properties for Emulsion-Based Creams – PMC

ingredients.name: Ethyl Linoleate
ingredients.note: Ethyl Linoleate is the ethyl ester of linoleic acid (an essential omega-6 fatty acid), providing lightweight emollient and barrier-repairing benefits, and is sometimes used to address sebum imbalances in acne-prone skin. It's a gentle ingredient with a strong safety record.

sources.label: ScienceDirect – Composition based on ethyl ester of linoleic acid for treatment of seborrhea and acne

ingredients.name: Ethyl Macadamiate
aliases: Ethyl ester of macadamia oil fatty acids
ingredients.note: An ester derived from macadamia nut oil and ethanol, used as a lightweight emollient that gives skin a smooth, non-greasy feel. It is well-tolerated and considered very safe for topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC – Clinical Efficacy in Skin Hydration and Reducing Wrinkles of Nanoemulsions Containing Macadamia integrifolia Seed Oil

ingredients.name: Ethyl Menthane Carboxamide
ingredients.note: A synthetic cooling agent (known as WS-3) that creates a refreshing sensation on skin without the strong scent of menthol. It is widely used in lip balms, oral care, and skin products; it has a favorable safety profile at recommended concentrations.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Olivate
ingredients.note: A lightweight ester derived from olive oil used as an emollient to soften and smooth skin without feeling greasy. It is very well tolerated and considered safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Olea europaea (Olive)-Derived Ingredients (2023)

ingredients.name: Ethyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: A simple, lightweight ester formed from ethanol and palmitic acid that gives products a silky feel without heaviness and helps moisturize skin. It is considered safe and is not associated with significant irritation or sensitization.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Stearate
ingredients.note: A lightweight ester of stearic acid and ethanol that conditions and softens skin without feeling heavy or greasy. It has a well-established safety record in cosmetics and is considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Ethylcellulose
ingredients.note: A cellulose-derived polymer used as a film-former and thickener in cosmetics and pharmaceuticals; it forms a thin, flexible coating on skin or nails. It is inert, not absorbed, and considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Ethylene/Propylene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic polyolefin polymer used as a viscosity/texture agent and binder; large inert polymer with negligible skin penetration.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Styrene and Vinyl-type Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics (includes ethylene/propylene/styrene copolymer)
CIR Safety Assessment of Polyene Group as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic block copolymer used as a viscosity-controlling/gelling agent for oil-based formulas. Considered safe; covered by CIR styrene/vinyl polymers assessment, allergic reactions rare.

ingredients.name: Ethylhexanediol
ingredients.note: Alkane diol functioning as a solvent/humectant and preservative-boosting agent. CIR assessed alkane diols as safe at present cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Cocoate
ingredients.note: This is a lightweight ester derived from coconut oil fatty acids used to soften and moisturize skin without a heavy or greasy feel. It belongs to the CIR-reviewed class of alkyl esters and is generally considered safe.

sources.label: CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Hydroxystearate
ingredients.note: A lightweight ester made from hydroxystearic acid and 2-ethylhexanol; it spreads easily on skin and is used as an emollient to make products feel smooth and silky without being heavy or greasy. It is considered safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Methoxycrylene
ingredients.note: A UV absorber/photostabilizer that stabilizes UV filters such as avobenzone and retinyl palmitate; minimal dermal absorption. Used at low levels in sunscreens.

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Olivate
ingredients.note: Ester of ethylhexyl alcohol and olive oil fatty acids used as a lightweight emollient; olive-derived, well tolerated.

sources.label: CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: Lightweight palmitic-acid ester emollient with dry skin feel; CIR-reviewed alkyl ester considered safe.

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Stearate
ingredients.note: Ester of 2-ethylhexyl alcohol and stearic acid used as an emollient. CIR assessed safe; essentially non-irritating and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Ethylhexyl Triazone
ingredients.note: EU-approved organic UVB filter (max 5%) with very low dermal penetration and no evidence of genotoxicity or sensitization per SCCS. Not approved as a sunscreen active in the US.

sources.label: PMC – Quantification of Sunscreen Ethylhexyl Triazone in Topical Skin-Care Products by TLC/Densitometry
PubMed – Ethylhexyl triazone sunscreen and its disinfection byproducts

ingredients.name: Ethylhexylglycerin
ingredients.note: Glyceryl-ether deodorant and preservative-boosting humectant, very well tolerated. Sensitization is rare.

ingredients.name: Etidronic Acid
ingredients.note: Phosphonate chelator used to sequester metals and stabilize formulas. Considered safe at the low levels used.

ingredients.name: Euphorbia Cerifera (Candelilla) Wax
ingredients.note: Plant wax from the candelilla shrub used as a thickener, structuring and film-forming agent in sticks/balms. CIR-assessed safe; low sensitization.

ingredients.name: Euphorbia Cerifera (Candelilla) Wax/Euphorbia Cerifera Cera
ingredients.note: Plant-derived wax from candelilla shrub used as a thickener, structurant and gloss/emollient texture agent in balms and sticks. CIR-reviewed and regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: Euterpe Oleracea (Açaí) Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Antioxidant-rich açaí palm fruit oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. No significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Euterpe Oleracea Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Acai berry fruit extract rich in polyphenol antioxidants and vitamins, used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical. Reported non-irritating and non-mutagenic.

ingredients.name: Euterpe Oleracea Sterols
ingredients.note: Plant sterol fraction from acai used as a barrier-supporting emollient/skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign plant-derived ingredient.

sources.label: PubMed – Standardized Euterpe oleracea (açaí) seed extract: potential use in cosmetic products
PubMed – Euterpe oleracea modifies sterol metabolism and attenuates atherosclerosis
PMC – Açaí (Euterpe oleracea Mart.) in Health and Disease: A Critical Review

ingredients.name: Evodia Rutaecarpa Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the fruit of the Evodia shrub used in traditional Chinese medicine, prized for its anti-inflammatory effects on skin. Studies show it can calm redness and irritation when applied topically.

sources.label: PubMed 16423507 – Anti-inflammatory activity in skin by biomimetic of Evodia rutaecarpa extract

ingredients.name: Faex/Yeast Extract
ingredients.note: Yeast-derived extract rich in amino acids, peptides, vitamins and beta-glucan; used as soothing, hydrating, skin-conditioning agent.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Yeast-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
PMC – Evaluation of physicochemical and microbial properties of yeast lees extracts in novel cosmetic products

ingredients.name: Ferric Ammonium Ferrocyanide (CI 77510)
ingredients.note: Inorganic blue iron pigment (Prussian Blue type) with cyanide tightly bound and not released; does not penetrate skin. Approved colorant, used mainly in eye makeup.

ingredients.name: Ferric Ferrocyanide
ingredients.note: Insoluble iron-cyanide complex blue pigment. Approved by FDA (not for lips) and EU; the bound cyanide is non-bioavailable and it is considered safe topically.

ingredients.name: Ferulic Acid
ingredients.note: Plant-derived antioxidant that stabilizes and boosts vitamin C and E formulas. Well tolerated at cosmetic levels with low irritation risk.

ingredients.name: Ficus Carica (Fig) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Fig fruit extract used as a conditioning/antioxidant botanical. Raw fig latex is phototoxic, but the cosmetic fruit extract is generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Ficus Carica Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Fig fruit extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Note: fig leaf/sap (not fruit) is a known phototoxic furanocoumarin source, but fruit extract is benign.

ingredients.name: Folic Acid
ingredients.note: A B-vitamin used in skincare for its antioxidant and UV-protective properties; helps shield skin cells from sun-related damage. Aqueous folic acid can degrade under UV, so it is best used in stable formulations; safe for topical application.

sources.label: Folic acid as UV-protective molecule in cosmetics – PMC

ingredients.name: Fomes Officinalis (Mushroom) Extract
ingredients.note: Bracket-mushroom extract used as an astringent, pore-tightening, film-forming skin-conditioning agent. Standard cosmetic botanical; mild astringency may be drying on very dry skin.

sources.label: PubMed – Characterisation of chemical constituents in aqueous extract of Fomes officinalis
PubMed – Fomitopsis officinalis: a species of arboreal mushroom and its medicinal potential
PMC – Medicinal potential of mycelium and fruiting bodies of Fomitopsis officinalis in lifestyle diseases

ingredients.name: Fragaria Ananassa (Strawberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from strawberry fruit rich in vitamin C, natural AHAs, and antioxidant polyphenols, used in cosmetics to brighten skin and support a more even tone. It is generally safe and well tolerated, though those with known strawberry allergies should use caution.

sources.label: PMC: Fragaria vesca L. Extract: A Promising Cosmetic Ingredient with Antioxidant Properties

ingredients.name: Fragaria Vesca (Strawberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Wild strawberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Fragaria Vesca (Strawberry) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Wild strawberry leaves are packed with ellagitannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that give this extract antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and mild antiseptic properties. Research including cell studies found it supports fibroblast and keratinocyte health, with no significant toxicity concerns.

sources.label: Fragaria vesca L. Extract: A Promising Cosmetic Ingredient with Antioxidant Properties – PMC
Assessment of cytotoxicity and antioxidant properties of berry leaves with potential cosmetic application – PMC

ingredients.name: Fraxinus Excelsior Bark Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the bark of the European ash tree, rich in polyphenols and coumarins with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent and is generally well tolerated.

sources.label: PubMed — Safety evaluation of Fraxinus excelsior seed extract (FraxiPure)

ingredients.name: Fructan
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide of fructose units used as a humectant/film-forming moisturizer (prebiotic). Benign standard sugar polymer.

ingredients.name: Fructooligosaccharides
ingredients.note: Sugar-based humectant with prebiotic effect on skin flora; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Fructose
ingredients.note: Simple sugar used as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Galactoarabinan
ingredients.note: Larch-wood polysaccharide used as a humectant/film-former that reduces TEWL; low irritation, non-photosensitizing, reviewed within CIR polysaccharide-gum assessments.

ingredients.name: Galactomyces Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Yeast-fermented filtrate rich in amino acids, vitamins and organic acids, functioning as a humectant/conditioning agent. CIR preliminary review indicates it is safe as used.

ingredients.name: Gallyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: This is a glucoside form of gallic acid (a natural plant phenolic) designed to improve the stability and skin penetration of its potent antioxidant activity. It helps protect skin from oxidative stress and shows better anti-lipid peroxidation than gallic acid alone.

sources.label: PMC – Transglycosylation of gallic acid and characterization as a functional cosmetic agent

ingredients.name: Ganoderma Lucidum (Mushroom) Stem Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the stem of reishi mushroom, rich in beta-glucans and triterpenoids, used in anti-aging and soothing skincare for its antioxidant and skin-calming properties. Studies confirm no toxicity to skin cells (keratinocytes/fibroblasts) at cosmetic use concentrations.

sources.label: PMC — Assessment of Cosmetic Properties and Safety of Washing Gels with Reishi Extracts
ScienceDirect — Mushroom ethanolic extracts as cosmeceutical ingredients: Safety and ex vivo skin permeation

ingredients.name: Garcinia Cambogia Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Garcinia cambogia fruit extract (source of hydroxycitric acid) used topically as an astringent/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign at cosmetic concentrations; weight-loss claims relate to oral use, not topical.

ingredients.name: Gardenia Florida Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Cape-jasmine fruit extract rich in crocins; skin-conditioning botanical with low irritation/sensitization risk.

sources.label: PMC – Hair Growth-Promoting Effect of Gardenia florida Fruit Extract and Its Molecular Regulation
PMC – Gardenia jasminoides Extract Attenuates UVB-Induced Expressions of Cytokines in Keratinocytes

ingredients.name: Gardenia Taitensis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Tiare (Tahitian gardenia) flower extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Considered non-sensitizing; benign plant extract.

sources.label: PMC – Identification of Antioxidant Metabolites from Gardenia taitensis and Other Polynesian Plants for Skin Care

ingredients.name: Gellan Gum
ingredients.note: Microbial fermentation polysaccharide used as a thickener/gelling and suspending agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Geranium Robertianum Extract
ingredients.note: Herb Robert botanical extract used as an astringent, antioxidant skin conditioner. Benign plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed – Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities of Geranium robertianum L. decoctions
PubMed – Chemical characterization and bioactive properties of extracts of Geranium robertianum L.
PMC – Herb Robert's Gift against Human Diseases: Anticancer and Antimicrobial Activity of Geranium robertianum L.

ingredients.name: Ginseng Extract
ingredients.note: Ginsenoside-rich antioxidant and revitalizing extract; well tolerated with low sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Globularia Alypum Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the leaves of Globularia alypum, a Mediterranean shrub traditionally used in folk medicine, which is added to cosmetics as a skin conditioner with antioxidant properties. It is used at very low concentrations and no significant safety concerns have been documented.

sources.label: PMC – HPLC-DAD Analysis and Biological Properties of Globularia alypum Leaves
PMC – Globularia alypum L. and Related Species: LC-MS Profiles and Biological Potential

ingredients.name: Glucomannan
ingredients.note: Konjac-derived polysaccharide used as a natural thickener/film former; FDA GRAS for food use, benign.

ingredients.name: Gluconic Acid
ingredients.note: Polyhydroxy acid used for mild exfoliation, hydration and metal-ion chelation; gentler than AHAs. Benign at cosmetic use levels (also a chelating/pH-adjusting agent).

sources.label: CIR Final Report: Safety Assessment of Glycolactones (including gluconic acid) as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Gluconolactone
ingredients.note: Polyhydroxy acid that exfoliates gently while acting as a humectant and antioxidant. Generally well tolerated; mild irritation possible at high strength.

ingredients.name: Glucosamine HCl
ingredients.note: Amino-sugar salt used as a pH adjuster and skin-conditioning agent; CIR concluded glucosamine ingredients safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Glucose
ingredients.note: Simple sugar used as a humectant/skin-conditioning agent; benign.

ingredients.name: Glucosyl Ceramide
ingredients.note: A naturally occurring lipid found in skin that helps strengthen the moisture barrier; both topical and oral use have been studied and shown to improve skin hydration and barrier function with a good safety profile.

sources.label: PMC: Efficacy and Safety of Wine Lees-Derived Ceramides and Glucosylceramides for Skin Barrier
PubMed: Orally administered glucosylceramide improves skin barrier function

ingredients.name: Glucosyl Hesperidin
ingredients.note: This is a water-soluble form of hesperidin (a citrus flavonoid) made more stable and bioavailable by attaching a glucose molecule; it is used in cosmetics for its antioxidant and skin-brightening effects. Safety studies show no genotoxicity and high tolerability even in sensitive applications like eye drops.

sources.label: PMC – Safety of glucosyl hesperidin as a Novel food (EU 2015/2283)

ingredients.name: Glucosylrutin
ingredients.note: A water-soluble, enzymatically modified form of the flavonoid rutin; a potent antioxidant that protects skin against UV-induced oxidative damage and inflammation. Widely used in anti-aging and photoprotective cosmetics with a good safety profile.

sources.label: Photoprotective effects of quercetin, hesperidin, and alpha-glucosylrutin – PubMed

ingredients.name: Glutamic Acid
ingredients.note: Naturally occurring amino acid used as a humectant, pH adjuster and skin-conditioning agent. Benign and widely used; CIR reviewed amino acids as safe.

ingredients.name: Glutamylamidoethyl Imidazole
ingredients.note: This synthetic pseudopeptide (derived from histamine and glutamine) acts like a cell-communicating signal, prompting the skin to activate its natural defense mechanisms and supporting cellular energy metabolism. It's used in anti-aging formulas and is considered low-risk.

ingredients.name: Glycereth-25 PCA Isostearate
ingredients.note: PEG-glyceryl PCA isostearate ester used as an emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Glycereth-26
ingredients.note: Glycerin-derived humectant; considered safe in cosmetics with low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Glycerin
ingredients.note: Core moisturizing humectant; skin-identical, extremely safe.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Acrylate/Acrylic Acid Copolymer
ingredients.note: Film-forming/humectant acrylate copolymer used for texture and moisture retention; high-MW polymer, not penetrating.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Arachidonate
ingredients.note: Glycerol monoester of arachidonic acid acting as an emollient/skin-conditioning lipid. Standard fatty-acid ester.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Behenate/Eicosadioate
ingredients.note: Glycerin esters of behenic and eicosanedioic acids used as a waxy emollient, emulsifier and oil-gelling/thickening agent. CAS 215322-01-3.

sources.label: PubMed – Amended final report on safety assessment of glyceryl monoesters including glyceryl behenate as used in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Caprate
ingredients.note: Glyceryl caprate is a glycerol ester of capric acid (a fatty acid from coconut oil) used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning emollient and mild antimicrobial emulsifier. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in cosmetics at current use concentrations.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Glyceryl Monoesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Caprylate
ingredients.note: Glycerin/caprylic-acid monoester used as emollient, emulsifier and mild antimicrobial; CIR-reviewed glyceryl ester considered safe.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Citrate/Lactate/Linoleate/Oleate
ingredients.note: A mixed ester of glycerin with citric acid, lactic acid, linoleic acid, and oleic acid, used as an emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. The CIR Expert Panel found it safe for use in cosmetics and confirmed it is non-mutagenic and non-irritating at typical concentrations.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Glyceryl Monoesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Diisostearate
ingredients.note: A diester of glycerin and isostearic acid used as an emollient and emulsifier in lipsticks and skin-care products, giving them a smooth, non-sticky feel. The CIR Expert Panel has found glyceryl diesters safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Glyceryl Diesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Dioleate
ingredients.note: Diester of glycerin and oleic acid used as an emollient/emulsifier; CIR-assessed safe, not irritating or sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Sugar-glycerin humectant that supports skin hydration channels; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Isostearate
ingredients.note: Glycerin monoester of isostearic acid used as a non-ionic emulsifier and emollient/skin-conditioning agent. Glyceryl fatty-acid esters are CIR-reviewed and safe as used.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Laurate
ingredients.note: Glyceryl laurate is a natural ester of glycerin and lauric acid (found in coconut oil) that acts as an emulsifier and mild preservative booster in cosmetics. The CIR assessed a large group of glyceryl monoesters including this one and found them safe as used; undiluted material can mildly irritate skin but at cosmetic concentrations it is not an irritant.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Glyceryl Monoesters as Used in Cosmetics
Final Report of the Amended Safety Assessment of Glyceryl Laurate and related esters – PubMed

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Linolenate
ingredients.note: Glycerol monoester of linolenic acid used as an emollient and skin-conditioning lipid. Benign fatty-acid derivative.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Oleate
ingredients.note: Glycerin/oleic acid monoester used as emulsifier and emollient. CIR judged safe as used; FDA GRAS, only minimal reversible irritation reported.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Polymethacrylate
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate polymer used as a film former and viscosity/texture agent; large inert polymer, low concern.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Glyceryl Acrylates as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Rosinate
ingredients.note: A resin-derived ester of glycerin and rosin (pine resin) used as a tackifier and binder in makeup and hair products. The CIR Panel has reviewed it and found it safe at concentrations used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Stearate
ingredients.note: Common, gentle emulsifier and emollient; benign and widely used.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Stearate SE
ingredients.note: Self-emulsifying grade of glyceryl stearate containing a small amount of soap (stearate salt). Safe, widely used in creams.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Undecyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Glyceryl-modified silicone (dimethicone derivative) used as an emollient and conditioning/texture agent. Standard low-concern silicone.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Dimethicone, Methicone, and Substituted Methicones as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Safety Assessment of Polyoxyalkylene Siloxane Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Undecylenate
ingredients.note: A glycerol ester of undecylenic acid (a naturally derived fatty acid from castor oil) used as a mild, natural preservative in cosmetics with antifungal and antimicrobial properties. It is considered a gentler alternative to synthetic preservatives and is well-tolerated on skin.

ingredients.name: Glycine Max (Soybean) Polypeptide
ingredients.note: Short chains of amino acids derived from soybean protein hydrolysis, used in cosmetics as a skin and hair conditioning agent. The CIR reviewed soy-derived ingredients and concluded they are safe in cosmetics; people with soy allergies should patch-test first.

sources.label: PubMed — Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
CIR — Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients (PDF)

ingredients.name: Glycine Max (Soybean) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from soybean seeds packed with isoflavones, phenolic acids, and small proteins that help condition skin, support elastin, and provide antioxidant benefits. It has been assessed as safe by the CIR panel, though people with soy allergies should patch-test first.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (2020 FR)
PubMed – Dermatological and cosmeceutical benefits of soybean (2015)

ingredients.name: Glycine Max Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from soybean seeds rich in isoflavones and antioxidants that helps brighten, soothe, and moisturize skin. The CIR Expert Panel has found soy-derived cosmetic ingredients safe in the present practices of use.

sources.label: PubMed 38342963 – Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed 25850195 – Dermatological and cosmeceutical benefits of Glycine max (soybean)

ingredients.name: Glycine Soja (Soybean) Oil
ingredients.note: Refined soybean oil used as an emollient; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Glycine Soja (Soybean) Oil Unsaponifiables
ingredients.note: The phytosterol/vitamin-rich unsaponifiable fraction of soybean oil, used as an emollient and barrier-conditioning agent. Standard, low-hazard lipid ingredient.

sources.label: CIR ingredient page – Glycine soja (soybean) oil unsaponifiables
PubChem compound summary – Glycine Soja (Soybean) Oil Unsaponifiables
CIR Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glycine Soja (Soybean) Sterols
ingredients.note: Plant-derived sterols from soybeans that act as skin-conditioning and barrier-supporting agents; studies show they are safe for cosmetic use and may help reduce inflammation. Avoid if you have a confirmed soy allergy.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics – PubMed
Dermatological and cosmeceutical benefits of Glycine max (soybean) – PubMed

ingredients.name: Glycine Soja Germ Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from soybean (Glycine soja) germ, used in skin care for its moisturizing and conditioning properties. CIR reviewed soy-derived ingredients and found this non-irritating and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic concentrations.

sources.label: EU CosIng – Glycine Soja Germ Extract
CIR Safety Assessment of Soy-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (Final 2020)

ingredients.name: Glycogen
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide humectant; benign and well tolerated in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Glycol Distearate
ingredients.note: Fatty diester used as an opacifier/pearlizing agent and emollient texture modifier in cleansers and shampoos. CIR concluded it is safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Glycolic acid
ingredients.note: Exfoliant; safe but increases sun sensitivity — precautionary reminder to pair with SPF, not a toxicity concern.

ingredients.name: Glycoprotein
ingredients.note: Glycoproteins are proteins with sugar chains attached, derived from plant, animal, or microbial sources; they are used in cosmetics for hydrating, film-forming, and skin-conditioning properties. They are generally considered safe for topical use.

sources.label: PubMed: Cosmetic composition containing glycoproteins for skin care (patent reference)

ingredients.name: Glycoproteins
ingredients.note: Glycoproteins are molecules that combine a protein with sugar chains, naturally found in skin's extracellular matrix where they support hydration and cell communication. In cosmetics they function as conditioning and film-forming agents, and the CIR found skin-derived proteins and peptides safe at current use levels.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Skin and Connective Tissue-Derived Proteins and Peptides as Used in Cosmetics – PubMed

ingredients.name: Glycosphingolipids
ingredients.note: Ceramide-based glycolipids that reinforce the stratum corneum barrier and improve hydration/corneocyte cohesion. Skin-identical, well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Glycosyl Trehalose
ingredients.note: A disaccharide-derived sugar used as a humectant and moisture-binding skin conditioner. Well tolerated with low irritation potential.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Monosaccharides, Disaccharides, and Related Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Glycyrrhetinic Acid
ingredients.note: Licorice-derived active used for soothing and brightening of redness and pigmentation. Well tolerated at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Glycyrrhiza Glabra (Licorice) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Licorice root extract (glabridin/glycyrrhizin) used as a soothing, anti-inflammatory skin-brightening botanical. CIR concluded it is safe as used.

ingredients.name: Glycyrrhiza Uralensis (Licorice) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Licorice root extract used as a brightening, soothing and antioxidant agent; CIR concluded it is safe as used in cosmetics. Mild irritation possible only in very sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Goat Milk Extract
ingredients.note: Goat-milk extract used for skin conditioning/moisturizing; CIR reviews milk-protein ingredients as safe, though milk-allergic individuals should note potential residual milk proteins.

ingredients.name: Gold
ingredients.note: Elemental gold (CI 77480) used as a metallic colorant/pigment for shimmer in cosmetics. Inert and benign as used.

sources.label: PMC – Physicochemical Properties of Gold Nanoparticles for Skin Care Creams
PMC – Dermal Toxicity Influence of Gold Nanomaterials after Embedment in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Gossypium Herbaceum (Cotton) Extract
ingredients.note: Cotton-plant extract used as a soothing, softening skin-conditioning botanical. Benign, though plant extracts may rarely irritate sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed – Final report on safety assessment of Hydrogenated Cottonseed Oil, Cottonseed (Gossypium) Oil and related ingredients
CIR ingredient page – Gossypium herbaceum (cotton) seed oil

ingredients.name: Gossypium Herbaceum (Cotton) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Cotton seed-derived botanical used as an emollient/skin-conditioning agent; CIR deems cottonseed ingredients safe when gossypol and heavy-metal limits are controlled. Refined material is non-irritating/non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Grapeseed (Wine) Extract
ingredients.note: Grape/grapeseed-derived polyphenol-rich extract used for antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits; CIR reviews Vitis vinifera-derived ingredients as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Green Tea Extract
ingredients.note: EGCG-rich antioxidant that is anti-inflammatory and photoprotective; benign and well tolerated in skincare.

ingredients.name: Guanine
ingredients.note: Natural pearlescent pigment traditionally derived from fish scales. Used for shimmer; considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Guar Gum
ingredients.note: Plant-seed polysaccharide thickener. Natural and well tolerated; cationic guar derivatives are common conditioning agents.

ingredients.name: Guar Hydroxypropyltrimonium Chloride
ingredients.note: A cationic derivative of guar gum used as a hair and skin conditioning agent that reduces static and improves combing; the CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in current cosmetic uses and concentrations. It is mild and well-tolerated.

sources.label: CIR – Guar Hydroxypropyltrimonium Chloride ingredient page
CIR Final Report on Safety Assessment of Galactomannans as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Hamamelis Virginiana (Witch Hazel) Extract
ingredients.note: Plant astringent and skin-conditioning extract; FDA recognizes witch hazel as an OTC astringent. CIR noted possible geraniol/linalool oxidation products but found it safe at usual levels.

ingredients.name: Hamamelis Virginiana (Witch Hazel) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Tannin-rich witch hazel leaf extract used as a natural astringent, soothing antioxidant and toner. Generally well tolerated; tannins can be drying/mildly irritating on very sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Harungana Madagascariensis Extract
ingredients.note: African plant extract with retinol-like gene-expression effects used as an anti-aging skin-conditioning botanical; considered safe topically.

ingredients.name: HDI/Trimethylol Hexyllactone Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Crosslinked polyurethane-type powder used for soft focus, texture and oil absorption. Cured polymer, standard low-hazard texture agent.

ingredients.name: Hectorite
ingredients.note: A naturally occurring clay mineral used in cosmetics as a thickener, suspending agent, and emulsion stabilizer. It contains no crystalline silica and is considered safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR – Final Report on the Safety Assessment of Ammonium Hectorites as Used in Cosmetics (2012)

ingredients.name: Hedera Helix (Ivy) Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Ivy leaf/stem extract used as a toning, astringent skin-conditioning botanical. Standard cosmetic plant extract at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Hedychium Coronarium Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the roots of the tropical white ginger plant, studied for its antioxidant, anti-wrinkle, and skin-brightening properties. Lab irritation tests (HET-CAM) show no signs of irritation at cosmetic concentrations.

sources.label: PMC — Promising Anti-Wrinkle Applications of Aromatic Extracts of Hedychium coronarium
PubMed — Anti-wrinkle applications of Hedychium coronarium via antioxidation and collagenase inhibition

ingredients.name: Heilmoor Clay
ingredients.note: A natural mineral-rich peat clay from Austria used in cosmetics to absorb excess oil, draw out impurities, and deliver trace minerals to skin. It is suitable for all skin types, and peat-based cosmetic ingredients generally have a good dermal safety profile.

sources.label: PMC – Peat: A Natural Source for Dermatocosmetics and Dermatotherapeutics

ingredients.name: Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Extract
ingredients.note: Sunflower extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Sunflower-derived flower extract used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Generally regarded as benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seed Oil
