sources.label: Bioactive Azadirachta indica and Melia azedarach leaves extracts - PMC
Effect of cream containing Melia azedarach flowers on skin - PubMed

ingredients.name: Melissa Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Lemon balm extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical with a long history of safe use. Aromatic components may irritate sensitive skin at high levels; non-genotoxic.

ingredients.name: Melissa Officinalis Flower/Leaf/Stem Water
ingredients.note: A floral water (hydrosol) distilled from lemon balm (Melissa officinalis), carrying its mild antioxidant and soothing aromatic compounds in an aqueous base. Genotoxicity studies confirm the aqueous extract is non-genotoxic, and it is listed as a recognized cosmetic ingredient in the EU CosIng database.

sources.label: EC CosIng – MELISSA OFFICINALIS FLOWER/LEAF/STEM WATER
PubMed – Toxicological safety evaluation of an aqueous lemon balm extract

ingredients.name: Melissa Officinalis Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Lemon balm (Lamiaceae) leaf extract used as an antioxidant, soothing skin conditioner. Generally well tolerated as a low-level extract.

ingredients.name: Mentha Aquatica Extract
ingredients.note: Water mint extract used as an antioxidant/soothing skin-conditioning botanical with a light cooling effect. Lower allergen load than mint essential oils; patch test advised for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Mentha Arvensis Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from corn mint leaves used to cool and refresh skin; naturally antimicrobial and soothing. Sensitive skin types may experience mild tingling — patch-test if you react to mint products.

sources.label: Water extract of Mentha arvensis – PubMed
Mentha arvensis leaf extract and radiation protection – PubMed

ingredients.name: Mentha Suaveolens Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Apple mint leaf extract used as an astringent, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract at typical low levels.

sources.label: Anti-Thermal Skin Aging Activity of Apple Mint (Mentha suaveolens) in Human Dermal Fibroblasts - PubMed
Anti-Thermal Skin Aging Activity of Apple Mint (Mentha suaveolens) in Human Dermal Fibroblasts - PMC

ingredients.name: Mentha Viridis (Spearmint) Extract
ingredients.note: Spearmint leaf extract used as a refreshing skin-conditioning botanical; as an extract (vs essential oil) sensitization risk is low. Standard botanical at typical use levels.

sources.label: Medicinal Uses, Phytochemistry, Pharmacology, and Toxicology of Mentha spicata (syn. viridis) - PMC
The traditional uses, phytochemistry and pharmacology of spearmint (Mentha spicata) - PubMed
Safety and tolerability of a dried aqueous spearmint extract - PubMed

ingredients.name: Mentha Viridis (Spearmint) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Spearmint leaf extract contains carvone and rosmarinic acid, giving it antioxidant and mild antimicrobial properties useful in toners and cleansers. It is generally well tolerated, though those with sensitivity to mint-family plants should patch-test before use.

sources.label: Medicinal Uses, Phytochemistry, Pharmacology, and Toxicology of Mentha spicata – PMC

ingredients.name: Menthone Glycerin Acetal
ingredients.note: A menthol-derived molecule that gives a cool, refreshing sensation on skin without the sharp smell of menthol itself; it is used in skincare, oral care, and cooling products. It has FEMA GRAS status and is considered safe at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Menthoxypropanediol
ingredients.note: Synthetic menthol derivative used as a long-lasting cooling/sensate agent in skin, lip, hair and shaving products.

sources.label: Allergic contact dermatitis caused by menthoxypropanediol in a skin care lotion - PubMed
Anti-itching effects of menthoxypropanediol (TRPM8 agonist) cream in atopic dermatitis - PubMed

ingredients.name: Menthyl Lactate
ingredients.note: A long-lasting cooling/sensate agent (menthol-lactic acid ester) milder than menthol; reported non-genotoxic, non-sensitizing and non-irritating at typical 0.1-1% use.

ingredients.name: Methicone
ingredients.note: Methyl hydrogen silicone used as a hydrophobic surface coating for pigments and a skin conditioner; CIR-assessed as safe when non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Methionine
ingredients.note: Essential sulfur-containing amino acid used as an antioxidant and conditioning agent. Considered safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Methyl Dihydroabietate
ingredients.note: A synthetic ester derived from pine resin (rosin) used as an emollient and fixative in cosmetics. It is considered low hazard at cosmetic use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Methyl Diisopropyl Propionamide
ingredients.note: Synthetic menthol-free cooling/sensate agent (WS-23) with long-lasting, low-odor cooling effect. Considered non-irritating and non-sensitizing at use levels.

ingredients.name: Methyl Gluceth-10
ingredients.note: Methyl Gluceth-10 is a glucose-derived humectant that draws moisture into the skin and helps formulas feel smooth and non-tacky. The CIR Expert Panel found no evidence of irritation or sensitization for this ingredient class.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Methyl Gluceth-20
ingredients.note: Glucose-derived PEG humectant used to bind water and soften skin. CIR considers methyl glucose ethers safe as used.

ingredients.name: Methyl Glucose Sesquistearate
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived (methyl glucose + stearic acid) nonionic emulsifier and skin softener. CIR found it safe; non-irritating even at high levels.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
CIR Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters (PDF)

ingredients.name: Methyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: A methyl ester of palmitic acid (a common saturated fatty acid) used as a lightweight emollient and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. It is biodegradable, low in toxicity, and considered safe for topical use.

ingredients.name: Methyl Trimethicone
ingredients.note: Volatile silicone used as a light, fast-spreading emollient and carrier. Standard low-hazard silicone fluid.

ingredients.name: Methylene Bis-Benzotriazolyl Tetramethylbutylphenol
ingredients.note: Broad-spectrum organic particulate filter (Tinosorb M) that works partly by scattering, with a large insoluble molecule and minimal absorption. Photostable and very well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Methylglucamine
ingredients.note: An organic amine base used as a pH-adjusting and solubilizing agent; widely used as a pharmaceutical excipient and generally regarded as nontoxic at use levels.

ingredients.name: Methylglucoside Phosphate
ingredients.note: A phosphate ester of methyl glucose used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent and cellular energy source, believed to support collagen and elastin production as part of anti-aging complexes. It is considered safe and well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Methylheptylglycerin
ingredients.note: A glycerin derivative with antimicrobial and moisturizing properties used as a preservative booster in personal care products. Similar in function to the well-studied ethylhexylglycerin; considered low-hazard at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: Antimicrobial Ingredients as Preservative Boosters in Self-Preserving Cosmetics – PubMed
Deciphering trends in replacing preservatives in cosmetics – PMC

ingredients.name: Methylpropanediol
ingredients.note: Humectant and penetration aid; CIR concluded it is safe at use levels with no notable irritation or sensitization.

ingredients.name: Mica
ingredients.note: Natural silicate mineral used as a pearlescent base and filler. Considered safe topically; ethical-sourcing concerns are separate from skin safety.

ingredients.name: Micrococcus Lysate
ingredients.note: Bacterial lysate (enzymes, peptides) used as a skin-conditioning/DNA-repair-support active; CosIng function skin conditioning.

sources.label: Topical liposomal DNA-repair enzymes (Micrococcus lysate) in polymorphic light eruption - PubMed
Six critical questions for DNA repair enzymes in skincare products - PubMed
UV protective effects of DNA repair enzymes and RNA lotion - PubMed

ingredients.name: Microcrystalline Cellulose
ingredients.note: Purified cellulose used as a thickener, bulking agent and stabilizer. Inert, widely used and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Microcrystalline Wax
ingredients.note: Petroleum-derived structuring wax used in balms and sticks; inert and safe.

ingredients.name: Milk Protein Extract
ingredients.note: Amino-acid and peptide-rich milk-derived protein used as a film-forming, moisturizing and skin-conditioning agent. CIR-reviewed milk proteins are safe; rare reactions in dairy-allergic users.

ingredients.name: Mineral Oil
ingredients.note: Highly refined cosmetic-grade mineral oil is inert and non-comedogenic in most formulas; regulators consider refined grades safe, unlike crude/unrefined oils.

ingredients.name: Mineral Salt
ingredients.note: Generic mineral salt blend used as a viscosity/texture agent and astringent. Benign inorganic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Mineral Salts
ingredients.note: Generic mix of inorganic mineral salts used for conditioning, viscosity or fragrance-masking. Generally benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Molasses
ingredients.note: Molasses is a thick byproduct of sugar refining that is rich in minerals and polyphenols; in cosmetics it is used as a humectant to draw moisture to the skin and as an antioxidant. It is considered safe for topical use at typical cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Momordica Charantia Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Bitter melon fruit extract with antioxidant, antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds used for oily/acne-prone skin. Standard benign cosmetic plant extract at 1-3%.

sources.label: Antioxidative and antimelanogenesis effect of Momordica charantia methanol extract - PubMed
Bitter melon protease as cosmetic enzyme for skin exfoliation and hydration - doi.org
Momordica charantia peel, pulp, seed extract inhibits mouse skin papillomagenesis - PubMed

ingredients.name: Monarda Didyma Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Bee balm (Monarda didyma) leaf extract is rich in geraniol, linalool, and thymol, giving it antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial benefits in skincare. Research including in vitro and in vivo assays confirms its antioxidant and anti-angiogenic properties, with no known significant safety concerns at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: Pharmacognostic Evaluation of Monarda didyma for Cosmeceutical Applications – PMC

ingredients.name: Monascus/Rice Ferment
ingredients.note: This is a fermented rice product made using Monascus purpureus (red yeast) that produces antioxidant pigments and compounds shown to inhibit collagenase and stimulate collagen in skin. It is used in cosmetics for anti-aging and skin-brightening benefits.

sources.label: PMC – Anti-aging potential of Monascus purpureus pigments in skin care

ingredients.name: Moonstone Powder
ingredients.note: This is finely ground feldspar mineral (moonstone) used in cosmetics as a decorative shimmer and light-diffusing agent. As an inert mineral pigment it poses no significant health concerns.

sources.label: EU CosIng – Ingredient: FELDSPAR POWDER (EC Europa)

ingredients.name: Morinda Citrifolia Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Noni flower extract rich in flavonoids and phenolics, used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Morinda Citrifolia Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Noni leaf extract containing flavonoids, vitamins and antioxidants. Used as a skin-conditioning botanical; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Moringa Oleifera Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Emollient oil pressed from moringa seeds, rich in oleic acid. Standard benign cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Moringa Pterygosperma Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Moringa seed extract used as a conditioning and purifying botanical. A standard, low-hazard plant extract.

sources.label: Moringa oleifera Extracts as Bioactive Ingredients That Increase Safety of Body Wash Cosmetics - PMC
Isothiocyanate-rich moringa seed extract reduces skin inflammation - PMC
Isothiocyanate-rich moringa seed extract reduces skin inflammation - PubMed

ingredients.name: Morus Alba (White Mulberry) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from white mulberry leaves used in cosmetics as a skin-brightening active because its key compounds (including mulberroside F) inhibit tyrosinase, the enzyme responsible for producing melanin (skin pigment). Clinical studies show it is effective for reducing dark spots and is generally well tolerated.

sources.label: PubMed – A comparative study of the safety and efficacy of 75% mulberry extract oil versus placebo for melasma
PubMed – Mulberroside F isolated from Morus alba inhibits melanin biosynthesis

ingredients.name: Morus Alba (White Mulberry) Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the roots of the white mulberry tree that inhibits the enzyme tyrosinase, helping to fade dark spots and brighten skin tone; it has been studied in clinical trials and is generally safe for topical use. It is increasingly popular as a natural skin-brightening active.

sources.label: PubMed 22052272 – Safety and efficacy of 75% mulberry extract oil for melasma
PubMed 40806718 – Inhibition of Tyrosinase and Melanogenesis by White Mulberry Fruit Extract

ingredients.name: Morus Alba Bark Extract
ingredients.note: White mulberry bark extract used as an antioxidant, brightening skin conditioner containing arbutin and flavonoids. Plant extract, not isolated drug.

ingredients.name: Morus Alba Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: White mulberry leaf extract rich in polyphenols/flavonoids used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical. Considered benign at cosmetic levels.

sources.label: Identification of Anti-Melanogenesis Constituents from Morus alba L. Leaves - PMC
Characterization of Melanogenesis Inhibitory Constituents of Morus alba Leaves - PMC

ingredients.name: Morus Alba Root Extract
ingredients.note: White mulberry root extract, a natural source of arbutin, used as a tyrosinase-inhibiting skin-brightening botanical. Considered benign at cosmetic levels.

sources.label: Antioxidant and antityrosinase activity of mulberry (Morus alba) root bark - PubMed
Phytochemistry, pharmacology, and clinical trials of Morus alba - PubMed

ingredients.name: Musa Sapientum (Banana) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Banana flower botanical extract used as a skin-conditioning antioxidant. Standard benign plant extract.

sources.label: Extraction and characterization of proteins from banana (Musa sapientum) flower - PMC
Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities of extracts from Musa sapientum peel - PubMed
Functional Properties of Banana Starch (Musa spp.) and Its Utilization in Cosmetics - PMC

ingredients.name: Musa Sapientum (Banana) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from banana fruit used in cosmetics for its softening and moisturizing properties. It is rich in vitamins and antioxidants; topical use at cosmetic concentrations is considered safe and is generally non-irritating.

sources.label: PubMed — Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities of extracts from Musa sapientum peel

ingredients.name: Musa Sapientum (Banana) Pulp Extract
ingredients.note: Banana fruit pulp extract used as a humectant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign food-derived extract.

ingredients.name: Musa Sapientum (Banana) Water
ingredients.note: Aqueous extract or water derived from banana plants, containing vitamins, antioxidants, and natural sugars that help hydrate and soothe skin. It is very gentle and widely considered safe.

sources.label: PubMed – Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities of Musa sapientum peel extracts (2013)

ingredients.name: Myosotis Sylvatica Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Forget-me-not (Boraginaceae) aerial-part extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Standard, low-concern cosmetic plant extract.

ingredients.name: Myristic Acid
ingredients.note: Fatty acid used in cleansers and as an emulsifier; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Myristyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: A fatty alcohol derived from coconut or palm oil that thickens products and softens skin. The CIR Expert Panel has confirmed it is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Myristyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived nonionic alkyl glucoside used as a mild surfactant and emulsifier. Considered safe and gentle.

ingredients.name: Myristyl Myristate
ingredients.note: Wax-like fatty ester (myristyl alcohol + myristic acid) used as emollient, opacifier and thickener. CIR-reviewed safe as used.

sources.label: Myristyl myristate - CIR Cosmetic Ingredient Review
Amended Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: N-Hydroxysuccinimide
ingredients.note: A synthetic compound used in under-eye products to break down iron pigments from blood that cause dark circles; a 6-week clinical study found it reduced under-eye hyperpigmentation by ~48% with no adverse effects reported.

sources.label: PMC: Clinical Efficacy of a Novel Topical Formulation on Periorbital Dark Circles
PubMed: Clinical Efficacy of a Novel Topical Formulation on Periorbital Dark Circles

ingredients.name: Nelumbium Speciosum Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from sacred lotus flowers rich in carbohydrates, vitamin C, and phenolic compounds; used in skincare for hydration, antioxidant protection, and mild skin-brightening. Well-tolerated and generally safe for all skin types.

sources.label: Skin-whitening effect of Nelumbo nucifera callus extract – PubMed
Nelumbo nucifera extracts as whitening and anti-wrinkle agent – ResearchGate

ingredients.name: Nelumbo Nucifera (Sacred Lotus) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Sacred lotus flower extract used as a conditioning/antioxidant botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Nelumbo Nucifera Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Lotus leaf extract used as a skin-conditioning agent with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.

ingredients.name: Nelumbo Nucifera Root Extract
ingredients.note: Lotus root extract used as soothing, brightening (tyrosinase-inhibiting flavonoids) and antioxidant skin-conditioning agent.

Skin-Whitening Effect of a Callus Extract of Nelumbo nucifera - PubMed

ingredients.name: Nelumbo Nucifera Root Water
ingredients.note: Lotus root water (aqueous fraction). Mild hydrating, antioxidant botanical used in soothing toners.

Skin-Whitening Effect of a Callus Extract of Nelumbo nucifera - PubMed

ingredients.name: Neopentyl Glycol Dicaprate
ingredients.note: Synthetic ester (neopentyl glycol with capric acid) used as a light emollient/skin-conditioning agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Neopentyl Glycol Diethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: Synthetic glycol diester used as a light emollient and spreadability/texture enhancer. Standard makeup and skincare ingredient.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Monoalkylglycol Dialkyl Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: Neopentyl Glycol Diheptanoate
ingredients.note: Light synthetic ester emollient/silicone alternative giving silky slip and spread. EWG low-hazard, low allergenic potential.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Monoalkylglycol Dialkyl Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Reaction strategies for enzymatic synthesis of neopentyl glycol diheptanoate - PubMed

ingredients.name: Neoruscogenin
ingredients.note: Steroidal sapogenin from Ruscus aculeatus (butcher's broom) used as a skin-conditioning and anti-inflammatory cosmetic active. No notable sensitization concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Niacinamide
ingredients.note: Well-tolerated multitasker: supports barrier, evens tone, helps acne. The safe brightening route.

ingredients.name: Nylon-12
ingredients.note: Synthetic polymer powder that adds soft-focus blur and silkiness; inert and generally regarded as safe topically.

ingredients.name: Nylon-6
ingredients.note: Synthetic polyamide powder used as bulking/opacifying agent; CIR concluded safe with little skin-penetration potential.

ingredients.name: Nylon-66
ingredients.note: Polyamide polymer used as a bulking and opacifying agent. CIR concluded nylon polymers are safe with negligible skin penetration.

ingredients.name: Nymphaea Alba Flower Extract
ingredients.note: White water-lily flower extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Nymphaea Alba Root Extract
ingredients.note: Derived from the roots of the white water lily, this extract contains flavonoids, gallic acid, and tannins that hydrate, soothe, and mildly tone the skin. It's a gentle botanical with no significant safety concerns at concentrations used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CosIng: NYMPHAEA ALBA ROOT EXTRACT (EC Europa, entry 35570)

ingredients.name: Nymphaea Caerulea Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Blue lotus flower extract used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical (flavonoids, nuciferine). Limited safety data but no major topical concern at cosmetic dilution.

ingredients.name: Nymphaea Odorata Root Extract
ingredients.note: White water lily root extract used as a soothing, conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: o-Cymen-5-ol
ingredients.note: o-Cymen-5-ol is a plant-derived phenol used in cosmetics as a broad-spectrum antimicrobial preservative; it is considered a safer alternative to triclosan. The CIR panel considers it safe at concentrations up to 0.5% in cosmetic products.

sources.label: PMC: Mouthwash with o-cymen-5-ol targets pathogens without impairing oral microbiome

ingredients.name: Oat Amino Acids
ingredients.note: Oat amino acids are the individual building-block amino acids derived from oat protein; they help condition skin and hair while supporting the skin's natural moisture factor. Both amino acids and oat-derived ingredients have well-established safety records as cosmetic conditioning agents.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of α-Amino Acids as Used in Cosmetics – PubMed

ingredients.name: Ocimum Sanctum Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Holy basil (tulsi) leaf extract used as an antioxidant, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Ocimum Tenuiflorum Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the holy basil (tulsi) plant used in cosmetics for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Research confirms it can soothe reactive skin and combat acne-causing bacteria; it is generally safe for topical use, with minimal risk of irritation.

sources.label: PMC — The Clinical Efficacy and Safety of Tulsi in Humans: A Systematic Review
PMC — Fibroblast and keratinocyte gene expression following exposure to Ocimum tenuiflorum extract

ingredients.name: Octanediol
ingredients.note: 1,2-octanediol (caprylyl glycol) used as a humectant, emollient and preservative booster; CIR concluded safe, low irritation/sensitization.

ingredients.name: Octisalate
ingredients.note: Weak UVB filter mainly used as a solvent and avobenzone stabilizer. Large, lipophilic salicylate with low penetration and a favorable safety record; minimal endocrine concern.

ingredients.name: Octyldodecanol
ingredients.note: Branched fatty alcohol used as emollient/solvent; CIR judged it safe with low irritation/sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Octyldodeceth-16
ingredients.note: PEG ether of octyldodecanol used as a nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. CIR found octyldodeceth-16 safe as used (about 0.1-2%).

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Myristate
ingredients.note: Ester of octyldodecanol and myristic acid used as a low-irritation emollient; CIR concluded myristate esters safe.

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Neopentanoate
ingredients.note: Light viscous ester emollient providing glide, reduced tack and pigment dispersion. EWG low-hazard (1), no significant irritation or sensitization concerns.

sources.label: Amended Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Stearoyl Stearate
ingredients.note: Heavy emollient ester used for gloss and conditioning in lip and color cosmetics. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Oenothera Biennis (Evening Primrose) Extract
ingredients.note: Evening primrose extract rich in linoleic and gamma-linolenic acids, used as a soothing, moisturizing, anti-inflammatory skin-conditioning agent. Standard well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Oenothera Biennis (Evening Primrose) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Evening primrose flower botanical extract used for skin conditioning and soothing. Benign plant extract.

An Oenothera biennis Cell Cultures Extract Improves Cell Mechanical Properties - PMC
Effect of Evening Primrose Sprout Extract on Skin Function in UVB-irradiated Mice - PMC

ingredients.name: Oenothera Biennis (Evening Primrose) Oil
ingredients.note: Evening primrose seed oil rich in GLA/linoleic acid used as an emollient; CIR found it safe with no dermal irritation, sensitization, or phototoxicity.

ingredients.name: Oenothera Biennis (Evening Primrose) Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the roots of the evening primrose plant, containing polyphenols and flavonoids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits for skin. It is considered safe for topical cosmetic use; note that most safety data available is for the seed oil form.

sources.label: PMC — Phytochemical and Biological Screening of Oenothera biennis L. Hydroalcoholic Extract

ingredients.name: Olea Europaea (Olive) Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Olive fruit oil used as an occlusive, fatty-acid-rich emollient. Standard well-tolerated botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Olea Europaea (Olive) Oil Unsaponifiables
ingredients.note: Sterol/squalene-rich non-saponifiable fraction of olive oil used as an emollient and antioxidant. CIR reviewed olive-derived ingredients as safe.

ingredients.name: Olea Europaea Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Classic olive oil used as an emollient to soften and hydrate skin; it is rich in oleic acid and antioxidants. It has been assessed as safe by the CIR panel, though people with extremely dry skin may need richer oils to see benefit.

sources.label: CIR – Olea europaea (olive) fruit oil ingredient page
CIR – Safety Assessment of Olea europaea (Olive)-Derived Ingredients

ingredients.name: Olea Europaea Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from olive leaves rich in oleuropein and other polyphenols with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and photoprotective benefits. The CIR assessment found olive-derived ingredients safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Olea europaea (Olive)-Derived Ingredients (PDF)
PubMed 32333467 – Efficacy of olive leaf extract-containing cream for facial rejuvenation

ingredients.name: Oleic Acid
ingredients.note: Monounsaturated fatty acid emollient; safe, though can feel heavy and may disrupt barrier on very sensitive skin at high levels.

ingredients.name: Oleic/Linoleic/Linolenic Polyglycerides
ingredients.note: A mixture of polyglyceryl esters of three key plant-derived fatty acids (oleic, linoleic, and linolenic acid) that acts as an emollient and emulsifier, helping skin feel soft while also stabilizing formulas. It is derived from vegetable oils and considered safe and gentle.

ingredients.name: Oleyl Erucate
ingredients.note: Ester of oleyl alcohol and erucic acid used as a jojoba-like occlusive emollient; standard skin-conditioning ester.

sources.label: Amended Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: Oligopeptide-2
ingredients.note: Synthetic short-chain peptide used as a skin-conditioning/anti-aging signaling active; standard cosmetic peptide.

Protective effects of skin permeable epidermal and fibroblast growth factor against UV-induced skin damage - PubMed

ingredients.name: Oligopeptide-3
ingredients.note: Synthetic short peptide used as a skin-conditioning anti-aging/regenerating active. Good safety profile; allergic reactions extremely rare.

ingredients.name: Olus (Vegetable Oil)
ingredients.note: Generic blend of vegetable triglycerides used as an emollient/moisturizing base; plant triglycerides regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: Olus Oil/Vegetable Oil
ingredients.note: Generic refined vegetable oil blend used as an emollient and skin-conditioning carrier. Benign cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Ophiopogon Japonicus Root Extract
ingredients.note: Root extract rich in saponins and polysaccharides used as a soothing, hydrating skin-conditioning botanical. Generally well tolerated.

Ophiopogon japonicus: phytochemical, ethnomedicinal and pharmacological review - PubMed

ingredients.name: Opuntia Coccinellifera Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from a species of prickly pear cactus fruit, containing flavonoids and antioxidants that moisturize skin and may inhibit melanin production for a mild brightening effect. Safe for topical use.

ingredients.name: Opuntia Ficus-Indica (Prickly Pear) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from prickly pear cactus flowers rich in isorhamnetin and other flavonoids; it is used in cosmetics to regulate sebum, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant protection. Clinical studies have confirmed good skin tolerability.

ingredients.name: Opuntia Ficus-Indica Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the prickly pear cactus rich in antioxidants, betalains, and polysaccharides. Studies show it boosts skin hydration for several hours after application and may help with anti-aging; it is very safe for topical use.

sources.label: PubMed – Cosmetic nanoemulsions containing Opuntia ficus-indica extract as moisturizing agent
PubMed – Anti-aging effects of Opuntia ficus-indica extracts in a D-galactose-induced skin aging model

ingredients.name: Opuntia Ficus-Indica Flower Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from the flowers of the prickly pear cactus, rich in antioxidant polyphenols and used in cosmetics for anti-aging and moisturizing benefits. It has a well-established safety profile based on its long history of food and traditional cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC – Opuntia ficus-indica: A Multi-Benefit Potential to Be Exploited
PubMed – Characterization and anti-aging effects of Opuntia ficus-indica extracts

ingredients.name: Opuntia Ficus-Indica Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from prickly pear cactus fruit, loaded with betalain pigments, vitamin C, and antioxidants that soothe skin and protect against oxidative damage. It is well-tolerated and widely used in clean and natural beauty products.

sources.label: PMC – Opuntia ficus-indica: a multi-benefit potential to be exploited
PMC – Phytochemical profiling, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities of prickly pear fruit and pulp extracts

ingredients.name: Opuntia Ficus-Indica Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Seed oil of prickly pear cactus high in linoleic acid and tocopherols, used as a conditioning emollient. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Opuntia Ficus-Indica Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Prickly pear cactus stem extract used as a humectant, soothing and antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Opuntia Tuna Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the fruit of the prickly pear cactus rich in antioxidants, betalains, and polyphenols that moisturizes and soothes skin; generally well-tolerated, with some research indicating skin-brightening potential.

sources.label: PMC: Mechanism and inhibitory effects of cactus (Opuntia) extract on melanocytes for whitening cosmetics

ingredients.name: Orbignya Oleifera (Babassu) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Lauric-rich babassu seed oil used as a lightweight emollient; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Orbignya Oleifera Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Babassu palm seed oil used as a lightweight emollient and skin/hair conditioner. Standard benign plant-derived oil.

Ingredient: ORBIGNYA OLEIFERA SEED OIL - EU CosIng

ingredients.name: Orchid Extract
ingredients.note: Orchid extract is a luxury botanical ingredient rich in flavonoids and polysaccharides that help protect skin from environmental stress and improve radiance. Studies show efficacy comparable to vitamin C derivatives for skin brightening, and it has a good safety profile with standard botanical cautions for plant-sensitive individuals.

sources.label: Whitening efficacy of orchid extracts on Japanese female skin with melasma – PubMed
Comprehensive Review of Cosmeceutical Benefits of Vanda Species (Orchidaceae) – PubMed

ingredients.name: Orchis Mascula Extract
ingredients.note: An extract derived from the tubers of Orchis mascula (early purple orchid), traditionally used in herbal preparations and now found in some luxury skincare products for skin-conditioning effects. It is a recognized cosmetic ingredient in the EU CosIng database; formal cosmetic safety data is limited but no significant hazard has been identified.

sources.label: EC CosIng – Ingredient: ORCHIS MASCULA EXTRACT

ingredients.name: Orchis Mascula Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Orchid (salep) flower extract used as a botanical skin-conditioning/soothing agent; standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Orobanche Rapum Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the parasitic plant Orobanche rapum (a type of broomrape), rich in phenylethanoid glycosides like acteoside and crenatoside, used in cosmetics for anti-inflammatory and photoprotective skin benefits. Research on related Orobanche species supports its use as a soothing active; it is considered safe as used.

sources.label: PubMed 29446095 – Orobanche cernua extract attenuates UVB-mediated photoaging in human dermal fibroblasts

ingredients.name: Orthosiphon Stamineus Extract
ingredients.note: Java tea leaf extract used as a sebum-reducing/astringent skin-conditioning botanical (acts on 5-alpha-reductase). Considered safe and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Bran
ingredients.note: Rice bran used as an exfoliating/skin-conditioning botanical rich in antioxidants and fatty acids. Benign plant-derived ingredient.

sources.label: Oryza sativa (rice) bran oil - CIR Cosmetic Ingredient Review
Ingredient: ORYZA SATIVA BRAN EXTRACT - EU CosIng

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Bran Extract
ingredients.note: Rice bran-derived botanical extract used for skin conditioning and antioxidant effect. Benign.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Bran Oil
ingredients.note: Oil expressed from rice bran rich in oleic/linoleic acids, vitamin E and gamma-oryzanol; used as an emollient antioxidant. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Bran Wax
ingredients.note: Plant-derived wax from rice bran used as a thickener and emollient/structuring agent. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Extract
ingredients.note: Rice plant extract used as a conditioning/soothing botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Germ Extract
ingredients.note: Extracted from rice germs, this ingredient delivers ferulic acid, vitamin E, and amino acids that protect skin from oxidative stress and support a brighter, smoother complexion. It's gentle and widely studied in Asian skincare science.

sources.label: PMC – Antioxidant, Anti-Inflammation, and Melanogenesis Inhibition of Sang 5 CMU Rice for Cosmetic Applications

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Germ Oil
ingredients.note: Expressed rice germ oil rich in gamma-oryzanol, vitamin E and phytosterols; emollient and moisturizing. CIR-assessed.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Lees Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the rice solids (lees) left over from sake (rice wine) production, rich in amino acids, vitamins, and antioxidants that brighten and condition skin. It is generally considered safe and well-tolerated in cosmetics.

sources.label: PMC — Antioxidant, Anti-Inflammation, and Melanogenesis Inhibition of Rice (Oryza sativa) Byproduct for Cosmetic Applications
Sage Journals — Amended Final Report on the Safety Assessment of Oryza sativa-Derived Ingredients

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Powder
ingredients.note: Powder of milled rice used as an absorbent, bulking and texturizing agent. Benign plant-derived cosmetic ingredient with no notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Seed Protein
ingredients.note: Rice-derived protein used as a film-forming, conditioning and moisture-binding agent for skin and hair. Benign protein-based cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Seed Protein Extract
ingredients.note: Plant-derived protein used to condition skin and hair (film-forming, moisturizing). Rice is a low-allergenicity source and CIR reviews plant proteins as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Seed Water
ingredients.note: The water extract of rice seeds, traditionally used in East Asian beauty routines to brighten and smooth skin. It contains phenolics and vitamins with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and mild skin-whitening properties; it is very gentle and broadly safe.

sources.label: PMC – Antioxidant, anti-inflammation, and melanogenesis inhibition of rice byproduct for cosmetic applications

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Starch
ingredients.note: Finely milled starch from rice grains used as a soft powder in cosmetics to absorb oil, give a smooth matte finish, and improve texture. It is a natural, gentle ingredient that is safe for all skin types including sensitive skin.

sources.label: PMC – Antioxidant, Anti-Inflammation, and Melanogenesis Inhibition of Rice (Oryza sativa) for Cosmetic Applications

ingredients.name: Oxidized Glutathione
ingredients.note: Oxidized glutathione (GSSG) is the spent, oxidized form of glutathione — a natural cellular antioxidant — and is used topically for skin brightening and anti-aging. In a 2% lotion form it has been shown to reduce the melanin index, with a good topical safety profile.

sources.label: PMC – Systematic review of the efficacy and safety of topical glutathione in dermatology
PMC – Exploring the safety and efficacy of glutathione supplementation for skin lightening: a narrative review

ingredients.name: Oxygen
ingredients.note: Elemental gas used as a propellant and skin-conditioning agent in a small fraction of cosmetics. Inert and benign at cosmetic exposures.

ingredients.name: Ozokerite
ingredients.note: Mineral wax used to gel oils and harden sticks. Refined cosmetic grade is inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: P-Anisic Acid
ingredients.note: This is a naturally derived antimicrobial ingredient (found in anise plants) increasingly used as a gentler alternative to traditional synthetic preservatives in cosmetics. It also has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and at typical use concentrations it is considered safe.

sources.label: PMC – Bioactive oligoesters as potential delivery systems of p-Anisic Acid for cosmetics

ingredients.name: Paeonia Albiflora Flower Extract
ingredients.note: White peony flower extract used as a soothing, anti-irritant, brightening antioxidant botanical with a long traditional-medicine history.

ingredients.name: Paeonia Albiflora Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the roots of white peony, traditionally used in East Asian medicine. It contains paeoniflorin, which has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and skin-brightening effects; a dedicated cosmetic safety assessment found it safe via the toxicological concern threshold approach.

sources.label: PubMed — Safety assessment of Paeonia lactiflora root extract for a cosmetic ingredient (TTC approach)
PubMed — Skin-depigmenting potential of Paeonia lactiflora root extract and paeoniflorin

ingredients.name: Paeonia Officinalis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the flowers of Paeonia officinalis (common peony) that contains paeoniflorin, albiflorin, rutin, and other bioactive compounds used in cosmetics for antioxidant, skin-brightening, and anti-inflammatory effects. Research on related peony species shows a favorable safety profile with UV-protective and skin barrier-supportive benefits.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety assessment of Paeonia lactiflora root extract for cosmetic ingredient using TTC approach
PMC – Skin health properties of Paeonia lactiflora flower extracts

ingredients.name: Paeonia Suffruticosa Root Extract
ingredients.note: Tree peony root extract used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical (paeoniflorin-rich). Standard plant extract with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Palmaria Palmata Extract
ingredients.note: Red algae (dulse) extract rich in minerals and antioxidants used as a hydrating, soothing skin-conditioning agent. Benign marine botanical.

sources.label: Extracts from dulse (Palmaria palmata) are effective antioxidants - PubMed
Exploring the Potential of Icelandic Seaweeds Extracts for Cosmetic Applications - PMC
Nutraceutical Extract from Dulse (Palmaria palmata) Inhibits Primary Human Neutrophil Activation - PMC

ingredients.name: Palmitic Acid
ingredients.note: Naturally occurring fatty acid used as emollient and emulsifier; safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Dipeptide-7
ingredients.note: Lipidated signal peptide (Pal-KT) marketed to stimulate collagen/laminin and support barrier and wound-healing pathways. Used at trace levels; regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Heptapeptide-27
ingredients.note: Palmitoylated synthetic signal peptide used at trace levels as a skin-conditioning/anti-aging active, within the CIR-reviewed palmitoyl oligopeptide class.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Hexapeptide-12
ingredients.note: Synthetic palmitoylated matrikine peptide used as a skin-conditioning anti-aging active. CIR found it safe at low use levels with no sensitization or genotoxicity.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Octapeptide-24
ingredients.note: Palmitoylated synthetic peptide used as a trace-level skin-conditioning/anti-aging active, within the CIR-reviewed palmitoyl oligopeptide class.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Oligopeptide-78
ingredients.note: Palmitoylated synthetic peptide used as a low-concentration skin-conditioning/anti-aging active, within the CIR-reviewed palmitoyl oligopeptide class.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl pentapeptide-4
ingredients.note: Cosmetic signal peptide; safe.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-10
ingredients.note: Palmitoylated synthetic peptide used as a skin-conditioning/anti-aging signal peptide at very low concentrations. CIR found palmitoyl oligopeptides safe as used.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl tetrapeptide-7
ingredients.note: Cosmetic peptide; safe.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl tripeptide-1
ingredients.note: Cosmetic peptide; safe.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-38
ingredients.note: Synthetic signal peptide (Matrixyl Synthe'6) marketed to support collagen/matrix synthesis; used at low levels and considered safe.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Palmitoyl Oligopeptides as Used in Cosmetics
Usage of Synthetic Peptides in Cosmetics for Sensitive Skin - PMC

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-5
ingredients.note: Synthetic collagen-stimulating lipopeptide used as an anti-aging active; CIR-reviewed peptide class found safe, non-irritating and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-8
ingredients.note: Palmitoylated tripeptide mimicking alpha-MSH used as a soothing/anti-inflammatory active for sensitive skin; good reported topical safety.

ingredients.name: Panax Ginseng Berry Extract
ingredients.note: An antioxidant-rich extract from ginseng berries that contains ginsenosides and syringaresinol, known to help reduce UV-induced aging, brighten skin, and support collagen; clinical trials show good tolerability with no adverse reactions.

sources.label: PubMed: Efficacy and Safety of Enzyme-Modified Panax ginseng for Anti-Wrinkle Therapy
ScienceDirect: Syringaresinol from Panax ginseng berry attenuates oxidative stress-induced skin aging
PMC: Ginseng and ginseng byproducts for skincare and skin health

ingredients.name: Panax Ginseng Callus Culture Extract
ingredients.note: Grown from ginseng plant cells in lab conditions, this extract preserves the antioxidant ginsenosides and polysaccharides found in ginseng root without the need to harvest wild plants. It conditions skin and provides brightening and anti-aging support.

sources.label: PMC – Potential Cosmetic Applications of Combined Extract of Panax ginseng and Asian Plants

ingredients.name: Panax Ginseng Root Water
ingredients.note: The aqueous extract of ginseng root, used in cosmetics to deliver antioxidant ginsenosides that help fight signs of aging and improve skin texture. A controlled clinical study found ginseng extract significantly reduced photo-damage scores and improved skin hydration.

sources.label: PubMed – Efficacy and Safety of Enzyme-Modified Panax ginseng for Anti-Wrinkle Therapy
PMC – Ginseng and ginseng byproducts for skincare and skin health

ingredients.name: Panax Ginseng Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Oil cold-pressed from ginseng seeds and rich in unsaturated fatty acids, phytosterols, and tocopherols; used in skincare for its moisturizing, antioxidant, and anti-aging properties. Clinical studies have shown it is well-tolerated with no adverse reactions reported.

sources.label: PubMed – Red ginseng oil: a comprehensive review of composition, health benefits, and safety
PMC – Ginseng and ginseng byproducts for skincare and skin health

ingredients.name: Panthenol
ingredients.note: Soothing humectant; safe.

ingredients.name: Pantolactone
ingredients.note: Hygroscopic cyclic ester (provitamin B5 precursor) used as humectant and skin-conditioning agent. Very low irritation/sensitization risk.

sources.label: Allergic contact dermatitis from pantolactone and dexpanthenol in wound healing creams - PMC
Allergic contact dermatitis from pantolactone and dexpanthenol - PubMed

ingredients.name: Pantothenic Acid
ingredients.note: Vitamin B5 used as a humectant, barrier-support and soothing skin-conditioning agent (active form of panthenol). Well established and safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Papaver Rhoeas Extract
ingredients.note: Corn (field) poppy extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning/firming botanical; distinct from opium poppy (P. somniferum), so no narcotic content. Well tolerated at low doses.

sources.label: Flower Extracts as Multifunctional Dyes in the Cosmetics Industry - PMC
Antioxidant and Cytoprotective Properties of Plant Extract from Dry Flowers for Cosmetic Products - PMC
CosIng Entry: Papaver Rhoeas - EU CosIng

ingredients.name: Papaver Somniferum Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A lightweight plant oil cold-pressed from poppy seeds that is rich in linoleic and oleic acids; it absorbs readily into skin and is used as a nourishing emollient in hair and skin products. The oil does not contain opiates (those are in the latex, not the seed), so it is safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC9562219 – Nutritional and Chemical Characterization of Poppy Seeds and Cold-Pressed Oil

ingredients.name: Paraffin
ingredients.note: Refined wax used for texture and occlusion; benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Passiflora Edulis (Passion Fruit) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Passion fruit extract used for antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits; standard low-risk botanical.

ingredients.name: Passiflora Edulis (Passionflower) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Linoleic-rich passionfruit seed oil used as an emollient; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Passiflora Edulis Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Passionfruit (maracuja) seed oil high in linoleic acid, used as a lightweight emollient and antioxidant skin-conditioning oil. Benign.

ingredients.name: Paullinia Cupana Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Guarana seed extract, naturally caffeine-rich, used as an antioxidant and astringent in firming/eye products. Typically used at 0.5-3%.

ingredients.name: PCA
ingredients.note: Natural moisturizing factor amino acid used as a humectant. CIR concludes it is safe; should not be used where N-nitroso compounds can form.

ingredients.name: PCA Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone modified with pyrrolidone carboxylic acid; a skin/hair conditioning agent giving a silky feel. Well tolerated, non-comedogenic.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of PCA and Its Salts as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Dimethicone, Methicone, and Substituted-Methicone Polymers

ingredients.name: Pearl Powder
ingredients.note: Finely ground pearl, primarily calcium carbonate with amino acids; used as a bulking/optical agent. CIR found calcium carbonate safe as used.

ingredients.name: PEG-10 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: PEG-modified silicone used as an emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. CIR considers PEG dimethicones safe as used.

ingredients.name: PEG-10 Isostearate
ingredients.note: A PEG-based emulsifier derived from isostearic acid that helps stabilize creams and lotions; generally well-tolerated, though manufacturers should control for the trace contaminant 1,4-dioxane, which is a byproduct of ethoxylation.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of PEG Diesters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: PEG-12 Carnauba
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated carnauba wax used as an emulsifier/emollient and texture agent. Standard low-concern wax derivative.

sources.label: CIR Amended Safety Assessment of PEGylated Oils as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-120 Methyl Glucose Dioleate
ingredients.note: A mild, water-soluble emulsifier and thickener derived from glucose and oleic acid (a plant-based fatty acid), commonly used to stabilize cleansers and make them feel luxurious. The CIR Panel concluded this class of ingredients is safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: PEG-150 Distearate
ingredients.note: A polyethylene glycol ester of stearic acid used as a thickener and emulsifier in shampoos and lotions to give products a rich, creamy consistency. The CIR Expert Panel has concluded that PEG stearates, including PEG-150 distearate, are safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: PEG-150 Pentaerythrityl Tetrastearate
ingredients.note: PEGylated pentaerythritol stearate ester used as a high-efficiency thickener, emulsifier and counter-irritant in surfactant systems. Large molecule, unlikely to penetrate skin.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of PEG-150 Pentaerythrityl Tetrastearate as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Safety Assessment of PEG-150 Pentaerythrityl Tetrastearate - PubMed

ingredients.name: PEG-16 Soy Sterol
ingredients.note: This is a polyethylene glycol ether of soy-derived plant sterols used as an emulsifier and conditioning agent in cosmetics. CIR concluded PEG-5, -10, -16, -25, -30, and -40 Soy Sterols are safe as used, but advises avoiding use on damaged skin.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of PEG Soy Sterols as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-20 Glyceryl Triisostearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated glyceryl triisostearate ester used as an emulsifier and emollient. Standard benign emulsifier.

ingredients.name: PEG-20 Methyl Glucose Sesquistearate
ingredients.note: This is a PEG-based emulsifier derived from glucose (sugar) and stearic acid, used in creams and lotions to keep water and oil from separating. The Cosmetic Ingredient Review Expert Panel has assessed methyl glucose polyethers and esters (including this type) and found them safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters

ingredients.name: PEG-200 Hydrogenated Glyceryl Palmate
ingredients.note: A PEGylated palm-derived glyceride used as an emulsifier and co-emulsifier to help stabilize creams and lotions; the large PEG-200 chain makes it very hydrophilic and gentle. It is listed in the EU CosIng database and covered under CIR assessments of PEGylated alkyl glycerides, which concluded they are safe in cosmetics.

sources.label: EC CosIng – PEG-200 HYDROGENATED GLYCERYL PALMATE
CIR – Safety Assessment of PEGylated Alkyl Glycerides as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)

ingredients.name: PEG-240/HDI Copolymer Bis-Decyltetradeceth-20 Ether
ingredients.note: Polyurethane-type associative rheology modifier/thickener and emulsion stabilizer; high-molecular-weight polymer with low skin penetration and standard cosmetic safety profile.

ingredients.name: PEG-30 Dipolyhydroxystearate
ingredients.note: PEG-ester water-in-oil emulsifier; PEG safety assessment identified no safety concern and it is minimally skin-absorbed.

ingredients.name: PEG-30 Stearate
ingredients.note: A PEG ester of stearic acid used as an emulsifier to blend oil and water phases smoothly in creams and lotions; the CIR Expert Panel has found the group of PEG stearates safe as cosmetic ingredients at current use levels. It is non-irritating and non-sensitizing in standard formulations.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of PEG Stearates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-32
ingredients.note: A medium-weight polyethylene glycol polymer that helps attract moisture to skin and improves the texture and spreadability of products. It is considered safe in cosmetics; the main concern with some PEGs is contamination with trace impurities during manufacturing, which is well-controlled in cosmetic-grade materials.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of PEG Stearates as Used in Cosmetics (includes PEG-32 data)

ingredients.name: PEG-4 Laurate
ingredients.note: PEG-4 Laurate is a mild emulsifier made by reacting lauric acid (from coconut/palm) with ethylene oxide; it helps oil and water mix together smoothly in creams and lotions. The CIR found it safe up to 25% and non-irritating in standard cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of PEG Diesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-5 Ethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: A PEG ester of 2-ethylhexanoic acid used as a lightweight skin-conditioning emollient and solubilizer in cosmetic formulations. The CIR assessed alkyl ethylhexanoates and found them safe for cosmetic use when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Alkyl Ethylhexanoates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-6 Caprylic/Capric Glycerides
ingredients.note: This PEG-derived ingredient acts as both an emulsifier and mild skin-conditioning agent, helping products blend smoothly and absorb well. It is generally considered non-irritating and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use levels, though like all PEG ingredients it should be manufactured to avoid trace 1,4-dioxane.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of PEGylated Alkyl Glycerides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-60 Glyceryl Isostearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated glyceryl isostearate emulsifier/solubilizer; CIR found PEG glyceryl esters safe, with 1,4-dioxane purified out.

ingredients.name: PEG-60 Hydrogenated Castor Oil
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated hydrogenated castor oil used as solubilizer/emulsifier/surfactant. CIR concluded PEGylated oils safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: PEG-75
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight polyethylene glycol used as humectant/solvent; CIR found PEGs low-toxicity and not absorbed through intact skin.

ingredients.name: PEG-8 Beeswax
ingredients.note: PEG-8 (ethoxylated) beeswax. Wax-derived emulsifier/texture agent that helps oil and water mix; low irritation potential.

sources.label: Final Report on the Safety Assessment of PEG-6, -8, and -20 Sorbitan Beeswax (CIR, SAGE journals)
CIR Safety Assessment of Fatty Ester End-Capped Alkoxylates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-8 Diisostearate
ingredients.note: PEG-8 diisostearate. A PEG ester used as an emollient emulsifier; considered safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of PEG Diesters as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Safety Assessment of Fatty Ester End-Capped Alkoxylates (incl. PEG/PPG-8/3 Diisostearate)

ingredients.name: PEG-8 Laurate
ingredients.note: PEG-8 Laurate is a PEG ester emulsifier made from lauric acid (a fatty acid found in coconut oil) that helps oil and water blend in formulas. The CIR Expert Panel has reviewed PEG laurate ingredients and considers them safe for use in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of PEG Diesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-9 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone-based nonionic emulsifier/surfactant used to stabilize water-in-silicone systems; standard and low concern.

sources.label: Ingredient: PEG-9 POLYDIMETHYLSILOXYETHYL DIMETHICONE - EU CosIng
CIR Safety Assessment of Alkoxy Polysiloxanes as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG-90
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight polyethylene glycol used as a humectant/solvent and viscosity agent; PEGs are reviewed as safe by CIR for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: PEG/PPG-15/15 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A silicone-based emulsifier that helps water and oil blend together in skincare formulas; it gives products a smooth, silky slip on the skin. The CIR Panel reviewed alkoxy polysiloxanes (this ingredient class) and found them safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Alkoxy Polysiloxanes as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: PEG/PPG-17/6 Copolymer
ingredients.note: A water-soluble polyethylene/polypropylene glycol copolymer used as a humectant and solvent/co-emulsifier. Standard, low-hazard formulation aid.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PEG/PPG-18/18 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone-polyether copolymer used as a nonionic emulsifier and skin-conditioning texture agent. Benign, common in formulations.

sources.label: Peg/ppg-18/18 dimethicone - CIR Cosmetic Ingredient Review

ingredients.name: PEG/PPG-20/15 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A silicone-based ingredient that acts as a water-in-silicone emulsifier, helping oil and water blend smoothly in products like sunscreens and moisturizers. It has been reviewed by the CIR Expert Panel and is considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: PEG/PPG/Polybutylene Glycol-8/5/3 Glycerin
ingredients.note: Polyalkylene-glycol glycerin block polymer used as a humectant/skin-conditioning agent; PEG/PPG derivatives concluded safe by CIR.

ingredients.name: Pentaerythrityl Distearate
ingredients.note: Pentaerythrityl distearate is a synthetic ester of pentaerythritol and stearic acid used in cosmetics to provide a smooth, moisturizing texture without heaviness. It is considered safe for cosmetic use based on toxicological data.

sources.label: CIR: Final Safety Assessment of Pentaerythrityl Tetraesters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Pentaerythrityl Tetra-Di-T-Butyl Hydroxyhydrocinnamate
ingredients.note: High-MW hindered-phenol antioxidant that stabilizes formulas; CIR concluded it safe with negligible skin penetration.

ingredients.name: Pentaerythrityl Tetraethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: Pentaerythritol ester used as a light, spreadable emollient and pigment dispersant. Benign cosmetic oil.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Pentaerythrityl Tetraesters as Used in Cosmetics - PubMed
CIR Final Safety Assessment of Pentaerythrityl Tetraesters

ingredients.name: Pentaerythrityl Tetraisostearate
ingredients.note: Pentaerythritol tetraester used as an occlusive emollient and viscosity agent. CIR concluded pentaerythrityl tetraesters are safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Pentapeptide-18
ingredients.note: Pentapeptide-18, also known by the brand name Leuphasyl, is a synthetic peptide that mimics the brain's natural enkephalin pathway to relax facial muscles and reduce the appearance of expression lines. At 0.05% in cream it produced measurable wrinkle reduction, with no significant side effects.

sources.label: PubMed – Pentapeptide-18 as an anti-aging candidate
PMC – Solid lipid nanoparticles with retinol and pentapeptide-18: optimization and cosmetic application

ingredients.name: Pentapeptide-3
ingredients.note: Synthetic anti-wrinkle pentapeptide (acetylcholine-receptor antagonist type) used to soften dynamic lines; good safety profile, rarely irritating.

ingredients.name: Pentapeptide-4
ingredients.note: Synthetic procollagen-fragment signal peptide used as an anti-aging skin-conditioning active; assessed safe within CIR pentapeptide group.

ingredients.name: Pentylene Glycol
ingredients.note: Multifunctional humectant with mild antimicrobial activity; well tolerated and considered safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Perilla Frutescens Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Perilla leaf extract (from the Asian herb used in Japanese cuisine) is loaded with rosmarinic acid and flavonoids that protect skin from UV-induced aging and reduce inflammation. Multiple studies show it improves skin elasticity and reduces hyperpigmentation with a good safety profile.

sources.label: Perilla frutescens leaves extract ameliorates UV-induced extracellular matrix damage in dermal fibroblasts – PubMed
Perilla frutescens Extracts Enhance DNA Repair Response in UVB Damaged HaCaT Cells – PMC

ingredients.name: Persea Gratissima (Avocado) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from avocado fruit used to moisturize and nourish skin, containing vitamins E and C and antioxidants. It has a very low hazard profile and is suitable for most skin types; people with avocado allergies should avoid it.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Punica granatum (Pomegranate)-Derived Ingredients (avocado oil reviewed in same class)

ingredients.name: Persea Gratissima (Avocado) Oil
ingredients.note: Plant-derived emollient oil rich in fatty acids used to soften and condition skin. CIR reviewed plant-derived oils as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Petrolatum
ingredients.note: Highly effective occlusive; cosmetic/USP grades are purified to remove aromatic hydrocarbons and are considered safe and non-comedogenic.

ingredients.name: Petroleum Resin
ingredients.note: A synthetic hydrocarbon polymer derived from petroleum that is used to improve the texture and film-forming properties of cosmetics such as mascaras and lip products; chemically inert and considered safe as a film-forming ingredient.

ingredients.name: Phaseolus Angularis Seed Powder
ingredients.note: Ground adzuki bean seed used as a natural abrasive/exfoliant. Benign botanical powder.

ingredients.name: Phaseolus Lunatus (Green Bean) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Lima/green-bean seed extract used as a skin-conditioning emollient with mild antioxidant properties. Low irritancy risk.

sources.label: Ingredient: PHASEOLUS LUNATUS SEED EXTRACT - EU CosIng

ingredients.name: Phaseolus Radiatus Extract
ingredients.note: Mung bean-derived botanical extract used as a skin-conditioning agent. Benign standard plant ingredient.

ingredients.name: Phaseolus Radiatus Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Mung bean seed extract used as a conditioning/soothing botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Phaseolus Radiatus Seed Powder
ingredients.note: Powdered mung bean seed used as a viscosity/abrasive and antioxidant botanical; benign food-grade plant material.

sources.label: Ingredient: PHASEOLUS RADIATUS SEED POWDER - EU CosIng
Inhibitory effects of mung bean (Vigna radiata) seed and sprout extracts on melanogenesis - PMC
The Whitening, Moisturizing, Anti-aging Activities of Selenium-Enriched Mung Bean Fermentation Broth - PMC

ingredients.name: Phaseolus Radiatus Sprout Extract
ingredients.note: Mung bean sprout extract used as antioxidant, soothing and skin/hair-conditioning botanical rich in flavonoids and polyphenols.

ingredients.name: Phellinus Linteus Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of the Phellinus linteus mushroom used as a skin-conditioning, anti-inflammatory antioxidant. Recognized as safe in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Phellodendron Amurense Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Amur cork tree bark extract (contains berberine) used as a soothing/anti-inflammatory botanical. Considered safe at cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Phenoxyethanol
ingredients.note: Widely used, well-tolerated preservative; the common paraben alternative. Safe at the regulated <=1%.

ingredients.name: Phenyl Trimethicone
ingredients.note: Phenyl-substituted silicone used as an emollient and to add shine/spreadability. Standard cosmetic silicone, well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Phenyl-tert-Butylnitrone
ingredients.note: Nitrone spin-trap antioxidant that stabilizes free radicals in anti-aging formulas. Low irritant potential and generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Phenylalanine
ingredients.note: Essential amino acid used as a skin-conditioning and antistatic agent. Benign topical cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Phenylisopropyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Hydrophobic phenyl-modified silicone fluid used as an emollient and skin/hair conditioning agent giving a smooth, glossy feel. Benign.

ingredients.name: Phenylpropanol
ingredients.note: Phenylpropanol (3-phenyl-1-propanol) is a naturally occurring fragrance compound with a mild floral-rosy scent used in small amounts in cosmetics. It has low acute toxicity and low sensitization potential, though fragrance-sensitive individuals should patch test products.

ingredients.name: Phosphatidylglycerol
ingredients.note: Phospholipid used as an emulsifier, liposome-forming carrier and skin-barrier conditioning agent. Within the CIR-reviewed lecithin/phosphoglyceride class, safe as used.

ingredients.name: Phosphatidylserine
ingredients.note: A phospholipid naturally found in skin cell membranes that helps support skin barrier function and collagen production; used in anti-aging skincare. It is gentle and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Phospholipids
ingredients.note: Amphiphilic lipids used as emulsifiers and skin-conditioning/barrier agents. Benign.

ingredients.name: Phragmites Communis Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the roots of the common reed plant shown to moisturize skin and protect against UV-induced damage. Studies show it is safe and effective at low concentrations in cosmetic formulas.

sources.label: PubMed 27778274 – Moisturizing effect of polysaccharide-rich extract of Phragmites communis in hydrogel bases
Springer Link – Aqueous extract of Phragmites communis rhizomes attenuates phototoxicity in skin cells

ingredients.name: Phyllanthus Emblica Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from amla (Indian gooseberry) berries, one of the richest natural sources of vitamin C and ellagic acid. It is used in skincare for antioxidant, brightening, and photoprotective benefits; research shows it reduces UV-induced skin cell damage and is very safe topically.

sources.label: PubMed – Phyllanthus emblica L. (amla) branch: A safe and effective ingredient against skin aging
PMC – Potential Use of Amla Fruit Extract to Protect Skin Keratinocytes from Inflammation and Apoptosis after UVB Irradiation

ingredients.name: Physalis Angulata Extract
ingredients.note: Botanical extract of Physalis angulata used as a skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Physalis Pubescens Fruit Juice
ingredients.note: Juice from the Physalis pubescens fruit (a small berry in the tomato family), containing amino acids, vitamins, flavonoids, and physalin compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is used in skincare for soothing and brightening; no safety concerns have been identified at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: PMC – Untargeted metabolomics analysis of Physalis pubescens with respect to different varieties

ingredients.name: Phytic Acid
ingredients.note: Plant-derived chelating acid used for gentle exfoliation and brightening. Mild but can increase sensitivity and irritate at high concentration/low pH.

ingredients.name: Phytosphingosine
ingredients.note: Sphingoid skin-barrier lipid used as a conditioning/soothing agent; non-irritating and non-sensitizing per CIR ceramide assessment.

ingredients.name: Phytosterols
ingredients.note: Plant-derived sterols used as emollients and skin-barrier conditioning agents. Reviewed as safe, non-irritating cosmetic ingredients.

ingredients.name: Phytosteryl Canola Glycerides
ingredients.note: A plant-derived blend of canola oil triglycerides and phytosterols that softens skin, supports the skin barrier, and improves product texture; made from food-grade ingredients and shown to be non-irritating with excellent skin compatibility.

sources.label: CosIng: PHYTOSTERYL CANOLA GLYCERIDES (EC Europa, entry 59387)

ingredients.name: Phytosteryl Macadamiate
ingredients.note: Phytosterol ester of macadamia fatty acids used as an emollient and barrier-supporting skin conditioner. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Phytosterols as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Final Report: Safety Assessment of Phytosterols

ingredients.name: Phytosteryl/Behenyl/Octyldodecyl Lauroyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Mixed amino-acid (glutamate) ester used as a ceramide-like emollient and barrier-replenishing skin conditioner; CIR concluded phytosteryl glutamates safe.

ingredients.name: Phytosteryl/Isostearyl/Cetyl/Stearyl/Behenyl Dimer Dilinoleate
ingredients.note: Branched dimer-acid ester blend used as a heavy emollient and gloss/structuring agent. No significant safety concerns.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Amended Safety Assessment of Dialkyl Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics
Amended Safety Assessment of Dialkyl Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics - PubMed

ingredients.name: Phytosteryl/Octyldodecyl Lauroyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Amino-acid (glutamate) lipid ester used as a skin-conditioning emollient and lamellar-structure former; benign.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Phytosteryl Glutamates as Used in Cosmetics
Nano-Emulsion Containing Ceramide-like Lipo-Amino Acid Cholesteryl Derivatives Improves Skin in Atopic Dermatitis - PMC

ingredients.name: Pinus Densiflora Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese red pine needle extract rich in proanthocyanidins/catechins. Used as antioxidant and soothing botanical; benign.

ingredients.name: Pinus Palustris Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Longleaf pine leaf extract used as an antioxidant/conditioning botanical. Standard plant extract; pine resin/terpenes are rare sensitizers but extract is generally tolerated.

ingredients.name: Pinus Pinaster Bark/Bud Extract
ingredients.note: Pinus pinaster bark extract (sold as Pycnogenol) is a highly researched extract rich in procyanidins and phenolic acids with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties; it has been shown in clinical trials to benefit skin health. It is considered safe for topical use and has over 370 published human studies.

sources.label: PMC: Pycnogenol French maritime pine bark extract in randomized double-blind human clinical studies

ingredients.name: Pinus Sylvestris Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from Scots pine needles is used in cosmetics for its flavonoid and tannin content, which provide antioxidant and mild antimicrobial benefits. People allergic to pine trees should use caution; otherwise it is generally safe at typical cosmetic concentrations.

sources.label: Antioxidant Activities of Ethanolic Extracts Obtained from α-Pinene-Containing Plants (incl. Pinus sylvestris needles) and Their Use in Cosmetic Emulsions — PMC
Antioxidant Activities of Ethanolic Extracts Obtained from α-Pinene-Containing Plants and Their Use in Cosmetic Emulsions — PubMed

ingredients.name: Piroctone Olamine
ingredients.note: Well-tolerated anti-dandruff and preservative agent, EU-permitted (up to 0.5-1.0% depending on use). Low irritation and sensitization profile.

ingredients.name: Pistacia Lentiscus (Mastic) Gum
ingredients.note: Aromatic resin from the mastic tree used as a skin-conditioning botanical; not photosensitizing and no EU CosIng restriction. Rare sensitivity possible in Anacardiaceae-allergic individuals.

sources.label: Overview of Chios Mastic Gum (Pistacia lentiscus) Effects on Human Health - PMC
EMA Assessment Report on Pistacia lentiscus (Mastic) Resin
Irritantcy Potential and Sub Acute Dermal Toxicity Study of Pistacia Lentiscus Fatty Oil as a Topical Remedy - PMC

ingredients.name: Pisum Sativum (Pea) Extract
ingredients.note: Pea-derived extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Pisum Sativum Extract / Pea Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from garden peas that is rich in peptides and proteins; in cosmetics it is used as a moisturizing and skin-conditioning agent that supports the skin's barrier. It is safe and well-tolerated, though people with pea allergies should exercise caution.

ingredients.name: Plantago Lanceolata Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Extracted from ribwort plantain leaves, this botanical is valued for soothing irritated or inflamed skin, providing antioxidant protection, and stimulating fibroblast activity to support healing. It's generally well tolerated, though its tannin and trace fragrance components may rarely sensitize extremely reactive skin.

sources.label: ScienceDirect – Biochemical properties, UV-protecting and fibroblast growth-stimulating activity of Plantago lanceolata extracts

ingredients.name: Platonia Insignis (Bacuri) Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Amazonian bacuri seed butter used as an emollient/skin-conditioning fat; benign cosmetic plant butter.

ingredients.name: Platonia Insignis Seed Butter
ingredients.note: A rich vegetable butter pressed from the seeds of the bacuri fruit, a tropical tree native to the Amazon. It is used in cosmetics as an emollient to soften and moisturize skin; research on wound healing in animals supports its skin-protective properties.

sources.label: PubMed — Effects of Bacuri Seed Butter (Platonia insignis) on Metabolic Parameters in Hamsters
PMC — Evaluation of Platonia insignis (Bacuri Butter) for Wound Healing in Horses

ingredients.name: Pleiogynium Timoriense Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the Burdekin plum, a wild Australian fruit rich in phenolic antioxidants; it is used in skincare for its antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits. Early research is promising and no safety concerns have been identified.

sources.label: PubMed – Antiproliferative trihydroxyalkylcyclohexenones from Pleiogynium timoriense (2015)

ingredients.name: Plukenetia Volubilis Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Omega-rich sacha inchi seed oil used as an emollient/skin-conditioning oil; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Podocarpus Elatus Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from an Australian native plum pine fruit high in antioxidant phenolic compounds like gallic acid that help protect skin from environmental stress. It is generally well tolerated and used in small concentrations in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Poloxamer 182
ingredients.note: This is a block copolymer surfactant made of polyethylene oxide and polypropylene oxide chains, used in cosmetics as a solubilizer and gentle cleansing agent. Poloxamers are stable, not metabolized by the body, and generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Poloxamer 184
ingredients.note: A non-ionic block-copolymer surfactant and emulsifier used in cleansers and lotions. The CIR Expert Panel reviewed safety data for poloxamers (including 184) and found no irritation or sensitization — it is safe for cosmetic use at standard concentrations.

sources.label: Safety assessment of poloxamers including 184 as used in cosmetics – PubMed

ingredients.name: Poloxamer 188
ingredients.note: A block copolymer (PEO-PPO-PEO) used as a gentle surfactant, emulsifier, and solubilizer in cosmetics and pharmaceuticals; it is non-irritating and non-sensitizing at typical use concentrations. Commercial grades should be purified to minimize trace impurities such as 1,4-dioxane.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety assessment of poloxamers 101–407 as used in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Poloxamer 338
ingredients.note: A large block copolymer surfactant (polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide) used to stabilize emulsions, solubilize fragrances, and thicken products; poloxamers are widely regarded as non-toxic and non-irritating. It has a good safety record across pharmaceutical and cosmetic applications.

ingredients.name: Poly C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate
ingredients.note: Crosslinked acrylate polymer used as an emulsion stabilizer, film former and rheology modifier; large polymer with low absorption, CIR-supported.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-11
ingredients.note: A crosslinked acrylic polymer used to thicken and stabilize water-in-oil emulsions, giving products a gel-like texture. It is considered safe for skin use; note that like other microplastic polymers, it has raised environmental (waterway) concerns on rinsing.

sources.label: Sage Journals — Safety Assessment of Cross-Linked Alkyl Acrylates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6
ingredients.note: Electrolyte-tolerant acrylic thickener giving a light gel texture. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate-13
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer used as a thickener and emulsion stabilizer (often paired with Polyisobutene). Inert, standard rheology modifier.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate-21
ingredients.note: Acrylate copolymer used as an emulsion stabilizer, film former and viscosity/rheology modifier; high-molecular-weight polymer reviewed as safe with negligible skin penetration.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylic Acid
ingredients.note: Crosslinked acrylic-acid polymer (carbomer-type) used as a thickener, stabilizer and film former. CIR found acrylate polymers/carbomers safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polybutene
ingredients.note: Tacky liquid polymer used for gloss and adhesion in lip products; considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polydextrose
ingredients.note: Polydextrose is a water-soluble glucose polymer (also used as a food additive) that functions as a humectant in cosmetics, helping skin hold onto moisture. Extensive safety studies in animals and humans have found no adverse effects at high doses.

sources.label: PMC: Re-evaluation of polydextrose (E 1200) as a food additive

ingredients.name: Polyester-5
ingredients.note: Synthetic copolymer used as a film former and viscosity controller in makeup and hair styling products. No significant safety concern.

ingredients.name: Polyester-7
ingredients.note: A synthetic polyester polymer used in cosmetics as a film-former and texture enhancer, often in long-wear makeup formulas to improve adhesion and water resistance. It is not absorbed through skin and is considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Polyester-8
ingredients.note: Polyester-8 is a high-molecular-weight UV-absorbing polymer used in cosmetics and sunscreens as a photostabilizer and film-former. Its large size means negligible skin penetration, and it is considered safe in its current cosmetic applications.

ingredients.name: Polyether-1
ingredients.note: PEG/PPG-based copolymer (PEG-180/dodoxynol-5/PEG-25 tristyrylphenol/glycoluril) used as a film former and texture/viscosity agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Polyglutamic Acid
