ingredients.note: Fermentation-derived peptide humectant that holds water at the skin surface; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyglycerin-10
ingredients.note: Polymerized glycerin used as a humectant and moisturizing agent. Benign, glycerin-class hydrating ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Diglycerin and Polyglycerin-3, -6, and higher
Synthesis, Characterization, Self-Assembly, and Irritation Studies of Polyglyceryl-10 Caprylates - PMC

ingredients.name: Polyglycerin-3
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol humectant and solvent used to hydrate and improve texture. Standard low-hazard cosmetic moisturizing ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglycerin-6
ingredients.note: Oligomer of glycerin used as a humectant, solvent and skin-conditioning agent. Glycerin-family material, well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Decaisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and dispersant. Glyceryl-ester surfactant, well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Diisostearate
ingredients.note: Non-ionic PEG-free polyglycerin/isostearic acid ester used as a gentle emulsifier; covered by CIR polyglyceryl fatty acid ester assessment.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Dioleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol diester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Benign texture agent.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Dipalmitate
ingredients.note: A gentle, plant-derived emulsifier made from polyglycerol and palmitic acid, used to blend water and oils in creams and lotions. The CIR evaluated polyglyceryl fatty acid esters as a class and found them safe; it is biodegradable and well tolerated.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Eicosanedioate/Tetradecanedioate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerin diacid esters used as an emollient, skin conditioner and gelling/emulsion agent. Covered by CIR polyglyceryl fatty acid ester assessment; non-irritating in testing.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Isostearate
ingredients.note: Non-ionic polyglycerol ester O/W emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Standard texture ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Laurate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived nonionic surfactant/emulsifier; CIR found polyglyceryl fatty-acid esters safe and it is non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Mono/Dioleate
ingredients.note: Mixed mono/diester of decaglycerol and oleic acid used as a non-ionic emulsifier/surfactant. Polyglyceryl fatty-acid esters are CIR-reviewed and safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Myristate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and surfactant. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol-ester, non-ionic emulsifier/surfactant used in mild cleansers and oil-in-water emulsions. Standard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Pentaisostearate
ingredients.note: This is a mild plant-derived emulsifier made from glycerin and isostearic acid, used to create stable water-in-oil or oil-in-water emulsions. CIR found polyglyceryl fatty acid esters to be safe, and this ingredient was non-irritating in both skin and eye model tests.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics (Final 2016)

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Stearate
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl fatty-acid ester used as a nonionic emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found polyglyceryl esters safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Caprate
ingredients.note: A mild plant-derived emulsifier and surfactant made from polyglycerin and capric acid (from coconut oil), used to help oil and water blend together gently in skin and hair care products. The CIR found polyglyceryl fatty acid esters as a class to be safe with minimal to no irritation.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Diisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of isostearic acid used as a nonionic W/O emulsifier; mild and standard.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Dipolyhydroxystearate
ingredients.note: A polyglycerol ester used as a water-in-oil emulsifier. Standard texture/emulsion ingredient with no notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Isostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a mild nonionic (often W/O) emulsifier and emollient. Standard glyceryl-ester surfactant, low irritation.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Isostearate/Dimer Dilinoleate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl-based ester copolymer used as an emulsifier and texturizing/structuring agent. Standard, low-concern cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Dimer Dilinoleates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Laurate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of lauric acid used as a nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Standard benign emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Sesquicaprylate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol caprylate ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Benign texture ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Stearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of stearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier. Standard, well-tolerated emulsifying agent.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Triisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol-ester, water-in-oil emulsifier and emollient used to stabilize anhydrous and oily formulas. Standard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Beeswax
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of beeswax acids used as a non-ionic emulsifier and texture/structuring agent. Benign cosmetic emulsifier.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Diisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol diester of isostearic acid; a mild nonionic water-in-oil emulsifier and emollient. CIR-reviewed polyglyceryl esters regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Distearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a nonionic oil-in-water emulsifier. CIR considers polyglyceryl esters safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Methylglucose Distearate
ingredients.note: Non-ionic, glucose-derived emulsifier used to stabilize oil-in-water creams. Standard, mild cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of oleic acid used as a non-ionic emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Polyglyceryl esters are CIR-reviewed and considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone-based polyglyceryl emulsifier used for water-in-silicone systems. Inert, standard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Polyricinoleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of ricinoleic acid used as a water-in-oil emulsifier. CIR considers polyglyceryl esters safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Stearate
ingredients.note: A gentle, plant-derived emulsifier made from glycerin and stearic acid that helps blend water and oil in lotions and creams. It is considered safe and is favored in natural/eco-friendly formulas.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Caprate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived, PEG-free nonionic O/W emulsifier and solubilizer. Gentle, low-irritation, suitable for sensitive skin.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Diisostearate/Polyhydroxystearate/Sebacate
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl polymeric ester used as a non-ionic water-in-oil emulsifier/dispersant. Within the CIR polyglyceryl fatty acid esters class concluded safe; non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Isostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl ester used as a mild non-ionic emulsifier. CIR safety assessment of polyglyceryl fatty acid esters found them safe; non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Laurate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty acid ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Standard, gentle cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Lauryl Ether
ingredients.note: Nonionic polyglycerol-based ether emulsifier used to stabilize oil/water emulsions and improve foam. Standard, well-tolerated emulsifying agent.

sources.label: Ingredient: POLYGLYCERYL-4 LAURYL ETHER - EU CosIng
CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of oleic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier/surfactant; mild and standard.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics
Synthesis, Characterization, Self-Assembly, and Irritation Studies of Polyglyceryl-10 Caprylates - PMC

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Behenate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of behenic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier/co-emulsifier. Benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Caprylate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and surfactant. Standard, well-tolerated texture agent.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics
Synthesis, Characterization, Self-Assembly, and Irritation Studies of Polyglyceryl-10 Caprylates - PMC

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Dicaprate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier/emollient. Benign cosmetic texture agent.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Dioleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol diester of oleic acid used as a nonionic W/O emulsifier and emollient. Benign texture/emulsifier ingredient.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Safety Assessment of Diglycerin and Polyglycerin-3, -6, and higher

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Distearate
ingredients.note: A gentle, plant-derived emulsifier made from polyglycerin and stearic acid used in natural and clean-beauty formulations to create stable water-in-oil or oil-in-water emulsions. It is biodegradable and well-tolerated by skin.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Laurate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and solubilizer. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Octastearate
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from vegetable oils and glycerin, used to blend water and oil phases and create smooth emulsion textures in moisturizers and sunscreens. The CIR Expert Panel found polyglyceryl fatty acid esters to be safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of oleic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier. Standard benign emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Pentaoleate
ingredients.note: A plant-derived emulsifier made from glycerin and oleic acid that helps blend water and oil in cosmetics. The CIR Expert Panel found polyglyceryl fatty acid esters safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyglyceryl Fatty Acid Esters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Polyhydroxystearate
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl ester emulsifier used mainly in water-in-oil formulas such as sunscreens and rich moisturizers; it helps oil and water blend smoothly without irritating the skin. It is made from natural polyglycerin and hydroxystearic acid and is considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Polyricinoleate
ingredients.note: Polymeric W/O emulsifier made from polyglycerol and castor-derived ricinoleic acid. Standard low-toxicity emulsifier listed in PubChem and CosIng.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Stearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of stearic acid used as a mild nonionic emulsifier. Benign texture/emulsifying ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Ximenia Americana Seedate
ingredients.note: This is an ester made from polyglycerol and the oil of Ximenia americana seeds (a wild African fruit), used as a skin-softening emollient. Ximenia oil has traditionally been used in Africa to moisturize skin and condition hair, and shows antioxidant and anti-aging properties.

sources.label: PMC – An overview on the properties of Ximenia oil used as cosmetic in Angola

ingredients.name: Polygonum Cuspidatum Root Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese knotweed root extract, a rich natural source of trans-resveratrol and polyphenols used as an antioxidant/anti-aging botanical.

ingredients.name: Polygonum Fagopyrum (Buckwheat) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Buckwheat seed extract rich in rutin/flavonoids, used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. A standard, low-hazard plant extract.

sources.label: Ingredient: POLYGONUM FAGOPYRUM SEED EXTRACT - EU CosIng
Antioxidant and photoprotective properties of an extract from buckwheat herb - PubMed
Phytochemical and Pharmacological Profiles of Three Fagopyrum Buckwheats - PMC

ingredients.name: Polygonum Tinctorium Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese indigo extract used as a skin-conditioning, anti-inflammatory botanical (contains indirubin/tryptanthrin). Benign plant extract.

sources.label: Ingredient: POLYGONUM TINCTORIUM LEAF/STEM EXTRACT - EU CosIng
Antimelanogenic Effects of Polygonum tinctorium Flower Extract from Traditional Jeju Fermentation - PMC

ingredients.name: Polyhydroxystearic Acid
ingredients.note: Fatty-acid-derived dispersant/emulsifier used to coat and stabilize pigments and oils. Standard cosmetic surface treatment.

ingredients.name: Polyisobutene
ingredients.note: Synthetic hydrocarbon polymer used as an emollient and film-former. CIR concludes Polyisobutene is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Polyisobutylene
ingredients.note: A synthetic rubber-like polymer used as a film former and texture agent in lip products, mascaras, and sunscreens; the CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in cosmetics, with no evidence of skin irritation, sensitization, or carcinogenicity.

sources.label: PubMed: Final CIR report on Polyisobutene and Hydrogenated Polyisobutene safety

ingredients.name: Polylactic Acid
ingredients.note: This is a biodegradable polymer derived from lactic acid, used in cosmetics as a film-former and texturizer; it is the same material used in biodegradable sutures. CIR concluded it is safe in cosmetics, noting it is a large molecule unlikely to be absorbed through skin.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Polyhydroxystearic Acid and Polylactic Acid as Used in Cosmetics (2022)

ingredients.name: Polylysine
ingredients.note: Polylysine is a natural antimicrobial polypeptide made by bacterial fermentation; it helps preserve cosmetics and has been explored for anti-acne and skin-regenerating uses. It's considered non-toxic — in the body it breaks down into lysine, an essential amino acid.

sources.label: PubMed – Polylysine for skin regeneration: A review

ingredients.name: Polymethyl Methacrylate
ingredients.note: Spherical acrylic polymer powder used for slip and blur; chemically inert and well tolerated on skin.

ingredients.name: Polymethylsilsesquioxane
ingredients.note: Crosslinked silicone resin powder used for soft-focus and slip; inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyoxyl 40 Stearate
ingredients.note: Polyoxyl 40 Stearate (also called PEG-40 Stearate) is a common nonionic emulsifier that stabilizes creams and lotions; it shows minimal skin and eye irritation in safety studies. It is used in both cosmetics and pharmaceutical formulations and has a well-established safety record.

ingredients.name: Polypodium Leucotomos Extract
ingredients.note: Fern-derived polyphenol antioxidant studied for photo-protection and reducing UV-induced oxidative stress. Safe in topical cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Polypropylene
ingredients.note: Inert thermoplastic polymer used as a bulking/opacifying agent. Large molecular size means negligible dermal absorption; CIR-class safe.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-10
ingredients.note: A cationic cellulose polymer widely used in shampoos and conditioners to reduce static, improve detangling, and add softness to hair. It has a long history of safe use in personal care products and is not considered irritating or sensitizing at typical concentrations.

sources.label: PMC – Detecting Levels of Polyquaternium-10 (PQ-10) via Potentiometric Titration

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-39
ingredients.note: Cationic conditioning polymer used as an antistatic, film-forming hair/skin conditioner; CIR concluded safe as used, not absorbed.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-47
ingredients.note: A positively charged polymer (amphoteric terpolymer of acrylamide, methyl acrylate, and acrylic acid) used in shampoos and conditioners as a film-former and hair conditioning agent that improves manageability and reduces static. It is considered safe for use in rinse-off and leave-on hair products at concentrations up to about 2%.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-51
ingredients.note: Phosphorylcholine-based polymer (Lipidure) used as a biomimetic humectant and film-former. CIR draft assessment of acryloyloxyethyl phosphorylcholine polymers indicates no risk at current use levels.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-67
ingredients.note: Cationic, hydrophobically-modified cellulose polymer used as a film-forming, antistatic hair-conditioning agent for softness and manageability.

sources.label: CIR GREEN Safety Assessment of Polyquaterniums as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-7
ingredients.note: Cationic conditioning polymer/film former. Safe as used; finished polymer is non-irritating with controlled residual monomers.

ingredients.name: Polysilicone-11
ingredients.note: Crosslinked silicone film former used for long-wear pigment hold in makeup; inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polysilicone-15
ingredients.note: Siloxane-backbone UVB absorber. SCCNFP found it safe up to 10% with very low (2%) dermal absorption and no sensitization or photoallergenicity.

sources.label: PubMed: Allergic contact dermatitis from Polysilicone-15 (Parsol SLX)
PubMed: Allergic contact cheilitis caused by Polysilicone-15

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 20
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated sorbitan ester used to solubilize fragrance/oils in water. Long history of safe use; ethoxylation means trace 1,4-dioxane is possible but purified grades control it.

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 60
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated sorbitan ester emulsifier for lotions and creams. Regarded as safe; like other ethoxylates may carry trace 1,4-dioxane controlled by purification.

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 61
ingredients.note: A mild, ethoxylated sorbitan ester used as an emulsifier and solubilizer in cosmetics to keep oil and water ingredients blended together. The CIR Panel has concluded that polysorbates (including Polysorbate 61) are safe as used at concentrations up to 5% in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Polysorbates as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 80
ingredients.note: Widely used nonionic emulsifier and solubilizer. Safe in cosmetics; ethoxylated process can leave trace 1,4-dioxane that reputable suppliers strip out.

ingredients.name: Polyurethane Film
ingredients.note: A film-forming polymer used in cosmetics — including nail products and skin-care — to create a flexible, durable coating on the surface. The CIR Expert Panel assessed polyurethane ingredients and found them safe for cosmetic use within current concentration limits.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Polyurethanes as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report, PDF)

ingredients.name: Polyurethane-35
ingredients.note: Synthetic polyurethane film-former used for water resistance and wear; CIR found polyurethanes safe as used, with only reversible mild eye irritation. Benign.

ingredients.name: Polyurethane-62
ingredients.note: A large-molecular-weight polyurethane polymer used as a film former and binder in sunscreens and skin-care products; it contains no detectable residual isocyanate and the CIR assessed cosmetic polyurethanes as safe.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Polyurethanes as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report PDF)

ingredients.name: Polyvinyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Synthetic polymer used as a binder, film former and viscosity agent (notably peel-off masks). CIR concluded it is safe as used; generally non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Pongamia Pinnata Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from karanja tree seeds rich in phenolic compounds with antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties; studied for applications in skin care and anti-psoriasis formulations. Safe for topical use.

sources.label: Phytochemical, bactericidal, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties of Pongamia pinnata – PubMed
Anti-inflammatory activity of Pongamia pinnata seed extracts – PubMed

ingredients.name: Poria Cocos Extract
ingredients.note: Poria cocos is a medicinal fungus used in Traditional Chinese Medicine; its extract is included in cosmetics for skin brightening (via tyrosinase inhibition), moisture retention, and anti-inflammatory effects. It is generally considered safe for topical use.

sources.label: PubMed: Poria cocos Wolf extracts represses pigmentation in vitro and in vivo
PubMed: Poria cocos Extract Stimulates Aquaporin-3 via PI3K/Akt/mTOR Pathway

ingredients.name: Portulaca Oleracea Extract
ingredients.note: Purslane extract used as a soothing, antioxidant-rich skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Portulaca Pilosa Extract
ingredients.note: Purslane-relative extract rich in hydrating polysaccharides and antioxidants (quercetin, rutin), used as a soothing humectant skin conditioner. Benign.

sources.label: PubMed: Non-clinical healing activities of Portulaca pilosa in skin wounds
PMC: Polysaccharides from Portulaca oleracea – biological activity and application

ingredients.name: Potassium Acrylates Copolymer
ingredients.note: Potassium Acrylates Copolymer is a large polymer used as a thickener, film-former, and texture agent in gels and serums. The CIR concluded that acrylates copolymers are safe in cosmetics when formulated to be non-irritating, noting these large molecules are not well absorbed through the skin.

sources.label: CIR – Amended Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics
Journals.Sagepub – Final Report on Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymer and 33 Related Ingredients

ingredients.name: Potassium Aspartate
ingredients.note: A potassium salt of the amino acid aspartic acid that helps supply trace minerals and support skin cell energy metabolism when applied topically. It is safe and well-tolerated as a cosmetic active ingredient.

ingredients.name: Potassium Benzoate
ingredients.note: Potassium salt of benzoic acid used as a preservative. CIR/EU-reviewed as safe at regulated levels.

ingredients.name: Potassium Cetyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: An alkyl phosphate O/W emulsifier; CIR found it safe when formulated to be non-irritating. May irritate eyes at high concentration in concentrate form.

ingredients.name: Potassium Chloride
ingredients.note: Common inorganic salt used as a viscosity controller and electrolyte; recognized as safe and ubiquitous.

ingredients.name: Potassium Citrate
ingredients.note: A potassium salt of citric acid used in cosmetics as a pH adjuster and chelating agent. The CIR Expert Panel found citric acid and inorganic citrate salts, including potassium citrate, safe for cosmetic use at current concentrations.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Citric Acid, Inorganic Citrate Salts, and Alkyl Citrate Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Potassium Cocoate
aliases: potassium salt of coconut fatty acids
ingredients.note: Potassium soap of coconut fatty acids used as a cleansing surfactant in liquid soaps. Considered safe; alkalinity may mildly irritate at high levels.

ingredients.name: Potassium Cocoyl Glycinate
ingredients.note: Mild amino-acid (glycine) surfactant derived from coconut fatty acids; gentle cleanser well tolerated by sensitive skin.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Amino Acid Alkyl Amides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Potassium Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Potassium salt of hyaluronic acid used as a humectant; CIR concluded hyaluronates safe as used.

ingredients.name: Potassium Hydroxide
ingredients.note: Strong base used to neutralize/saponify and adjust pH. Caustic in raw form but used at trace neutralizing levels in finished products.

ingredients.name: Potassium Laurate
ingredients.note: Potassium soap of lauric acid used as a surfactant/cleansing and emulsifying agent; fatty-acid soaps are reviewed as safe for cosmetic use, mildly alkaline.

ingredients.name: Potassium Magnesium Aspartate
ingredients.note: A mineral-amino-acid salt providing potassium and magnesium ions used as a skin-conditioning/energizing agent. Benign electrolyte-type cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Potassium Phosphate
ingredients.note: Inorganic potassium salt of phosphoric acid used as a pH buffer/adjuster. CIR assessed phosphoric acid and its salts as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Potassium Sorbate
ingredients.note: Very well-tolerated food-grade preservative effective against yeast and mold. EU-permitted up to 0.6% (as acid); low allergenicity.

ingredients.name: PPG-13-Decyltetradeceth-24
ingredients.note: This is a polyethoxylated propylene glycol ether used as an emulsifier and surfactant in cosmetic formulations, helping water and oil mix smoothly. It has low irritation potential and is considered safe in current cosmetic practices; as with all PEG-based ingredients there is a theoretical concern about ethylene oxide trace impurities from manufacturing, which is managed by good manufacturing practice.

ingredients.name: PPG-2 Hydroxyethyl Cocamide
ingredients.note: A coconut-derived amide modified with polypropylene glycol chains, used as a mild surfactant and foam booster in shampoos and body washes. It is listed in the EU CosIng database and covered under CIR assessments of alkoxylated fatty amides, which concluded these ingredients are safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Alkoxylated Fatty Amides as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)

ingredients.name: PPG-3 Benzyl Ether Myristate
ingredients.note: A synthetic ester made from polypropylene glycol, benzyl alcohol, and myristic acid, used as a skin-conditioning emollient and solvent. The CIR found alkyl PEG/PPG ethers safe in cosmetics in normal use; it gives a silky, non-greasy feel to formulations.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: PPG-5-Ceteth-10 Phosphate
ingredients.note: Alkoxylated cetyl alcohol phosphate ester used as a mild emulsifier, surfactant and cleanser. Reported non-irritating and non-sensitizing at use levels.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)
CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers (Draft Report)

ingredients.name: PPG-6-Decyltetradeceth-30
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated/propoxylated fatty alcohol used as a nonionic surfactant, emulsifier, and solubilizer. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Primula Veris Extract
ingredients.note: Cowslip (primrose) extract used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. A standard, low-hazard plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed: Bioactive Phenolic Compounds from Primula veris L. – extraction and purification
PMC: Bioactive Phenolic Compounds from Primula veris L.
PMC: Phytochemical Characterization and Anti-Biofilm Activity of Primula veris L. Roots

ingredients.name: Procollagen
ingredients.note: The precursor protein to collagen; when included in skincare, it is used as a conditioning agent and a biomarker of skin health. Topically applied collagen precursors are too large to penetrate deeply but can condition the skin surface.

sources.label: PubMed – Reduced type I and type III procollagens in photodamaged adult human skin (1995)

ingredients.name: Proline
ingredients.note: Amino acid used as a water-binding skin and hair conditioning agent. Within the CIR alpha-amino acids assessment found safe as used.

ingredients.name: Propane
ingredients.note: Liquefied-gas propellant used in aerosols; inert on skin with low toxicity but extremely flammable and an inhalation hazard. Precautionary for flammability and inhalation, not skin safety.

ingredients.name: Propanediol
ingredients.note: Corn-derived humectant and solvent often used as a gentler alternative to propylene glycol; low sensitization risk and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Propyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Simple primary alcohol used as a carrier solvent in cosmetics; CAS 71-23-8, PubChem CID 1031. Standard cosmetic solvent.

ingredients.name: Propylene Carbonate
ingredients.note: Polar solvent commonly paired with clays (e.g., hectorite) as an activator/viscosity agent; CIR-reviewed and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Propylheptyl Caprylate
ingredients.note: A light synthetic ester emollient providing spreadability and a non-greasy skin feel. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract derived from sweet almond fruit containing vitamins and conditioning compounds; used for skin softening and mild antioxidant protection. The CIR Expert Panel affirmed the safety of sweet almond-derived ingredients for topical use.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Seed Meal
Updated review on sweet almond oil in skin care applications – PubMed

ingredients.name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Oil
ingredients.note: Widely used emollient plant oil. CIR-reviewed as safe; potential allergen only for those with tree-nut allergy.

ingredients.name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Sweet almond seed-derived botanical used for skin conditioning. CIR found sweet almond ingredients practically nonirritating and nonsensitizing in topical use.

ingredients.name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Seed Meal
ingredients.note: Ground residue from sweet almond oil expression used as a physical abrasive/exfoliant in cleansers. CIR reaffirmed safe; not an irritant or sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Seed Powder
ingredients.note: Ground sweet almond seed used as a mechanical abrasive/exfoliant in scrubs and cleansers.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Meal Seed
PubMed: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Seed Meal – safety assessment
PMC: Phytochemical Profiling and Biological Potential of Prunus dulcis Shell Extracts

ingredients.name: Prunus Armeniaca (Apricot) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Apricot fruit extract used as a botanical skin-conditioning/antioxidant agent; standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Prunus Armeniaca (Apricot) Kernel Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from apricot kernels containing antioxidant polyphenols, fatty acids, and phytosterols; it is used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning and nourishing ingredient. Bitter apricot kernels contain amygdalin (a cyanogenic glycoside), but at typical cosmetic extract concentrations this is not a concern for topical use.

sources.label: PMC: Unlocking the Therapeutic and Antimicrobial Potential of Prunus armeniaca Seed Kernel Oil
PMC: Identification of Fatty Acid, Lipid and Polyphenol Compounds from Prunus armeniaca Kernel Extracts

ingredients.name: Prunus Armeniaca (Apricot) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Apricot kernel oil used as an emollient and skin conditioner; high in oleic/linoleic acids. Benign plant oil.

sources.label: CIR: Prunus armeniaca (apricot) kernel oil ingredient page
PMC: Apricot kernel characterization, oil extraction, and utilization – review
PMC: Unlocking the Therapeutic and Antimicrobial Potential of Prunus armeniaca Seed Kernel Oil

ingredients.name: Prunus Armeniaca (Apricot) Seed Powder
ingredients.note: Ground apricot kernel used as a natural physical exfoliant in scrubs. Considered gentle and benign.

sources.label: PubMed: Chemical Composition and Antioxidant Properties of Oils from Apricot (Prunus armeniaca) Seeds
PMC: Apricot kernel characterization, oil extraction, and utilization – review
PMC: Anticancer Potential and Pharmacological Properties of Prunus armeniaca L.

ingredients.name: Prunus Avium (Sweet Cherry) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from sweet cherry flowers that is rich in phenolic compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity; it is used in skin-care products to help protect against oxidative stress and soothe skin. It is generally well-tolerated with no significant safety concerns reported.

sources.label: PMC9138022 – Phenolic Compounds from Cherry Tree (Prunus avium) Branches: Impact on Cosmetic Properties
PubMed 36364838 – Anti-Inflammatory and Antimicrobial Activities of Prunus avium By-Product Extracts

ingredients.name: Prunus Avium (Sweet Cherry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Sweet cherry fruit extract used as a botanical antioxidant/skin-conditioning agent; standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Prunus Avium (Sweet Cherry) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Plant oil from sweet cherry kernels used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign botanical oil.

sources.label: CIR: Prunus avium (sweet cherry) seed oil ingredient page
PMC: Bioactive Potential of Sweet Cherry (Prunus avium L.) Waste: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties
DOI: Utilization of Blackthorn Plums and Sweet Cherry (Prunus avium) Kernel Oil – composition and oxidative stability

ingredients.name: Prunus Avium Flower Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the flowers of the sweet cherry tree, rich in phenolic antioxidants with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In vitro studies show no cytotoxic effects on skin cells, supporting its safe use in cosmetics.

sources.label: PMC — Phenolic Compounds Extracted from Cherry Tree (Prunus avium): Impact on Cosmetic Properties

ingredients.name: Prunus Domestica Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Plum fruit extract used as a skin-conditioning, antioxidant botanical. Standard benign plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed: Cosmetic potentials of Prunus domestica L. leaves
PMC: Free radical scavenging capacity and antioxidant activity of plum (Prunus domestica L.) extracts

ingredients.name: Prunus Domestica Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Plum seed oil is a lightweight emollient rich in oleic acid (72–75%) and natural antioxidants, making it excellent for moisturizing and protecting skin without feeling greasy. It has good oxidative stability and no known significant toxicity concerns in topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: Valorization of Prunus Seed Oils: Fatty Acids Composition and Oxidative Stability – PMC
Physico-Chemical Properties and Oxidative Stability of Fixed Oil from Plum Seeds – PMC

ingredients.name: Prunus Mume Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the Japanese/Chinese plum (ume) fruit used in cosmetics for antioxidant and skin-brightening effects. Studies show it suppresses melanin production and protects against UV-induced aging; it is safe at the recommended cosmetic concentration of up to 10%.

sources.label: PubMed — Prunus mume extract antioxidant and anti-melanogenesis activity
PubMed — Prunus mume Seed: Inhibitory Effect on Skin Senescence via SIRT1 and MMP-1 Regulation

ingredients.name: Prunus Mume Flower Water
ingredients.note: A floral water distilled from Japanese apricot (ume) blossoms that is used in toners and essences for its antioxidant, anti-glycation, and mild skin-brightening properties; animal and patch-test data confirm it is non-irritating and non-photosensitizing.

sources.label: PMC: Biological and epidemiological evidence of anti-allergic effects of traditional Japanese food ume (Prunus mume)

ingredients.name: Prunus Mume Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese apricot (ume) fruit extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed: Prunus mume Seed Inhibitory Effect on Skin Senescence via SIRT1 and MMP-1 Regulation
PMC: Comprehensive Review of Phytochemical Constituents and Pharmacological Properties of Prunus mume

ingredients.name: Prunus Mume Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Prunus Mume Fruit Water is a distillate from Japanese ume plums, containing organic acids and polyphenols that mildly tone, soothe, and hydrate the skin. It's a gentle ingredient used primarily in Korean and Japanese skincare with no notable safety issues.

sources.label: PubMed – Prunus mume seed inhibitory effect on skin senescence

ingredients.name: Prunus Persica (Peach) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Peach flower extract used as an antioxidant and soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Prunus Persica (Peach) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Peach pulp extract used as moisturizing, soothing and skin-conditioning agent with antioxidant properties.

sources.label: PubMed: Extract of Prunus persica flowers protects against UV-induced skin damage in vivo
PubMed: Protection of Prunus persica extract from UVB-induced damage of normal human keratinocytes
PMC: Topical or oral peach flower extract attenuates UV-induced epidermal thickening and pro-inflammatory cytokines

ingredients.name: Prunus Yedoensis Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the leaves of the Yoshino cherry tree (famous for its blossoms in Japan) that contains antioxidants shown to help protect skin from UV-induced damage and signs of aging. It is gentle and generally well tolerated.

sources.label: PMC – Inhibition of UV-induced MMP-1 by cherry blossom (Prunus yedoensis) extract (2021)
PubMed – Antiphotoaging effect of Prunus yedoensis blossom extract (2018)

ingredients.name: Pseudoalteromonas Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: Antarctic marine bacterial ferment yielding glycoproteins/polysaccharides used as a hydrating, antioxidant skin-conditioning active. Suitable for all skin types; low concern.

sources.label: PMC: Pseudoalteromone A from marine Pseudoalteromonas spp. suppresses melanogenesis
PMC: Marine Microbial-Derived Molecules – potential use in cosmeceutical and cosmetic products
PMC: Marine versus Non-Marine Bacterial Exopolysaccharides and their Skincare Applications

ingredients.name: Pseudozyma Epicola/Camellia Japonica Seed Oil Ferment Extract Filtrate
ingredients.note: A filtrate produced by fermenting camellia seed oil with the yeast Pseudozyma epicola, which creates bioactive molecules with moisturizing and antioxidant effects. It is lightly textured and absorbs quickly, making it a popular addition to serums and essences.

sources.label: EU CosIng Ingredient: PSEUDOZYMA EPICOLA/CAMELLIA JAPONICA SEED OIL FERMENT EXTRACT FILTRATE — European Commission

ingredients.name: Pseudozyma Epicola/Camellia Sinensis Seed Oil/Glucose/Glycine Soja (Soybean) Meal/Malt Extract/Yeast Extract Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: A complex ferment filtrate produced by fermenting green tea seed oil, soybean meal, and yeast with the yeast-like fungus Pseudozyma epicola. The resulting filtrate is used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent to support moisture retention and skin barrier function; it has a low concern profile based on available safety information.

sources.label: Mannosylerythritol lipids (Pseudozyma-derived glycolipids) ameliorate UVA-induced aquaporin-3 downregulation in human keratinocytes (PMC6384198)

ingredients.name: Psidium Guajava Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Guava fruit extract is rich in vitamins, flavonoids, and tannins with antioxidant and antimicrobial activity; cosmetic research shows whitening, anti-wrinkle, and skin-conditioning benefits. Safe for topical use.

sources.label: Guava fruit extract prepared by fermentation – PubMed
Chemical composition and biological activity of Psidium guajava leaf extracts – PMC

ingredients.name: Psidium Guajava Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Guava seed oil is pressed from guava seeds and is rich in linoleic acid, phenolic compounds, and phytosterols with strong antioxidant activity; it is used in cosmetics as a nourishing emollient. Safety studies have found it to be relatively non-toxic to normal cells at typical use concentrations.

sources.label: PubMed: Linoleic acid-rich guava seed oil: Safety and bioactivity
PMC: Phytosterol, Lipid and Phenolic Composition, and Biological Activities of Guava Seed Oil

ingredients.name: Pteris Multifida Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from the spider brake fern (used in traditional Chinese medicine) is explored in skincare for its antioxidant properties and ability to inhibit MMP-1, an enzyme that breaks down collagen and contributes to skin aging. It is a rare ingredient, and while initial data are promising, fewer large-scale safety studies are available compared to more common botanicals.

sources.label: Free radical-scavenging activity of aqueous extract of Pteris multifida Poiret — PubMed
Anti-Neuroinflammatory ent-Kaurane Diterpenoids from Pteris multifida Roots — PMC

ingredients.name: Pullulan
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide from starch fermentation used as a film former and binder; water-soluble, non-toxic, gives a temporary tightening film.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum (Pomegranate) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A rich oil cold-pressed from pomegranate seeds, high in punicic acid and antioxidants. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in cosmetics; it is prized for supporting skin barrier function and reducing inflammation.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Punica granatum-Derived Ingredients (Final Report)
PMC — Efficacy and safety of a proprietary Punica granatum extract in skin health (RCT)

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Pomegranate flower extract used as an antioxidant/conditioning botanical. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Fruit Juice
ingredients.note: Pomegranate juice in skincare delivers antioxidants like ellagic acid and punicalagins that help protect skin. The CIR Expert Panel concluded pomegranate-derived ingredients are safe in cosmetics, though formulators should avoid levels that could cause unwanted depigmentation.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Punica granatum-Derived Ingredients (PDF)
PMC 11733350 – Efficacy and safety of Punica granatum extract in skin health (clinical trial)

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Pericarp (Pomegranate) Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from pomegranate rinds, which make up ~40% of the fruit and are extremely rich in ellagitannins and ellagic acid; clinical studies show it reduces wrinkles, controls melanin production, and protects against UV damage. Safe and well-studied.

sources.label: Punica granatum pericarp extract, antioxidant properties and UVA protection – PMC
Efficacy and safety of Punica granatum extract for skin health – PMC

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Pericarp Extract
ingredients.note: Pomegranate peel extract rich in ellagitannins/ellagic acid and polyphenols used as an antioxidant and soothing botanical. Considered safe and non-sensitizing at 0.1-1%.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Pomegranate seed oil used as an emollient/moisturizing oil rich in fatty acids and antioxidants. CIR concluded pomegranate-derived ingredients safe as used; low concern profile.

ingredients.name: PVM/MA Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic film-former and viscosity/binding agent used for hold and texture. Standard cosmetic polymer considered safe by CIR.

ingredients.name: PVP
ingredients.note: Synthetic film-forming polymer used for hold and viscosity. Inert and broadly safe in topical use.

ingredients.name: Pycnogenol
ingredients.note: Standardized maritime pine bark polyphenol antioxidant used for anti-aging and brightening. Considered safe in topical products.

ingredients.name: Pyridoxine
ingredients.note: Vitamin B6, used as a skin/hair conditioning and antioxidant agent. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Pyrus Communis (Pear) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Pear fruit extract used as a humectant/soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fiber
ingredients.note: Ground dried apple fruit fiber used as a mild abrasive/exfoliant and skin-conditioning agent; covered by CIR apple-derived assessment.

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Apple fruit extract used as a conditioning/antioxidant botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Apple fruit water (aqueous fraction). Mild hydrating botanical; a gentle source of fruit-derived humectant sugars and acids.

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus Fruit Water
ingredients.note: This is the steam-distilled water (hydrosol) obtained from apples, used in cosmetics as a light, refreshing skin-conditioning ingredient. The CIR assessed apple-derived ingredients and found them safe; a patch test with a product containing 8.7% apple fruit water showed only very minor transient reactions in a small number of people.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Apple-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Pyruvic Acid
ingredients.note: Alpha-keto acid exfoliant that converts to lactic acid in skin and is used in peels. Increases sun sensitivity and can be irritating, especially at high strength.

ingredients.name: Quercetin
ingredients.note: Plant flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory and free-radical-scavenging activity. Safe at topical cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Quercus Suber Bark Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the bark of the cork oak tree containing phenolic acids and ellagitannins; it is used in cosmetics for antioxidant protection and a smoothing/tightening effect on skin. A clinical study confirmed a tensor effect and acceptable safety profile at 3% concentration.

sources.label: PubMed: Quercus suber cork extract displays tensor and smoothing effect on human skin
PMC: Added-Value Compounds in Cork By-Products for Pharmaceutical and Cosmetic Interest

ingredients.name: Quillaja Saponaria Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Saponin-rich soapbark extract used as a natural foaming surfactant; FDA GRAS for food use. Mild irritation possible at high concentration in sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed: Separation and structural analysis of saponins in a bark extract from Quillaja saponaria Molina
PMC: Re-evaluation of Quillaia extract (E 999) as a food additive – safety

ingredients.name: Raffinose
ingredients.note: Plant-derived trisaccharide used as a water-binding humectant. Not known to pose irritation risk.

sources.label: PubMed: Raffinose modulates epidermal differentiation through activation of liver X receptor
PMC: The trisaccharide raffinose modulates epidermal differentiation through activation of liver X receptor

ingredients.name: Raphanus Sativus (Radish) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from radish seeds rich in glucosinolates and antioxidants that conditions, soothes, and helps protect skin; also used as a natural preservation booster in some formulations, and considered safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: PMC: Comparison of Anti-Inflammatory and Antibacterial Properties of Raphanus sativus Extracts
PMC: Glucosinolate extract from Raphanus sativus seed

ingredients.name: Raphanus Sativus Root Extract
ingredients.note: Radish root extract is rich in vitamins B and C, glucosinolates, and polyphenols that provide antioxidant and gentle exfoliating benefits to skin. It is generally considered safe with no known significant toxicity, and is suitable for most skin types.

sources.label: Comparison of Anti-Inflammatory and Antibacterial Properties of Raphanus sativus Extracts – PMC

ingredients.name: Red 22 (CI 45380)
ingredients.note: A bromine-containing fluorescent red dye (also called Eosin Y) approved by the FDA for use in cosmetics such as lipsticks and blushers; it gives products a vivid red-to-pink color. It is approved for external use and considered safe at normal cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Red 22 Lake (CI 45380)
ingredients.note: This is the insoluble lake form of D&C Red No. 22 (eosin), a certified color additive approved by the FDA for use in cosmetics. It is subject to FDA batch certification and is generally considered safe for external use at permitted concentrations.

sources.label: FDA – Color Additives Permitted for Use in Cosmetics
FDA – Summary of Color Additives for Use in the United States

ingredients.name: Red 27 (CI 45410)
ingredients.note: FDA-certified synthetic xanthene dye approved for cosmetics/drugs (lip products; not for eye area); may cause irritation but low risk within FDA specifications.

ingredients.name: Red 28 (CI 45410)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved synthetic xanthene dye (Phloxine B) used as a colorant in lip and color cosmetics. SCCS found Acid Red 92 not a skin sensitizer under test conditions; FDA sets a daily limit.

ingredients.name: Red 28 Lake (CI 45410)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved synthetic xanthene dye (D&C Red 28) used in lip and color cosmetics; aluminum lake form. SCCS found it non-sensitizing and non-tumorigenic, with mild eye irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Red 30 (CI 73360)
ingredients.note: A synthetic thioindigo-based pink/red pigment (D&C Red No. 30) permanently listed by the FDA as an approved color additive for use in cosmetics and drugs for coloring lips, cheeks, and other products. It is considered safe as used; unlike azo dyes, thioindigo pigments are not associated with carcinogen concerns.

sources.label: FDA – Color Additives Permitted for Use in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Red 40 (CI 16035)
ingredients.note: FDA-certified synthetic azo colorant approved for cosmetics (max ~0.4%, not for eye area); EU requires warning labeling. Possible allergic reactions but topically considered safe.

ingredients.name: Red 6 (CI 15850)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved synthetic azo colorant (D&C Red 6/7) for lip, face and nail products; not permitted in the eye area. Used per GMP limits.

ingredients.name: Red 6 Lake (CI 15850)
ingredients.note: A red azo dye lake (D&C Red No. 6) that has been approved by the FDA for use in cosmetics including lip products; as a lake pigment it is insoluble and does not readily absorb into skin, making it safer than water-soluble dyes. It is widely used in lipsticks and blushes.

sources.label: FDA – Color Additives and Cosmetics Fact Sheet

ingredients.name: Red 7 Lake (CI 15850)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved, batch-certified red lake pigment used in lipsticks and color cosmetics. Safe as used per FDA color additive regulations.

sources.label: FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – DC Red 7

ingredients.name: Resveratrol
ingredients.note: Polyphenol antioxidant from grapes and Japanese knotweed used for anti-aging and brightening. Considered safe at topical cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Retinol
ingredients.note: Effective OTC anti-aging active; safe for general use. Precautionary: avoid in pregnancy and introduce slowly. Distinct from prescription tretinoin (Tier 1).

ingredients.name: Rheum Rhabarbarum Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from garden rhubarb containing stilbenes and anthraquinones with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties; it is used in skincare for brightening and photoprotection. It is generally safe topically, though rhubarb taken internally in large amounts can be harmful.

sources.label: PubMed – Photoprotective and antioxidant effects of rhubarb extract in melanocytes (2013)
PMC – Rheum rhaponticum and Rheum rhabarbarum as modulators of endothelial inflammatory response (2023)

ingredients.name: Rhizobian Gum
ingredients.note: A natural gum produced by fermenting Rhizobium bacteria, used as a thickener and stabilizer in cosmetic formulas. It is considered safe and is a biodegradable alternative to some synthetic thickeners.

sources.label: PMC 10819756 – Physicochemical and rheological properties of succinoglycan

ingredients.name: Rhus Succedanea Fruit Wax
ingredients.note: Japan wax from sumac-tree fruit (fatty acids, glycerides, phytosterols) used as an emollient/film-forming structuring wax; reviewed by CIR within waxes, low-risk.

ingredients.name: Ribes Nigrum (Black Currant) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: GLA-rich black currant seed oil used as an emollient/skin-conditioning oil; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Ribes Nigrum Bud Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from black currant buds, rich in anthocyanins and flavonols; shown to protect against UV-induced photoaging in human fibroblasts and reduce skin inflammation. Safe for topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: Ribes nigrum prevents UVB-mediated photoaging in human dermal fibroblasts – PubMed
Ribes nigrum leaf extract inhibits IFN-γ-mediated inflammation – PMC

ingredients.name: Rice Amino Acids
ingredients.note: Rice Amino Acids are the hydrolysis products of rice protein, providing a mix of conditioning amino acids that smooth and moisturize both skin and hair. The CIR and peer-reviewed literature confirm this ingredient class is safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Plant-Derived Proteins and Peptides as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed – Safety assessment of animal- and plant-derived amino acids as used in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Rice Ferment Filtrate (Sake)
ingredients.note: Fermented-rice (sake) filtrate rich in amino acids, organic acids and polyphenols used as a hydrating, brightening, antioxidant active. Low-hazard, well-tolerated ferment ingredient.

ingredients.name: Ricinus Communis (Castor) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Castor seed oil, an occlusive emollient rich in ricinoleic acid; CIR-assessed as safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Ricinus Communis Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Castor oil is a thick vegetable oil from castor bean seeds, rich in ricinoleic acid; it is widely used in cosmetics as a moisturizing emollient, hair conditioner, and film-forming agent. The CIR Expert Panel has confirmed it and its derivatives are safe in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Ricinus Communis (Castor) Seed Oil and Derivatives
CIR – Ricinus communis (castor) seed oil ingredient page

ingredients.name: Rosa Canina Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Rosehip (dog rose) fruit extract used as an antioxidant (vitamin C, carotenoids) and skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Rosa Canina Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Essential-fatty-acid-rich rosehip seed oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Considered safe and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Rosa Centifolia Flower Water
ingredients.note: Cabbage rose hydrosol (rose water). Soothing, hydrating aromatic water; gentle, may carry trace fragrance allergens.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Rosa centifolia-derived ingredients as used in cosmetics
Inhibition of neutrophil adhesion and antimicrobial activity of rose hydrosol (Rosa damascena) (PubMed)
In vitro efficacy of Rosa fermentation liquid and water extract on skin care (PMC)

ingredients.name: Rosa Gallica Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Rose flower extract used as a botanical soothing/antioxidant agent; standard plant extract (trace fragrance constituents).

ingredients.name: Rosa Gallica Flower Powder
ingredients.note: Dried rose flower powder used as a botanical skin-conditioning/abrasive additive. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Rosa Multiflora Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Rose-hip fruit extract rich in vitamin C and flavonoids, used as antioxidant skin-conditioning agent. Generally regarded non-irritating at cosmetic levels.

sources.label: PubMed: Rosa multiflora Flower Extract Attenuates UV-Induced Photoaging in Skin Cells and Hairless Mice
PubMed: Topical application of Rosa multiflora root extract improves atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions
PMC: Rosa multiflora with PPAR alpha/gamma dual agonistic effects prevents photoaging in hairless mice

ingredients.name: Rosa Rubiginosa Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Rosehip seed oil rich in unsaturated fatty acids used as an emollient/skin-conditioning oil; standard low-risk botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Rosehip Oil
ingredients.note: Essential-fatty-acid and provitamin-A oil supporting skin renewal; a benign, well-tolerated facial oil.

ingredients.name: Rosemary Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Carnosic-acid-rich antioxidant extract (not the essential oil); generally well tolerated and used to stabilize formulas and calm skin.

ingredients.name: Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Rosemary leaf extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical; CIR found derived ingredients safe, with the extract (not the oil) not an irritant or sensitizer at use levels up to 0.2%.

ingredients.name: Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Water
ingredients.note: The aromatic water produced during steam distillation of rosemary leaves; it functions as a skin conditioner and light fragrance in cosmetics. The CIR Panel found rosemary-derived ingredients safe when formulated to be non-sensitizing.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Rosmarinus officinalis (Rosemary)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)
Sage Journals — Safety Assessment of Rosmarinus officinalis (Rosemary)-Derived Ingredients

ingredients.name: Rubus Chamaemorus (Cloudberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Cloudberry is an Arctic berry exceptionally rich in vitamin C, anthocyanins, ellagitannins, and beta-carotene, all of which give this extract powerful antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties. There are no known significant safety concerns — it is well-tolerated and has been considered 100% safe in cosmetics databases.

sources.label: Comprehensive Characterization of Secondary Metabolites in Cloudberry (Rubus chamaemorus L.) – PMC

ingredients.name: Rubus Chamaemorus (Cloudberry) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: An oil cold-pressed from the seeds of cloudberries, a Nordic wild fruit rich in omega fatty acids, tocopherols, and carotenoids. It is used as a nourishing emollient in premium skincare; it is well tolerated and is supported by a PubChem patent documenting its use in cosmetics.

sources.label: PubChem Patent — Cosmetic compositions containing cloudberry (Rubus chamaemorus) seed oil
PMC — Comprehensive Characterization of Secondary Metabolites in Cloudberry (Rubus chamaemorus)

ingredients.name: Rubus Chamaemorus Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A cold-pressed oil from cloudberry seeds that is exceptionally rich in omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids plus vitamin E; it nourishes and protects skin and is used in premium skincare. It is safe and well-tolerated, with antioxidant benefits.

sources.label: PubChem Patent WO-0238109-A1 – Cosmetic compositions containing cloudberry seed oil
PMC – Comprehensive Characterization of Secondary Metabolites in Cloudberry (Rubus chamaemorus L.)

ingredients.name: Rubus Ellipticus Root Extract
ingredients.note: Yellow Himalayan raspberry root extract rich in tannins and polyphenols used as a firming, moisturizing, antioxidant skin conditioner.

ingredients.name: Rubus Idaeus (Raspberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from red raspberries rich in polyphenols, ellagic acid, and antioxidant vitamins. Research shows it helps hydrate skin, protects against UVB damage, and has anti-inflammatory properties; it is considered very safe for topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: PubMed – Oil-soluble Rubus idaeus extract enhances hydration and water homeostasis in skin cells
PMC – Red Raspberry Extract Protects the Skin against UVB-Induced Damage with Antioxidative and Anti-inflammatory Properties

ingredients.name: Rubus Idaeus (Raspberry) Juice
ingredients.note: Juice from red raspberries containing vitamin C, anthocyanins, and other antioxidants that help brighten and protect skin. It is gentle and considered very safe for topical use.

sources.label: PubMed – An oil-soluble extract of Rubus idaeus cells enhances hydration in skin cells (2015)
PMC – Red Raspberry (Rubus idaeus L.) Seed Oil: A Review (2021)

ingredients.name: Rubus Idaeus (Raspberry) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Linoleic/linolenic-rich raspberry seed oil used as an antioxidant emollient; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Ruscogenin
ingredients.note: Steroidal saponin/sapogenin from Ruscus aculeatus with vasoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties; used as a skin-conditioning botanical active. Benign at cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Rutin
ingredients.note: Plant flavonoid glycoside used as an antioxidant; PubChem CID 5280805, well characterized.

ingredients.name: Saccharide Hydrolysate
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed sucrose (mainly glucose/fructose) used as a humectant. CIR concluded it is safe in present practices of use.

ingredients.name: Saccharide Isomerate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived carbohydrate complex that binds to skin for long-lasting hydration; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces Ferment
ingredients.note: Yeast fermentation product used as a skin-conditioning/antioxidant active. CIR assessed yeast-derived ingredients as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Filtrate of fermented yeast used as a hydrating, barrier-supporting skin-conditioning active; benign as used.

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces/Camellia Sinensis Leaf/Cladosiphon Okamuranus/Rice Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Composite yeast/botanical ferment filtrate used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant active. CIR yeast-derived ingredient review found such ferment filtrates safe as used; rare yeast sensitivity possible.

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces/Copper Ferment
ingredients.note: Yeast ferment delivering bioavailable copper used as a skin-conditioning active; benign cosmetic ferment at use levels.

sources.label: PubMed: Evaluation of dermatological effects of cosmetic formulations containing Saccharomyces cerevisiae extract and vitamins
PMC: Evaluation of Biotechnological Active Peptides Secreted by Saccharomyces cerevisiae with Potential Skin Benefits

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces/Magnesium Ferment
ingredients.note: Yeast ferment delivering bioavailable magnesium used as a skin-conditioning/soothing active; benign cosmetic ferment.

sources.label: PubMed: Evaluation of dermatological effects of cosmetic formulations containing Saccharomyces cerevisiae extract and vitamins
PMC: Skin Health Function of Distilled Soju Byproduct Fermented with Saccharomyces cerevisiae

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces/Rice Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Yeast/rice ferment filtrate used as a low-hazard moisturizing and antioxidant active. CIR yeast-derived ingredient assessment supports safety as used; rare yeast sensitivity possible.

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces/Silicon Ferment
ingredients.note: Yeast ferment delivering bioavailable silicon used as a skin-conditioning active; benign cosmetic ferment.

sources.label: PubMed: Evaluation of dermatological effects of cosmetic formulations containing Saccharomyces cerevisiae extract and vitamins
PMC: Evaluation of Biotechnological Active Peptides Secreted by Saccharomyces cerevisiae with Potential Skin Benefits

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces/Xylinum/Black Tea Ferment
ingredients.note: This is a kombucha-like ferment of black tea by Saccharomyces yeast and Acetobacter xylinum (Komagataeibacter xylinus), producing a complex of organic acids, antioxidants, and bioactive compounds that soothe and protect skin. Cell and clinical studies suggest a favorable safety profile for topical use.

sources.label: PMC – Cosmetic and dermatological properties and safety of model skin tonics with kombucha-fermented red berry extracts

ingredients.name: Saccharum Officinarum (Sugar Cane) Extract
ingredients.note: Sugarcane extract used as a humectant and skin-conditioning botanical, marketed as a natural source of glycolic-type acids. Benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Saccharum Officinarum (Sugarcane) Extract
ingredients.note: Sugarcane extract, a source of glycolic acid and sugars, used as a humectant/conditioning agent. Standard benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Salicylic acid
ingredients.note: Effective acne exfoliant; safe at cosmetic levels. Precautionary note only: pregnancy caution at high concentrations and it can over-dry if layered. Not an endocrine/drug concern.

ingredients.name: Salicylic Acid (as preservative)
ingredients.note: Used at low levels as a preservative (distinct from its exfoliant role), EU-permitted up to 0.5% for that purpose. Generally well tolerated but restricted in products for children under 3.

ingredients.name: Salix Alba (Willow) Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Willow bark extract containing salicin (a salicylic acid precursor) used as a soothing/exfoliating botanical; CIR-reviewed as safe. Salicylate-allergic users should use caution.

ingredients.name: Salix Alba Bark Water
ingredients.note: A floral/botanical water made from white willow bark that contains naturally occurring salicylates (including salicin) with gentle exfoliating and anti-inflammatory properties; people who are allergic to aspirin (salicylates) should use caution.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Salix alba (Willow)-Derived Ingredients (PDF)
PubMed: Efficacy and Safety of White Willow Bark (Salix alba) Extracts

ingredients.name: Salix Nigra (Willow) Bark Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from black willow bark containing salicin, polyphenols, and antioxidants that soothe skin and have mild anti-acne activity. Unlike pure salicylic acid, the concentration of active compounds in this extract is low enough that it rarely causes irritation.

sources.label: PubMed 31604354 – United States Pharmacopeia Safety Review of Willow Bark
PubMed 37235713 – Effect of Salix alba bark extract on dark circles in vitro and in vivo

ingredients.name: Salmon Egg Extract
ingredients.note: A marine-derived extract from salmon eggs containing proteins, phospholipids, and antioxidants; clinical trials show benefits including wrinkle reduction, improved hydration, and more even pigmentation with no reported adverse effects. Avoid if you have a fish allergy.

sources.label: Chum salmon egg extracts induce collagen type I and exert antioxidative effects – PMC
Composition characterization and clinical efficacy of salmon egg extract – ResearchGate

ingredients.name: Salvia Hispanica (Chia) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from chia seeds containing omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols (rosmarinic acid, quercetin), and antioxidants; it is used in cosmetics to hydrate skin and strengthen its barrier. Clinical research on topical chia extract has shown skin hydration benefits and a good safety profile.

sources.label: Wiley Journal of Cosmetic Dermatology: HYVIA topical chia seed extract improves skin hydration
PMC: Safety of chia seeds (Salvia hispanica L.) as novel food (EFSA)

ingredients.name: Salvia Hispanica Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Chia seed oil rich in omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid, used as an emollient and barrier-replenishing skin-conditioning oil. Benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Salvia Miltiorrhiza Root Extract
ingredients.note: Danshen (red sage) root extract rich in tanshinones and salvianolic acids, used as an antioxidant/soothing skin-conditioning botanical.

sources.label: PubMed: Effects of Danshensu and Salvianolic Acid B from Salvia miltiorrhiza on cell proliferation and collagen and melanin production
PMC: Effects of Danshensu and Salvianolic Acid B from Salvia miltiorrhiza on collagen and melanin production
DOI: Danshensu and Salvianolic Acid B from Salvia miltiorrhiza – cell proliferation, collagen, and melanin

ingredients.name: Salvia Officinalis (Sage) Extract
ingredients.note: Sage extract contains antioxidant rosmarinic acid and has antimicrobial properties valued in skincare and haircare. The CIR found leaf-based sage ingredients safe in cosmetics, but notes that certain terpene constituents (like thujone) could be sensitizing at high levels, so formulators must keep concentrations in check.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Salvia officinalis (Sage)-Derived Ingredients (Technical Report)
CIR Safety Assessment of Salvia officinalis – SAGE Journals (2026)

ingredients.name: Salvia Officinalis (Sage) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from common sage leaves, used in cosmetics as an antioxidant, skin-conditioning, and fragrance ingredient. It is FDA-GRAS and safe at the low concentrations used in cosmetics, but contains terpenes (including thujone) that can sensitize some people, so those with sensitive skin should check the concentration.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Salvia officinalis (Sage)-Derived Ingredients (PDF)
SAGE Journals — Safety Assessment of Salvia officinalis Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (2026 publication)

ingredients.name: Salvia Sclarea (Clary) Extract
ingredients.note: Clary sage (non-volatile) extract used as a skin-conditioning/toning botanical. Lower allergen exposure than the essential oil; standard cosmetic plant extract.

ingredients.name: Sambucus Nigra (Elder) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: This is an extract from elderberries (Sambucus nigra), rich in anthocyanins and polyphenols with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is widely used in both food and cosmetics and is considered safe for topical application.

sources.label: PMC – Elderberry Extracts: Chemical Composition, Safety, and Oxidative Stress Attenuation
PMC – Sambucus nigra L. ameliorates UVB-induced photoaging in skin keratinocytes

ingredients.name: Saponaria Officinalis Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Soapwort leaf extract containing natural saponins that provide gentle surfactant/cleansing and foaming action. A benign plant-derived cleanser.

ingredients.name: Sarcosine
ingredients.note: A naturally occurring amino acid derivative found in muscles and other body tissues, used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent and mild amino acid building block. It is safe as used in cosmetics when formulated to avoid conditions that could form nitrosamines (its N-nitroso derivative is a known animal carcinogen).

sources.label: CIR – Amended Safety Assessment of Sarcosines and Sarcosinates
PubMed – Final report on safety assessment of Cocoyl Sarcosine, Lauroyl Sarcosine, and related sarcosinates

ingredients.name: Sargassum Vulgare Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed (algae) extract used as a remineralizing, antioxidant skin conditioner; brown-algae ingredients reviewed as safe by CIR. Benign marine botanical.

ingredients.name: Sasa Quelpaertensis Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from a small bamboo species native to Jeju Island, South Korea, that contains antioxidants and compounds with skin-brightening and anti-inflammatory effects. It is well tolerated and used in K-beauty products for a healthy-looking complexion.

sources.label: PubMed 33297496 – Sasa quelpaertensis leaf extract ameliorates dyslipidemia
PMC 9626218 – Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities of bamboo Sasa leaf extract

ingredients.name: Saururus Chinensis Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of Saururus chinensis used as an antioxidant, soothing and skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Schinziophyton Rautanenii Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed from the wild-harvested Mongongo nut of southern Africa, this oil is exceptionally rich in alpha-eleostearic acid, linoleic acid, and vitamin E, giving it strong antioxidant and emollient properties for skin and hair. It's considered safe and non-irritating.

sources.label: ResearchGate – Mungongo cold pressed oil (Schinziophyton rautanenii): A new natural product with potential cosmetic applications
ScienceDirect – Characterization of Schinziophyton rautanenii (Manketti) nut oil rich in conjugated fatty acids and tocopherol

ingredients.name: Sclerocarya Birrea (Marula) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: A light, fast-absorbing oil pressed from the seeds of the African marula tree, high in oleic acid (~69%). Clinical studies confirm it is non-irritating, provides strong moisturizing and occlusive effects, and the CIR has reviewed it within its plant-derived fatty acid oil assessment.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety and efficacy of Sclerocarya birrea (Marula) oil: A clinical perspective
CIR – Safety Assessment of Plant-Derived Fatty Acid Oils (supplement manuscript)

ingredients.name: Sclerocarya Birrea (Marula) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A highly nourishing oil pressed from the seeds of the marula tree in southern Africa, rich in oleic acid and antioxidants; it absorbs quickly and helps hydrate skin while reducing moisture loss. Clinical studies confirm it is non-irritating and effective as a moisturizer.

sources.label: PubMed 26528587 – Safety and efficacy of Sclerocarya birrea (Marula) oil: A clinical perspective
PubMed 37515510 – Characterisation of Sclerocarya birrea (marula) seed oil

ingredients.name: Sclerocarya Birrea Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Butter derived from the seeds of the marula tree, native to southern Africa, rich in oleic acid and antioxidants. Clinical evaluation found it to be non-irritating and effective at moisturizing and hydrating skin, with a long tradition of cosmetic use.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety and efficacy of Sclerocarya birrea (Marula) oil: a clinical perspective
PMC – Anti-aging potential of extracts from Sclerocarya birrea and chemical profiling

ingredients.name: Sclerocarya Birrea Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Marula seed oil, oleic-acid-rich plant oil used as a hydrating emollient; clinically shown non-irritating and moisturizing.

ingredients.name: Sclerotium Gum
ingredients.note: Fermentation-derived polysaccharide giving smooth gels and emulsion stability. Considered safe and skin-friendly.

ingredients.name: Scutellaria Baicalensis Root Extract
ingredients.note: Baikal skullcap root extract rich in baicalin flavonoids used as an antioxidant/soothing agent; CIR found it safe in current use. Benign botanical antioxidant.

ingredients.name: Sea Buckthorn Oil
ingredients.note: Fatty-acid and carotenoid-rich repairing oil for dry/aging skin; well tolerated (can transiently tint skin orange).

ingredients.name: Sea Salt/Maris Sal/Sel Marin
ingredients.note: Naturally evaporated seawater, mainly sodium chloride with trace minerals; used as a viscosity/texture and bulking agent and mild exfoliant. Recognized as safe in cosmetics.

sources.label: PubMed: Bathing in magnesium-rich Dead Sea salt solution improves skin barrier function and reduces inflammation in atopic dry skin
PubMed: Anti-inflammatory effects of topical formulations containing sea silt and sea salt on human skin in vivo
PMC: Possibilities of Using Salt-Containing Raw Materials for Cosmetic Purposes

ingredients.name: Secale Cereale (Rye) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of rye seed used as a skin-conditioning and moisturizing botanical. Standard cosmetic ingredient with no significant safety concern.

sources.label: PMC: Unraveling the Bioactive Profile, Antioxidant and DNA Damage Protection Potential of Rye (Secale cereale) Flour

ingredients.name: Secale Cereale Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Rye (Secale cereale) seed extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Sedum Sarmentosum Extract
ingredients.note: Plant extract from stringy stonecrop used as a soothing skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Serica/Silk Powder
ingredients.note: Finely pulverized silk fibroin (CAS 9009-99-8) used as a bulking, smoothing, oil-absorbing texture agent and skin conditioner. Benign cosmetic powder.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Silk Protein Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
PMC: Silk Sericin: A Promising Sustainable Biomaterial for Biomedical and Pharmaceutical Applications
PMC: Silk Sericin-Based Electrospun Nanofibers Forming Films for Cosmetic Applications

ingredients.name: Sericin
ingredients.note: Silk-derived protein used as a humectant and conditioning agent for skin and hair. CIR safety assessment of silk protein ingredients found it low-toxicity and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Serine
ingredients.note: Skin-identical amino acid and Natural Moisturizing Factor component used as a water-binding skin conditioner. CIR assessed alpha-amino acids as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Sesamum Indicum (Sesame) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from sesame seeds containing antioxidants (sesamol, sesamin) and conditioning compounds that help soften and protect skin; the CIR found sesame-derived ingredients safe for cosmetic use, but sesame is a known food allergen, so those with sesame allergy should patch test.

sources.label: PubMed: Amended safety assessment of Sesamum indicum seed oil and related ingredients

ingredients.name: Sesamum Indicum (Sesame) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Sesame seed oil used as a conditioning emollient. Standard benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Shea Butter
ingredients.note: Rich occlusive plant butter; safe and soothing with very low allergy risk.

ingredients.name: Shorea Robusta Resin
ingredients.note: Sal-tree resin (diterpenes, triterpenoids, resin acids) used as a viscosity/structuring and skin-conditioning agent; topically considered safe with only rare irritation reports.

sources.label: PubMed: Anti-inflammatory and antipyretic activities of the ethanolic extract of Shorea robusta resin
PubMed: Wound healing activity of ethanolic extract of Shorea robusta resin
PMC: Analgesic activity of the ethanolic extract of Shorea robusta resin in experimental animals

ingredients.name: Shorea Robusta Seed Butter
ingredients.note: A plant butter pressed from the seeds of the Indian sal tree, similar in texture and fatty-acid profile to cocoa butter; it deeply moisturizes and softens skin. It is considered safe for cosmetic use and is well-tolerated by most skin types.

sources.label: ScienceDirect — Sal (Shorea robusta) seed oil: A sustainable alternative for cocoa butter

ingredients.name: Shorea Stenoptera Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Illipe seed butter used as a rich emollient/skin-conditioning fat; standard cosmetic plant butter.

ingredients.name: Silanetriol
ingredients.note: Organosilicon used as a hair- and skin-conditioning film former. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Silk Extract
ingredients.note: Silk (Bombyx mori)-derived protein extract used as a skin/hair conditioning and film-forming agent; CIR reviews silk-derived proteins as safe as used.
