ingredients.name: Silybum Marianum (Blessed Thistle) Ethyl Ester
ingredients.note: A modified ester form of silymarin (the active flavonolignan from milk thistle), used in cosmetics for its potent antioxidant and anti-aging properties; research confirms anti-acne and anti-rosacea potential. Safe for topical use.

sources.label: Green extraction of anti-aging flavonolignans from Silybum marianum for cosmetic applications – PMC
Silibinin compound from milk thistle – PubMed

ingredients.name: Silybum Marianum Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the seeds of milk thistle containing silymarin, a complex of flavonolignans with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Research shows it protects skin against UV-induced oxidative stress, and it has a low toxicity profile in topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: PubMed – Silymarin from milk thistle inhibits UV-induced oxidative stress in mouse skin
PubMed – Molecular mechanisms of inhibition of photocarcinogenesis by silymarin from milk thistle

ingredients.name: Silybum Marianum Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from milk thistle fruit that is rich in silymarin, a group of flavonoid antioxidants known for protecting cells from oxidative stress; in cosmetics it is used for anti-aging and skin-soothing benefits. It is safe as a topical ingredient with no significant irritation reported.

sources.label: PMC – Green Ultrasound-Assisted Extraction of Antioxidant Flavonolignans from Milk Thistle for Cosmetic Applications

ingredients.name: Simethicone
ingredients.note: Mixture of dimethicone and silica used as an antifoaming/skin-conditioning silicone; inert, poorly absorbed.

ingredients.name: Simmondsia Chinensis (Jojoba) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Jojoba liquid wax ester used as an emollient closely mimicking skin sebum. Widely used, well-tolerated botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Siraitia Grosvenorii Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Monk fruit extract used as a botanical antioxidant/skin-conditioning agent; standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Snail Secretion Filtrate
ingredients.note: Mollusk-derived blend of glycoproteins and hyaluronic acid used for hydration and repair; generally well tolerated, with rare allergy in shellfish/mollusk-sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acetate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of acetic acid used as a buffering/pH-adjusting agent. Considered safe and benign in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acetylated Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Acetyl-modified, more skin-adherent hyaluronate humectant; CIR concluded hyaluronates safe as used.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acrylate/Acryloyldimethyltaurate/Dimethylacrylamide Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Crosslinked acrylate copolymer used as a thickener, emulsion stabilizer and film former. Covered by CIR acryloyldimethyltaurate polymers assessment. CAS 187725-30-0.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic anionic polymer used as a rheology modifier, thickener and emulsion stabilizer. CIR-reviewed acrylate copolymer regarded as safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acrylates Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic acrylate polymer used as a thickener/gelling and film-forming agent; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard texture agent.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic crosslinked acrylate copolymer used as a rheology modifier/thickener and emulsion stabilizer. Inert high-molecular-weight polymer, not skin-penetrating.

ingredients.name: Sodium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: This is a crosslinked polymer used as a thickener and emulsion stabilizer in water-based and silicone-containing formulas. The CIR concluded it is safe; formulators are advised to minimize residual monomers using good manufacturing practices.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Acryloyldimethyltaurate Polymers as Used in Cosmetics (Final, PDF)

ingredients.name: Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: Stable, water-soluble vitamin C derivative used for brightening and antioxidant benefit. Gentle and safe, including for acne-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Sodium benzoate
ingredients.note: Common food-grade preservative; safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Benzotriazolyl Butylphenol Sulfonate
ingredients.note: A water-soluble UV absorber (peaks ~290/335 nm) used to filter UV and stabilize formulas; CIR concluded it is safe with very low dermal penetration.

ingredients.name: Sodium Bicarbonate
ingredients.note: Baking soda used as a pH adjuster/buffering agent, absorbent and mild abrasive; CIR concluded it is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Sodium C14-16 Olefin Sulfonate
ingredients.note: Anionic surfactant/foaming cleanser. CIR concluded safe in rinse-off and up to 2% in leave-on; can irritate at high concentrations.

ingredients.name: Sodium Caproyl Methyltaurate
ingredients.note: Mild taurate anionic surfactant used as a gentle cleansing agent with low skin/eye irritation. CAS 20461-70-5. Ruled safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl Taurate Amides and Taurate Salts as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Carbomer
ingredients.note: Pre-neutralized sodium salt of carbomer that gels without added base. Inert and non-irritating like carbomer.

ingredients.name: Sodium Carbonate
ingredients.note: Alkaline salt used as a pH buffer/adjuster; CIR concluded carbonate salts are safe as used, though concentrated solutions are irritating. Standard formulation aid.

ingredients.name: Sodium Carboxymethyl Beta-Glucan
ingredients.note: Water-soluble beta-glucan derivative acting as a humectant, film-former, and soothing agent; low-hazard.

sources.label: PubMed: Exploring the Properties and Application Potential of beta-Glucan in Skin Care
PubMed: Skin health promotion effects of natural beta-glucan derived from cereals and microorganisms

ingredients.name: Sodium Carboxymethyl Starch
ingredients.note: Modified starch derivative used as a thickener/viscosity controller and absorbent. Benign standard texture agent.

ingredients.name: Sodium Chloride
ingredients.note: Common salt used as viscosity controller/thickener; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Chondroitin Sulfate
ingredients.note: Glycosaminoglycan sodium salt used as a humectant and hydrating, soothing skin-conditioning agent. Generally well tolerated.

sources.label: PubChem: Chondroitin Sulfate B Sodium Salt (CID 53420572)
PMC: Novel Cosmetic Ingredient CS-AA Polyion Complex and Skin Moisturizing Effect

ingredients.name: Sodium Citrate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of citric acid used as a buffering agent and mild chelator. Safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Sodium Coco PG-Dimonium Chloride Phosphate
aliases: coco PG-dimonium chloride phosphate sodium salt
ingredients.note: A phospholipid-like quaternary ammonium surfactant derived from coconut oil, used in hair and skin cleansing products for mild cleansing and conditioning in one step. It is a recognized ingredient in cosmetic formulations; no major safety concerns have been reported at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Coco-Sulfate
ingredients.note: Coconut-derived sulfate blend chemically close to SLS and similarly capable of being drying or irritating with heavy use. Non-ethoxylated, so no 1,4-dioxane concern, and not a toxin.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoamphoacetate
ingredients.note: Mild amphoteric co-surfactant used to reduce irritation and boost foam in gentle cleansers. Well tolerated with low sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoate
ingredients.note: Sodium salts of coconut fatty acids (saponified coconut oil); an anionic surfactant used as the primary cleansing agent in soaps and bath products. Can be mildly alkaline.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Alaninate
ingredients.note: Mild amino-acid (alanine + coconut) anionic surfactant used for gentle, low-irritation cleansing and foaming. Biodegradable and well tolerated, including on sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed: Sodium cocoyl alaninate as a bio-based surfactant – properties
CIR: Safety Assessment of Amino Acid Alkyl Amides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Amino Acids
ingredients.note: A mild, amino acid-based surfactant derived from coconut oil and plant amino acids that gently cleanses without stripping the skin's natural oils; it is often used in sensitive-skin and baby cleansers. The CIR panel's assessment of acylated amino acid surfactants supports its safety.

sources.label: CIR Final Report on Safety Assessment of Sodium Cocoyl amino acids
PMC12446884 – Clinical Evaluation of Sodium Cocoyl Apple Amino Acids Cleansing Lotion

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Apple Amino Acids
ingredients.note: A mild amino-acid-based anionic surfactant made from coconut fatty acids and apple-derived amino acids, used as a gentle cleansing/foaming agent. Considered mild and skin-friendly.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Coconut- and glutamic-acid-derived mild surfactant widely used in gentle, sulfate-free cleansers. Low irritancy and good skin compatibility.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Glycinate
ingredients.note: Mild glycine-based surfactant that produces soft foam at near-skin pH, common in low-irritation facial cleansers. Considered gentle and safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate
ingredients.note: Very mild anionic surfactant derived from coconut, prized for creamy lather and low irritancy in syndet bars and gentle cleansers. Considered safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Sodium Dehydroacetate
ingredients.note: Benign, widely used preservative authorized in the EU up to 0.6% (as acid). Low irritation and sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Sodium Dilauramidoglutamide Lysine
ingredients.note: Amino-acid-derived lipid (lauroyl glutamate/lysine) used as a skin-barrier-mimicking emollient and texture stabilizer. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: PubMed: Skin accumulation and penetration of a hydrophilic compound by sodium dilauramidoglutamide lysine gemini surfactant

ingredients.name: Sodium DNA
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of DNA used as a skin-conditioning/repair biopolymer (related to PDRN); listed in EU CosIng.

ingredients.name: Sodium Formate
ingredients.note: Sodium formate is the sodium salt of formic acid used in cosmetics as a mild preservative and pH adjuster, typically at concentrations up to 0.34%. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in the present practices of use and concentration in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Formic Acid and Sodium Formate as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Gluconate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of gluconic acid used as a chelating/buffering and humectant agent; low-hazard standard additive.

ingredients.name: Sodium Glutamate
ingredients.note: The sodium salt of the amino acid glutamic acid, used in cosmetics as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent to attract and retain moisture. The CIR Panel considers amino acids like sodium glutamate safe for topical use in cosmetics at typical concentrations.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of α-Amino Acids as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Sodium Glycerophosphate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of glycerophosphoric acid used as a buffering/pH-adjusting and skin-conditioning agent, also common in oral care. Mild and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Skin-identical hydrator; safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Cross-linked hyaluronate that forms a hydrating film at the surface; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sodium Hydrolyzed Potato Starch Dodecenylsuccinate
aliases: Hydrolyzed Potato Starch Dodecenylsuccinate Sodium Salt
ingredients.note: A modified potato starch derivative that acts as a mild surfactant and foam booster in cleansers; it helps water and oil mix evenly and is rated low hazard by cosmetic safety databases with minimal irritation risk.

sources.label: PMC: Safety Assessment of Starch Nanoparticles as an Emulsifier in Human Skin Cells and 3D Skin

ingredients.name: Sodium Hydroxide
ingredients.note: Strong base used in trace amounts to neutralize acids and adjust pH. Corrosive as a raw material, but in finished products it is fully reacted and present only as a residual pH adjuster, so the product itself is safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Hydroxypropylsulfonate Laurylglucoside Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A mildly-surfactant polymer derived from natural glucose that functions as a gentle cleanser and skin conditioner in wash-off products. It is based on alkyl polyglucoside chemistry, known for very low irritation potential even on sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Sodium Isethionate
ingredients.note: Mild water-soluble surfactant/cleansing agent used in syndet bars and cleansers. CIR concluded isethionate salts are safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lactate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of lactic acid used as a buffer and humectant (NMF component). Safe and skin-conditioning.

ingredients.name: Sodium Laurate
ingredients.note: The sodium salt of lauric acid (a fatty acid from coconut oil) that acts as a soap and cleanser, producing a nice lather; it is a common ingredient in bar soaps and cleansers. It is generally safe, though like most soaps it can dry out skin if used frequently.

ingredients.name: Sodium Laureth-12 Sulfate
ingredients.note: Highly ethoxylated anionic sulfate surfactant used as a foaming cleanser. Can irritate at high levels but is not a sensitizer; ensure low 1,4-dioxane via purified grades.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroamphoacetate
ingredients.note: Mild amphoteric surfactant; CIR Expert Panel concluded amphoacetates are safe as used in cosmetics. Standard gentle cleanser.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Aspartate
ingredients.note: Amino-acid (aspartic acid) based anionic surfactant used as a mild, sulfate-free cleansing and conditioning agent. Gentle alternative to sulfates.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Glutamic-acid-derived anionic surfactant known for very mild, skin-friendly cleansing at low pH. Well tolerated with low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Hydrolyzed Silk
ingredients.note: Lauroyl-acylated hydrolyzed silk protein used as a mild conditioning/cleansing agent for skin and hair. CIR found hydrolyzed silk and silk proteins safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Isethionate
ingredients.note: Mild isethionate cleansing surfactant similar to SCI, common in syndet bars and facial cleansers. Low irritation and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Lactylate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived anionic surfactant and emulsifier; CIR-reviewed as safe, with low irritation and non-sensitizing at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate
ingredients.note: Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate is a mild anionic surfactant derived from coconut; it cleans effectively while being gentler on skin than traditional sulfates, making it a popular ingredient in sensitive-skin cleansers and bars. The CIR assessment for isethionate surfactants supports its safety.

sources.label: CIR – Amended Safety Assessment of Isethionate Salts as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Methylaminopropionate
ingredients.note: Amphoteric/amino-acid-type anionic surfactant used as a mild cleansing agent. Standard gentle surfactant.

sources.label: PubMed: Allergic contact dermatitis due to sodium lauroyl methylaminopropionate in shampoo
PubMed: Allergic contact dermatitis induced by sodium N-methyl-N-(1-oxododecyl)-beta-alaninate in shampoo

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Oat Amino Acids
ingredients.note: Mild oat-derived amino-acid anionic surfactant used as a gentle cleanser. Benign and suited to sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubChem: Sodium Lauroyl Oat Amino Acids
CIR: Safety Assessment of Amino Acid Alkyl Amides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauroyl Sarcosinate
ingredients.note: Gentle amino-acid-derived surfactant used as a mild foaming agent and in toothpaste. Low irritation; not a meaningful nitrosamine concern in modern grades.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Glycol Carboxylate
ingredients.note: Mild anionic surfactant used as a high-foaming, low-irritation cleansing agent in washes and shampoos. Stable across a wide pH range.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Phosphate
ingredients.note: Anionic phosphate-ester surfactant used as a mild cleanser and emulsifier. Standard cosmetic surfactant.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl Phosphates as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
ingredients.note: Effective foaming cleanser and a well-known skin/eye irritant that can be drying, especially at high concentration or with prolonged contact. It is an irritant, not a toxin or endocrine disruptor, and is not absorbed to cause systemic harm.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfoacetate
ingredients.note: A mild, plant-derived surfactant (cleanser/foaming agent) commonly used in shampoos, body washes, and bath products. Despite sounding similar to the harsher SLS, it is larger in molecular size and considerably gentler on skin; the CIR has confirmed it as safe.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Sodium Lauryl Sulfoacetate as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Levulinate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of levulinic acid used as a gentle antimicrobial preservative and skin conditioner. CIR concluded it is safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Sodium Magnesium Silicate
ingredients.note: A synthetic clay mineral used to thicken and stabilize cosmetic formulas. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in cosmetics; the main precaution is to avoid inhaling powder forms.

sources.label: PubMed 12851164 – CIR Final report on safety assessment of silicates including sodium magnesium silicate

ingredients.name: Sodium Mannose Phosphate
ingredients.note: A sodium salt of mannose phosphate used as an active anti-aging ingredient that helps reorganize collagen in the dermis and improve skin's mechanical properties; clinical studies show reduction in wrinkle depth. Safe for topical use.

sources.label: Mannose-6-phosphate complex and improvement in biomechanical properties of skin – PMC

ingredients.name: Sodium Metaphosphate
ingredients.note: Phosphate salt serving as pH buffer, chelating agent and oral-care agent; covered by CIR phosphoric acid/simple salts assessment.

ingredients.name: Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Mild taurine-based anionic surfactant producing dense foam, common in gentle facial cleansers. Low irritation and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Methyl Oleoyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Taurine-derived mild surfactant used as a co-surfactant for creamy lather in cleansers. Gentle with low irritancy.

ingredients.name: Sodium Methyl Stearoyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Sodium methyl stearoyl taurate is a mild, biodegradable surfactant derived from stearic acid and taurine, used in cleansers and hair care products for its gentle cleansing action. The CIR Expert Panel found taurate-class surfactants, including this one, to be safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl Taurate Amides and Taurate Salts as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium Myristoyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Mild amino-acid-derived anionic surfactant used as a gentle cleanser and emulsifier. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Palmitate
ingredients.note: Soap-type fatty-acid salt used as cleansing surfactant/emulsifier in rinse-off products; CIR concluded fatty acid soaps safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Palmoyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Amino-acid (glutamate) derived anionic surfactant used as a mild cleanser. Gentle, benign cosmetic surfactant.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Amino Acid Alkyl Amides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Sodium PCA
ingredients.note: Natural moisturizing factor component and strong humectant; well tolerated and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Phosphate
ingredients.note: Inorganic salt used as a pH buffer/adjuster; widely used and recognized as safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Phytate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived (rice/grain) chelator used as a natural EDTA alternative. Considered safe and biodegradable.

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyacrylate
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight anionic polymer used as a thickener and water absorber. Safe as used; residual acrylic acid monomer is limited in cosmetic grades.

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate polymer used as a rheology modifier/thickener and emulsion stabilizer; EWG low-hazard and well tolerated.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Acryloyldimethyltaurate Polymers

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyglutamate
ingredients.note: Salt form of polyglutamic acid used as a water-binding humectant; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sodium Stearate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of stearic acid (a soap) used as emulsifier, thickener and cleansing agent. CIR assessed stearate salts as safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Stearoyl Glutamate
ingredients.note: Amino-acid-derived (glutamate) mild anionic emulsifier/surfactant used in gentle cleansers and emulsions. Standard well-tolerated ingredient.

ingredients.name: Sodium Stearoyl Lactylate
ingredients.note: A sodium salt of stearoyl lactic acid used as a gentle emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent in creams and lotions. The CIR found alkyl lactyl lactate salts safe in cosmetics at current use levels; it is also an approved food additive, reflecting its excellent safety profile.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Alkanoyl Lactyl Lactate Salts as Used in Cosmetics (Final)

ingredients.name: Sodium Succinate
ingredients.note: Sodium salt of succinic acid used as a buffering/pH-adjusting agent; an endogenous metabolite of low toxicity.

ingredients.name: Sodium Sulfate
ingredients.note: An inorganic salt used in cosmetics primarily as a viscosity-adjusting and texture-building agent (especially in bath powders and bath salts) and sometimes as a filler or diluent. The CIR Expert Panel concluded sodium sulfate is safe in rinse-off products and safe at up to 1% in leave-on formulas.

sources.label: CIR – Final Amended Safety Assessment of Sodium Sulfate as Used in Cosmetics
CIR Supplemental Doc – Amended Safety Assessment of Sodium Sulfate

ingredients.name: Sodium Surfactin
ingredients.note: Cyclic lipopeptide biosurfactant fermented from Bacillus subtilis, used as a mild emulsifier/cleanser and penetration aid; shown compatible with human skin.

ingredients.name: Sodium Thiosulfate
ingredients.note: An inorganic compound used in cosmetics and topical pharmaceuticals as an antioxidant and reducing agent to protect formulas from oxidation; at low concentrations it is well-tolerated on skin. It is also used medically as an antidote and is generally considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sodium Tocopheryl Phosphate
ingredients.note: Water-soluble vitamin E (tocopherol) phosphate derivative used as an antioxidant; tocopherols and related phosphate derivatives assessed safe by CIR. Benign.

ingredients.name: Solanum Lycopersicum (Tomato) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from ripe tomatoes that provides lycopene, vitamin C, and other antioxidants that help protect against UV-related skin aging and brighten skin tone; generally safe, though those with nightshade sensitivities should patch test.

sources.label: PMC: Aqueous Extract of Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) and skin properties

ingredients.name: Solanum Melongena (Eggplant) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Eggplant fruit extract used as a conditioning/antioxidant botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Soluble Collagen
ingredients.note: Native water-soluble collagen used as a film-forming humectant and skin conditioner. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Soluble Elastin
ingredients.note: Water-soluble elastin protein used as a skin/hair conditioning film former; precautionary because animal/bovine-sourced grades raise BSE sourcing considerations per CIR.

ingredients.name: Soluble Proteoglycan
ingredients.note: Proteoglycan (glycosaminoglycan-protein) used as a moisturizing/skin-conditioning humectant. Benign standard biological moisturizer.

ingredients.name: Sophora Angustifolia Root Extract
ingredients.note: Botanical root extract used as a brightening/skin-conditioning active that inhibits tyrosinase and melanin production. Generally well tolerated in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Sophora Japonica Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Pagoda tree flower extract rich in rutin and quercetin flavonoids used as an antioxidant, soothing, brightening botanical.

sources.label: PubMed: Local and traditional uses, phytochemistry, and pharmacology of Sophora japonica L.: A review
PMC: Green method to extract rutin from Sophora japonica
MDPI Plants: Effects of Roasting on Antibacterial and Antioxidant Properties of Sophora japonica Buds

ingredients.name: Sorbeth-30 Tetraoleate
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated sorbitol tetraoleate ester used as a nonionic emulsifier/surfactant and emollient. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Sorbic Acid
ingredients.note: Mild food-grade preservative permitted in the EU up to 0.6%. Can cause minor transient irritation but is rarely a true allergen.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Isostearate
ingredients.note: Sorbitan fatty-acid ester used as a nonionic emulsifier; CIR-reviewed sorbitan esters considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Laurate
ingredients.note: A sorbitan ester emulsifier derived from sorbitol and lauric acid (a fatty acid in coconut oil); it helps blend water and oil phases in cosmetics. It is not mutagenic and shows minimal skin irritation; the CIR has found it safe.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Sorbitan Esters as Used in Cosmetics (2014 FAR)

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Oleate
ingredients.note: Sorbitan ester of oleic acid used as a water-in-oil co-emulsifier. Non-ethoxylated and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Olivate
ingredients.note: Olive-derived sorbitan ester co-emulsifier paired with cetearyl olivate. Mild and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Palmitate
ingredients.note: A nonionic sorbitan-ester emulsifier (Span 40) used to stabilize oil-in-water emulsions. Standard, low-hazard formulation aid.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety Assessment of Sorbitan Esters as Used in Cosmetics
CIR: Safety Assessment of Sorbitan Esters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Sesquiisostearate
ingredients.note: A sorbitan ester emulsifier made from sorbitol and isostearic acid (a branched vegetable-derived fatty acid) that helps blend water and oil in creams and lotions. The CIR Panel found sorbitan esters including sorbitan sesquiisostearate non-irritating and safe at up to 5% in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Sorbitan Esters as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Stearate
ingredients.note: Non-ethoxylated sorbitol/stearic acid ester used as a low-HLB co-emulsifier. Mild and broadly considered safe.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Tristearate
ingredients.note: Sorbitan-stearic-acid ester used as W/O emulsifier and stabilizer. CIR sorbitan esters assessment supports cosmetic safety.

ingredients.name: Sorbitol
ingredients.note: Sugar-alcohol humectant similar to glycerin; benign and widely used.

ingredients.name: Sorghum Bicolor Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: An antioxidant-rich extract from sorghum leaves and stems that helps protect skin from free-radical damage and supports moisture retention. It is considered safe and well tolerated at cosmetic use concentrations.

sources.label: The antiwrinkle and antimelanogenic effects of the nonedible part of Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench and their augmentation by fermentation — PubMed
West African Sorghum bicolor Leaf Sheaths Have Anti-Inflammatory and Immune-Modulating Properties In Vitro — PMC

ingredients.name: Soybean Glycerides
ingredients.note: A mixture of mono-, di-, and triglycerides derived from soybean oil that functions as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent, helping skin feel soft and smooth. It is considered safe for topical use, though people with soy allergies should be aware of its origin.

sources.label: PMC – Enzymatic Deglycosylation and Lipophilization of Soy Glycosides for Cosmetic Applications

ingredients.name: Sphingolipids
ingredients.note: Skin-identical lipid class used as barrier-supporting emollients and skin conditioners. No significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Sphingomonas Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: Bacterial ferment extract rich in sphingolipids (ceramide precursors); used as skin-conditioning/anti-aging viscosity controller.

ingredients.name: Spinacia Oleracea (Spinach) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from spinach leaves rich in vitamins A, C, and K, beta-carotene, and chlorophyll; used in cosmetics for antioxidant, anti-aging, and skin-soothing effects. Generally safe and well-tolerated.

sources.label: Extraction of functional ingredients from spinach using liquid solvent and supercritical CO2 – PubMed

ingredients.name: Spiraea Ulmaria Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Meadowsweet flower extract is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and natural salicylates; it is used in cosmetics for its anti-inflammatory, tonic, and mildly antiseptic properties, particularly suitable for sensitive skin. It is generally considered safe for topical use.

sources.label: EU CosIng Ingredient: SPIRAEA ULMARIA EXTRACT — European Commission
DNA-Protective, Antioxidant and Anti-Carcinogenic Potential of Meadowsweet (Filipendula ulmaria) Dry Tincture — PMC

ingredients.name: Spirulina Maxima Extract
ingredients.note: Spirulina maxima is a blue-green microalgae packed with proteins, phycobiliproteins, and antioxidants that support skin hydration, anti-aging, and photoprotection. Clinical studies show spirulina-containing cosmetics improve skin hydration and the lipid barrier with no significant safety concerns reported.

sources.label: Spirulina as a Key Ingredient in the Evolution of Eco-Friendly Cosmetics – PMC
Spirulina platensis in cosmetic formulations – PubMed

ingredients.name: Spirulina Platensis Extract
ingredients.note: Blue-green microalga extract rich in phycocyanin/carotenoids used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning active; benign.

ingredients.name: Spondias Mombin (Caja) Pulp Extract
ingredients.note: Tropical caja fruit pulp extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign fruit-derived extract.

ingredients.name: Squalane
ingredients.note: Lightweight, stable emollient; safe (note: 'squalane' the hydrogenated form, not unstable 'squalene').

ingredients.name: Sr-Hydrozoan Polypeptide-1
ingredients.note: This is a recombinant protein (polypeptide) derived from hydrozoan marine organisms, used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning active ingredient often marketed for anti-aging or firming effects. It is a biotech-derived ingredient with a low likelihood of toxicity at cosmetic use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Sr-Spider Polypeptide-1
ingredients.note: A biotechnology-derived protein that mimics the amino acid sequences of spider dragline silk, produced by fermentation in E. coli. It is used as a film-forming agent that may improve skin firmness; it is generally considered safe, but people allergic to insect or spider proteins should exercise caution.

sources.label: SVX Spider Silk-Inspired Biopolymer and Enhanced Cosmetics Efficacy (contains Sr-Spider Polypeptide-1) — MDPI Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Stearalkonium Hectorite
ingredients.note: Quaternized hectorite clay used as a suspending/rheology agent in anhydrous systems. CIR found it safe, not a skin/eye irritant or mutagen, with low nitrosamine risk.

ingredients.name: Steareth-8
ingredients.note: A PEG-derived emulsifier made from stearyl alcohol and 8 units of ethylene oxide, used to help blend oil and water phases in creams and lotions. The CIR has assessed the steareth family as safe in cosmetics; they are non-irritating and non-sensitizing at typical use levels.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)
CIR – Steareth-30 ingredient page (representative steareth series)

ingredients.name: Stearic Acid
ingredients.note: Common fatty acid emulsifier and thickener; benign and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Stearoxymethicone/Dimethicone Copolymer
ingredients.note: Alkyl-modified silicone copolymer used as an emollient, film former and skin/hair conditioning agent providing a smooth feel. Benign.

ingredients.name: Stearoyl Inulin
ingredients.note: A natural inulin (plant fiber from chicory) modified with stearic acid to act as an emulsifier in water-free or low-water formulas. It is gentle, biodegradable, and considered safe.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Plant Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics (2014 SLR)

ingredients.name: Stearyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Fatty alcohol used to thicken and soften formulas; benign and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Behenate
ingredients.note: A waxy ester of stearyl alcohol and behenic acid used as an emollient and occlusive agent that softens skin and helps lock in moisture. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in cosmetics based on its chemical similarity to other reviewed stearyl esters.

sources.label: Sagepub – CIR Safety Assessment of Stearyl Heptanoate and Related Stearyl Alkanoates (2012)
PubMed 23064772 – Safety assessment of stearyl heptanoate and related stearyl alkanoates

ingredients.name: Stearyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Stearyl-modified silicone used as an emollient, film former and hair conditioner; CIR-assessed safe as used.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Glycyrrhetinate
ingredients.note: Stearyl ester of licorice-derived glycyrrhetinic acid used as an anti-inflammatory, soothing, antioxidant agent. CIR concluded glycyrrhetinate ingredients are safe. CAS 13832-70-7.

sources.label: PubMed: Final report on safety assessment of Glycyrrhetinic Acid and related compounds including Stearyl Glycyrrhetinate
PubMed: Effects of oils and emulsifiers on the skin penetration of stearyl glycyrrhetinate

ingredients.name: Stearyl Heptanoate
ingredients.note: An ester of stearyl alcohol and heptanoic acid used as a skin-conditioning emollient that provides a smooth, non-greasy feel; the CIR Expert Panel has confirmed it is safe for use in cosmetics. It is commonly found in moisturizers and hair products.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Stearate
ingredients.note: A waxy ester of stearic acid and stearyl alcohol that gives creams and lipsticks a rich, luxurious texture while softening skin. It is considered safe for cosmetic use; the CIR Expert Panel noted its safety profile is equivalent to related stearate esters.

ingredients.name: Stevia Rebaudiana Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the stevia plant's leaves and stems containing steviol glycosides (natural sweeteners) and antioxidants; in cosmetics it is used for its skin-soothing and antioxidant properties. It is considered safe for topical use and is listed in the EU CosIng database.

sources.label: EU CosIng – STEVIA REBAUDIANA LEAF/STEM EXTRACT
PMC – Stevia Rebaudiana Leaf Extracts as a Multifunctional Source of Natural Antioxidants

ingredients.name: Stevioside
ingredients.note: Stevia-derived glycoside used as a sweetener and antioxidant/anti-inflammatory conditioning agent (chiefly in oral care). JECFA found no safety concern at intake levels; not carcinogenic.

ingredients.name: Streptococcus Thermophilus Ferment
ingredients.note: A probiotic-derived ferment used as a humectant/skin-conditioning agent shown to raise stratum-corneum ceramide levels. Benign, well-tolerated ingredient.

ingredients.name: Styrax Japonicus Branch/Fruit/Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Derived from the Japanese snowbell tree, this extract functions as a skin-conditioning agent that helps maintain moisture balance and smooth skin texture. It's a niche botanical ingredient with no documented safety concerns at cosmetic concentrations.

sources.label: Triterpenoid from Styrax japonica and effects on MMP-1 / type 1 procollagen in UV-irradiated human skin fibroblasts – PubMed 16204966

ingredients.name: Styrene Isoprene Styrene Block Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic rubber-based polymer used as a pressure-sensitive adhesive in patch-type cosmetics (e.g., pore strips, eye patches) and as a film-forming agent in long-wearing makeup. It has a low systemic absorption profile and is considered safe for its intended skin contact applications.

sources.label: PubMed – Design and evaluation of a monolithic drug-in-adhesive patch based on styrene-isoprene-styrene block copolymer

ingredients.name: Styrene/Acrylates Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic film-forming polymer used in color cosmetics and water-resistant formulas; large molecule that does not penetrate skin. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Styrene/Acrylates/Ammonium Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic film-forming/opacifying acrylic copolymer (often as latex microspheres) used for texture and sensory effects. Inert high-MW polymer, not skin-penetrating.

ingredients.name: Succinic Acid
ingredients.note: Mild dicarboxylic acid used for blemishes, oil control, and pH adjustment. Well tolerated at cosmetic levels with low irritation risk.

ingredients.name: Sucrose
ingredients.note: Common sugar used as a humectant and occasional physical exfoliant. Benign, food-grade ingredient.

ingredients.name: Sucrose Acetate Isobutyrate
ingredients.note: Sucrose ester used as a film-former, plasticizer and viscosity modifier. Stable, odorless; standard texture/stabilizing ingredient.

sources.label: PubMed: Sucrose acetate isobutyrate (SAIB): historical aspects and review of toxicity studies
PubMed: Oral toxicity and carcinogenicity studies of sucrose acetate isobutyrate (SAIB)

ingredients.name: Sucrose Cocoate
ingredients.note: Sucrose ester of coconut fatty acids; a mild nonionic surfactant, emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Gentle and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Sucrose Distearate
ingredients.note: Sucrose ester of stearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Benign sugar-based cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Sucrose Palmitate
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived nonionic emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Mild and well tolerated in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Sucrose Stearate
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived nonionic ester used as a mild emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Sunflower Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Linoleic-acid-rich, barrier-supporting emollient; safe and widely used.

ingredients.name: Superoxide Dismutase
ingredients.note: Enzymatic antioxidant that neutralizes superoxide radicals in the skin. Safe and well tolerated in topical formulations.

ingredients.name: Sweet Almond Oil
ingredients.note: Gentle, well-tolerated emollient oil; safe except for those with tree-nut allergies.

ingredients.name: Swertia Japonica Extract
ingredients.note: Swertia herb extract used as a skin/scalp-conditioning botanical (microcirculation, hair-root support); benign with rare individual sensitivity.

sources.label: PubMed: Effects of Swertia japonica extract and its main compound swertiamarin
PMC: Swertiajaponin inhibits skin pigmentation by dual mechanisms to suppress tyrosinase
PubMed: Anticholinergic action of Swertia japonica and an active constituent

ingredients.name: Synthetic Beeswax
ingredients.note: Synthetic ester/wax blend mimicking beeswax, used as a thickener and structuring agent. Standard texture ingredient with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Synthetic Fluorphlogopite
ingredients.note: Lab-made mica alternative used as a smooth, high-purity pearlescent filler; inert and free of the natural-mica contamination concerns.

ingredients.name: Synthetic Sapphire
ingredients.note: Crystalline aluminum oxide used as a bulking, opacifying or gentle mechanical exfoliant powder. Chemically inert and classified low-risk.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety Assessment of Alumina and Aluminum Hydroxide as Used in Cosmetics
CIR: Safety Assessment of Alumina and Aluminum Hydroxide as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Synthetic Wax
ingredients.note: Inert hydrocarbon wax used as a structuring/viscosity agent; benign and non-penetrating.

ingredients.name: Syringa Vulgaris (Lilac) Extract
ingredients.note: Lilac extract is derived from the flowers of Syringa vulgaris and is rich in phenylpropanoids (like acteoside) and oleuropein, giving it antioxidant and skin-toning properties. Research confirms strong antioxidant activity and cytotoxic effects only at concentrations far above typical cosmetic use.

sources.label: Chemical Profile, Cytotoxic Activity and Oxidative Stress Reduction of Syringa vulgaris Extracts – PubMed
Characterization of antioxidant phenolics in Syringa vulgaris flowers and fruits – PubMed

ingredients.name: T-Butyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Tertiary alcohol used as solvent, denaturant and perfume carrier at low cosmetic levels. CIR concluded safe as used.

ingredients.name: Tamarindus Indica Seed Gum
ingredients.note: Natural polysaccharide used as thickener/gelling and emulsion stabilizer; standard plant gum rheology modifier.

ingredients.name: Tapioca Starch
ingredients.note: Cassava-derived starch used as an absorbent, thickener and texturizing/mattifying agent; CIR reviews starch ingredients as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Taraxacum Officinale (Dandelion) Rhizome/Root Extract
ingredients.note: Antioxidant-rich dandelion root extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Non-sensitizing for most, though Asteraceae-family allergy is possible; patch testing advised.

ingredients.name: Tartaric Acid
ingredients.note: Grape-derived AHA used for exfoliation and as a pH buffer. Can increase UV sensitivity and irritate sensitive skin at high levels.

ingredients.name: Tasmannia Lanceolata Fruit/Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the Australian mountain pepper plant, containing polygodial and other antioxidant polyphenols with strong anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties. A clinical study showed it can visibly improve stretch marks, and safety evaluations in zebrafish embryo models found no significant toxicity.

sources.label: PubMed – Tasmannia lanceolata leaf extract alleviates stretch marks
PMC – Phytochemical and safety evaluations of finger lime, mountain pepper, and tamarind in zebrafish embryos

ingredients.name: Terminalia Ferdinandiana Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Kakadu plum fruit extract exceptionally rich in vitamin C and phenolics. Used as a brightening antioxidant botanical; benign.

sources.label: PubMed: Chemical and Nutritional Composition of Terminalia ferdinandiana (Kakadu Plum) Kernels
PMC: Antioxidant Rich Extracts of Terminalia ferdinandiana Inhibit the Growth of Foodborne Bacteria
MDPI: Kakadu Plum (Terminalia Ferdinandiana) — A Native Australian Fruit with Functional Properties

ingredients.name: Terminalia Ferdinandiana Seed Oil
ingredients.note: An oil pressed from the seeds of the Australian Kakadu plum, which is one of the world's richest sources of vitamin C; the oil is high in linoleic and oleic acids and has antioxidant properties. It is a nourishing, gentle emollient.

sources.label: PubMed – Chemical and nutritional composition of Terminalia ferdinandiana kernels (2018)
PMC – Chemical and nutritional composition of Terminalia ferdinandiana kernels (PMC full text, 2018)

ingredients.name: Tetraacetylphytosphingosine
ingredients.note: A stable, acetylated form of phytosphingosine (a ceramide precursor naturally found in skin) that helps reinforce the skin barrier and has antibacterial properties useful against acne. It converts to phytosphingosine in skin, replenishing essential lipids.

sources.label: PubMed 37401590 – Fermentative production of tetraacetyl phytosphingosine: a review

ingredients.name: Tetradecane
ingredients.note: Saturated C14 straight-chain hydrocarbon used as an emollient/solvent in cosmetics. Inert benign alkane.

ingredients.name: Tetradecene
ingredients.note: A 14-carbon alpha-olefin hydrocarbon used in cosmetics as a solvent and emollient helper to improve product texture and spreadability. No significant safety concerns have been identified at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Tetrahexyldecyl Ascorbate
ingredients.note: Oil-soluble, stable vitamin C ester valued for penetration and antioxidant brightening. Safe and non-irritating in formulas.

ingredients.name: Tetrahydrobisdemethoxydiferuloylmethane
ingredients.note: A reduced curcuminoid derived from turmeric with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that is more stable in cosmetic formulas than curcumin itself; helps fade discoloration and soothe irritation, and is considered safe at typical use levels (0.02–1%).

sources.label: PMC: Tetrahydrocurcumin as a Melanogenesis Inhibitor in Melanoma Cell Lines

ingredients.name: Tetrahydrocurcumin
ingredients.note: Colorless curcumin derivative used as an antioxidant and brightener. Gentle and well tolerated at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Tetrahydrodemethoxydiferuloylmethane
ingredients.note: This is a colorless, stable curcuminoid (related to turmeric's active ingredient) that acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, helping to visibly calm redness, fade discoloration, and protect against UV-related damage — without the yellow stain of regular curcumin. It's considered safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: PubMed – Curcumin: a novel treatment for skin-related disorders
PMC – Tetrahydrocurcumin (THC) as a Melanogenesis Inhibitor in Melanoma Cell Lines

ingredients.name: Tetrahydrodiferuloylmethane
ingredients.note: The fully saturated, colorless form of curcumin (from turmeric), used in cosmetics for its potent antioxidant and skin-brightening properties without the yellow staining. It is more stable than curcumin and research supports good safety and efficacy in topical anti-aging and anti-inflammatory formulations.

sources.label: PMC – Tetrahydrocurcumin lipid nanoparticle gel promotes penetration into deeper skin layers and alleviates atopic dermatitis

ingredients.name: Tetrahydroxypropyl Ethylenediamine
ingredients.note: Tetrahydroxypropyl ethylenediamine (THPE) is a polyol amine used in cosmetics as a pH adjuster, chelating agent, and anti-aging active — it works by triggering keratinocyte contraction to visibly firm skin within minutes. Repeat insult patch testing in 220 subjects showed no irritation or sensitization, and at up to 0.5% it is considered safe.

sources.label: Clinical evidence for the activity of THPE, a new anti-aging cosmetic – PubMed

ingredients.name: Tetrakis Methane
ingredients.note: This is a large synthetic antioxidant molecule (trade names Irganox 1010 / Tinogard TT) used at very low levels (up to 0.8%) in cosmetics to stabilize formulas and prevent rancidity. Its high molecular weight means it does not significantly absorb through skin, and long-term feeding studies found no carcinogenic effect.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Pentaerythrityl Tetra-Di-t-Butyl Hydroxyhydrocinnamate (Final, PDF)

ingredients.name: Tetrapeptide-4
ingredients.note: Synthetic signal peptide marketed to support collagen/elastin and reduce wrinkle appearance. Used at trace levels and regarded as benign skin-conditioning agent.

sources.label: MDPI Cosmetics: Topical Peptide Treatments with Effective Anti-Aging Results
PubMed: Prediction, screening and characterization of novel bioactive tetrapeptide matrikines for skin rejuvenation

ingredients.name: Tetrasodium EDTA
ingredients.note: Alkaline tetrasodium salt of EDTA used as a metal chelator. Safe at typical low use levels; mainly an environmental persistence concern.

ingredients.name: Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate
ingredients.note: Biodegradable amino-acid-derived chelating agent that binds metal ions and boosts preservation. CIR (2021) concluded it is safe as used; not a skin sensitizer.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Beta-Alanine Diacetic Acid and Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate (Final Report PDF)
PubMed: Safety Assessment of Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate

ingredients.name: Tetrasodium Pyrophosphate
ingredients.note: A sodium salt of pyrophosphoric acid used in cosmetics as a chelating agent, pH buffer, and texture modifier (especially in toothpastes for tartar control). The CIR reviewed phosphoric acid salts in cosmetics and finds them safe at use levels; at very high concentrations it can be irritating.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Phosphoric Acid and Its Salts as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Tetrasodium Tetracarboxymethyl Naringeninchalcone
ingredients.note: Water-soluble stabilized naringenin chalcone derivative used as an anti-redness, anti-inflammatory, soothing skin-conditioning active.

sources.label: PubMed: Anti-allergic activity of naringenin chalcone from a tomato skin extract
PubMed: In Vivo Anti-inflammatory and Antiallergic Activity of Naringenin Chalcone in Mice

ingredients.name: Theobroma Cacao (Cocoa) Extract
ingredients.note: Cocoa extract used as an antioxidant/conditioning botanical (polyphenols, theobromine). Standard plant extract with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Theobroma Cacao (Cocoa) Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Cocoa butter used as a rich occlusive emollient. Standard benign cosmetic butter.

ingredients.name: Theobroma Cacao (Cocoa) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Flavonoid- and mineral-rich cocoa seed extract used as antioxidant, soothing and nourishing botanical. EWG low-hazard rating.

sources.label: PMC: Chemical and Skincare Property Characterization of Cocoa Byproducts
PMC: Cocoa Bioactive Compounds: Significance and Potential for the Maintenance of Skin Health
PubMed: Efficacy of cocoa pod extract as antiwrinkle gel on human skin surface

ingredients.name: Theobroma Grandiflorum (Cupuacu) Seed Butter
ingredients.note: A rich butter from the seeds of the cupuacu fruit (an Amazonian relative of cacao) that is highly moisturizing and helps improve skin elasticity and moisture retention. It melts at body temperature and is considered very safe.

sources.label: PubMed – Effect of cupuassu butter on human skin fibroblast cells (2018)
PMC – Skin regenerative potential of cupuacu seed extract topical formulation (2022)

ingredients.name: Theobroma Grandiflorum (Cupuaçu) Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Rich plant butter from cupuacu seeds used as an emollient/occlusive moisturizer. Standard benign cosmetic butter.

ingredients.name: Thermus Thermophilus Ferment
ingredients.note: Thermophilic-bacteria ferment used as an antioxidant/anti-pollution skin-conditioning active; standard ferment ingredient.

ingredients.name: Thioctic Acid
ingredients.note: Both fat- and water-soluble antioxidant used for smoothing and anti-aging. Well tolerated at cosmetic levels, with rare tingling at higher concentrations.

ingredients.name: Threonine
ingredients.note: Essential amino acid used as a skin-conditioning humectant. Covered as safe as used under the CIR alpha-amino acids review.

ingredients.name: Thymol Trimethoxycinnamate
ingredients.note: Thymol ester (C22H26O5) used as an antioxidant and skin-protecting agent in a limited number of products.

ingredients.name: Thymus Serpyllum Extract
ingredients.note: Wild thyme extract used as a skin-conditioning/antiseptic agent; aromatic compounds can occasionally irritate sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed: Green Extraction of Bioactive Compounds in Wild Thyme (Thymus serpyllum L.) — Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Activity
PubMed: Extraction and formulation of Thymus serpyllum L. for topical preparations
PMC: Review of Ethnobotanical, Phytochemical, and Pharmacological Study of Thymus serpyllum L.

ingredients.name: Thymus Vulgaris (Thyme) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the leaves of common thyme (Thymus vulgaris) containing thymol, carvacrol, and flavonoids; used in cosmetics for its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and soothing properties. Thyme constituents are classified as GRAS (Generally Recognized as Safe) in food, and topical use at cosmetic concentrations is considered safe.

sources.label: PubMed – Phytochemical Profiling and Therapeutic Potential of Thyme (Thymus spp.)
PMC – Evaluation of antibacterial and antifungal properties of Thymus vulgaris oil

ingredients.name: Tilia Cordata (Linden) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Linden flower extract used as a soothing/skin-conditioning botanical; standard low-risk plant extract.

ingredients.name: Tin Oxide
ingredients.note: Inert mineral used as an opacifier, abrasive and to enhance pearlescent pigments. CIR concluded safe; insoluble and not percutaneously absorbed.

ingredients.name: Tin Oxide (CI 77861)
ingredients.note: Inorganic tin(IV) oxide used as a base/coating for pearlescent effect pigments and as an opacifier; CAS 18282-10-5. CIR assessed it as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Titanium dioxide
ingredients.note: Mineral sunscreen filter; safe topically (inhalation concern only for loose powders, not creams).

ingredients.name: Titanium Dioxide and Mica (Coated Micas)
ingredients.note: Mica platelets coated with titanium dioxide to create pearlescent effects. Widely approved and considered safe for cosmetic use including makeup.

ingredients.name: Tocopherol
ingredients.note: Antioxidant; safe.

ingredients.name: Tocotrienols
ingredients.note: Vitamin E family compounds with strong lipid antioxidant activity, often from palm or rice. Safe and well tolerated topically.

ingredients.name: Tranexamic Acid
ingredients.note: Topical brightener (typically 2-5%) with minimal systemic absorption and a strong cosmetic safety record. The oral form is a prescription antifibrinolytic with clot risk; only the topical use is cosmetic.

ingredients.name: Trehalose
ingredients.note: Sugar humectant valued for protecting cells against dehydration; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Tremella Fuciformis (Mushroom) Extract
ingredients.note: Edible mushroom extract used as a humectant/antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Considered safe and well tolerated when properly sourced and purity-tested.

ingredients.name: Tremella Fuciformis Polysaccharide
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight mushroom-derived polysaccharide used as a humectant and film former; hyaluronic-acid-like water binding. Considered safe and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Tremella Mushroom Extract
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide humectant comparable to hyaluronic acid for hydration; gentle and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Triacontanyl PVP
ingredients.note: A synthetic polymer made by combining a long-chain fatty alcohol with PVP, used as a film former and light conditioning agent in hair styling products and some skincare; the CIR reviewed VP polymers and found them safe, and it is generally well tolerated.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Vinylpyrrolidone Polymers as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report PDF)

ingredients.name: Tribehenin
ingredients.note: Behenic acid triester of glycerin used as an emollient/thickening agent; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic lipid.

ingredients.name: Tricaprylin
ingredients.note: Caprylic-acid triglyceride used as emollient, solubilizer and carrier. CIR found triglycerides safe but noted it can enhance penetration of co-formulated actives.

ingredients.name: Tridecane
ingredients.note: A volatile C13 hydrocarbon solvent/emollient used as a lightweight silicone alternative, commonly blended with undecane. Considered a benign cosmetic solvent.

ingredients.name: Trideceth-10
ingredients.note: Nonionic PEG-ether surfactant/emulsifier and solubilizer. CIR found alkyl PEG ethers safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Trideceth-12
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated tridecyl alcohol used as a mild nonionic surfactant/emulsifier. Considered safe at typical levels; ethoxylates require purification to limit 1,4-dioxane.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)
CIR: Alkyl PEG Ether Panel Meeting Report (PDF)

ingredients.name: Trideceth-6 Phosphate
ingredients.note: Phosphoric-acid ester of trideceth-6 used as a surfactant/emulsifier; CIR considers it safe at typical concentrations, mild irritation possible on compromised skin.

ingredients.name: Trideceth-9
ingredients.note: Trideceth-9 is a nonionic ethoxylated alcohol surfactant that acts as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer in creams, cleansers, and perfume bases. The CIR Expert Panel has confirmed it is safe for cosmetic use, and it has low aquatic and mammalian toxicity.

ingredients.name: Tridecyl Trimellitate
ingredients.note: Trimellitic-acid ester emollient and film former (CAS 94109-09-8) used in color cosmetics; considered a safe, mineral-oil-alternative emollient.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety Assessment of Trialkyl Trimellitates as Used in Cosmetics
CIR: Safety Assessment of Trialkyl Trimellitates as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Triethoxycaprylylsilane
ingredients.note: Silane used to surface-treat/coat pigments and powders for dispersion; inert at use levels.

ingredients.name: Triethyl Citrate
ingredients.note: A citric acid ester used as a solvent, deodorant active (lowers skin pH to inhibit odor-causing bacteria), and plasticizer in nail products. FDA-recognized as GRAS; CIR Expert Panel confirmed it is safe in cosmetics at concentrations up to ~6%.

sources.label: Final report safety assessment of acetyl triethyl citrate and related esters – PubMed

ingredients.name: Triethylhexanoin
ingredients.note: Synthetic ester emollient (glyceryl trioctanoate) used as a light skin-conditioning oil and solvent for pigments. Standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Trifolium Pratense Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Red clover flower extract is rich in isoflavones (like genistein and formononetin) with antioxidant and mild phytoestrogenic activity; it is used in cosmetics to support skin elasticity and reduce signs of aging. It is considered safe for topical use, but those with hormone-sensitive conditions may wish to be cautious about regular use of isoflavone-rich products.

sources.label: PubMed: Effects of isoflavones from red clover on skin changes induced by ovariectomy in rats
NCCIH: Red Clover – Usefulness and Safety

ingredients.name: Trigonella Foenum-Graecum Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Fenugreek seed extract used as a hydrating antioxidant/anti-inflammatory botanical. Generally well tolerated; caution noted for those with legume (peanut/chickpea) allergy.

ingredients.name: Triheptanoin
ingredients.note: Triglyceride of glycerin and heptanoic acid used as a light emollient/occlusive and dispersant for UV filters. Non-comedogenic, low irritation.

sources.label: PubMed: Final report on the safety assessment of triheptanoin and related glyceryl triesters

ingredients.name: Trihydroxystearin
ingredients.note: Castor-oil-derived glyceryl ester used as a thixotropic thickener and emulsion structurant. Low irritation/allergy risk.

ingredients.name: Triisostearin
ingredients.note: A triglyceride made from glycerin and isostearic acid used as an emollient and thickener in lipsticks and skin-care products, giving them a rich, skin-softening feel. The CIR Expert Panel reviewed triglycerides including triisostearin and confirmed they are safe in current cosmetic practices.

ingredients.name: Trimethyl Pentaphenyl Trisiloxane
ingredients.note: Phenyl-substituted trisiloxane used as a high-refractive-index emollient/gloss agent in skin and color cosmetics. Standard low-concern silicone.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Linear Phenyl-Substituted Methicones as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Trimethylolpropane Triethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: A lightweight, non-greasy ester made from trimethylolpropane and 2-ethylhexanoic acid that leaves skin feeling smooth and conditioned; it is widely used in sunscreens and makeup. It has a well-established safety profile and is considered safe at cosmetic use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Trimethylolpropane Triisostearate
ingredients.note: Synthetic ester emollient used as a skin-conditioning and viscosity agent. CIR-reviewed as safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Trimethylsiloxyphenyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Phenyl-substituted silicone used as a gloss agent and conditioning emollient. CIR reviewed phenyl-substituted methicones; benign.

ingredients.name: Trimethylsiloxysilicate
ingredients.note: Silicone resin that creates long-wear, transfer-resistant films; chemically inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Trioctyldodecyl Citrate
ingredients.note: Citric acid triester of octyldodecanol used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign ester emollient.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety Assessment of Citric Acid, Inorganic Citrate Salts, and Alkyl Citrate Esters as Used in Cosmetics
CIR: Final Report on Safety Assessment of Citric Acid and Alkyl Citrate Esters (PDF)

ingredients.name: Triolein
ingredients.note: Triglyceride of glycerol and oleic acid used as an emollient/refatting oil; PubChem CID 5497163, CAS 122-32-7, benign and minimally absorbed.

ingredients.name: Tripeptide-1
ingredients.note: Copper-free signal peptide that supports collagen-related skin renewal. Used at low concentrations and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Tripeptide-2
ingredients.note: Synthetic three-amino-acid peptide used as a skin-conditioning/firming active. Standard cosmetic peptide.

ingredients.name: Tripeptide-3
ingredients.note: Synthetic three-amino-acid peptide used as a skin-conditioning/anti-wrinkle active. Standard cosmetic peptide.

ingredients.name: Tripropylene Glycol
ingredients.note: Glycol used as a solvent and humectant/viscosity agent. Low-toxicity standard cosmetic solvent.

ingredients.name: Tris-Biphenyl Triazine
ingredients.note: Newer broad-spectrum micronized triazine filter (Tinosorb A2B) with a large particle size and essentially no systemic absorption. Strong central-UV coverage and a clean safety assessment.

ingredients.name: Trisiloxane
ingredients.note: Low-molecular-weight silicone used as an emollient, slip agent and anti-foamer. Listed in PubChem and reviewed by CIR among siloxanes.

ingredients.name: Trisodium EDTA
ingredients.note: A chelating agent that binds to metal ions in cosmetic formulas to prevent them from destabilizing the product or making preservatives less effective; it also helps products rinse off cleanly. It is used at very low concentrations and is considered safe, with good rinse-off before any meaningful skin absorption.

ingredients.name: Trisodium Ethylenediamine Disuccinate
ingredients.note: Readily biodegradable chelator used as a greener alternative to EDTA. Safe and mild at use levels.

ingredients.name: Triticum Aestivum (Wheat) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Wheat seed extract used as an antioxidant and conditioning botanical. Benign; theoretical relevance only for wheat/gluten-sensitive users.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety Assessment of Wheat-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed: Triticum aestivum sprout extract attenuates atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions
PMC: General Health Benefits and Pharmacological Activities of Triticum aestivum L.

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat) Germ Oil Unsaponifiables
ingredients.note: The vitamin-E/phytosterol-rich unsaponifiable fraction of wheat germ oil, used as an antioxidant emollient. Benign; relevant only to wheat-sensitive users.

sources.label: CIR: Triticum vulgare (wheat) germ oil unsaponifiables ingredient page
PubMed: Safety Assessment of Wheat-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
CIR: Safety Assessment of Wheat-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - Final Report (PDF)

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from wheat seeds used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent, providing antioxidant and moisturizing benefits. The CIR Expert Panel assessed 27 wheat-derived ingredients and concluded they are safe for use in cosmetics at current practice levels.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Wheat-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
CIR – Triticum vulgare (wheat) germ oil ingredient page

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat) Sprout Extract
ingredients.note: Wheat sprout extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical; precautionary for potential wheat/gluten allergen reactivity. CAS 84012-44-2.

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat) Starch
ingredients.note: Starch extracted from wheat grains and used in cosmetics as a thickener, absorbent, and silky skin-feel agent. The CIR Expert Panel assessed wheat-derived ingredients and found wheat starch safe for cosmetic use; note that individuals with severe wheat/gluten contact allergy should exercise caution.

sources.label: CIR – Triticum vulgare (wheat) starch ingredient page
CIR – Safety Assessment of Wheat-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Tromethamine
ingredients.note: Amino-buffer used to neutralize carbomer and stabilize pH. Considered safe at low buffering levels.

ingredients.name: Tropaeolum Majus Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Nasturtium extract with antioxidant/anti-inflammatory glucosinolates, used for skin and scalp conditioning. Mustard-allergic individuals may cross-react.

ingredients.name: Tropolone
ingredients.note: Seven-membered aromatic ketone with antimicrobial activity used as a preservative/skin-conditioning agent; the related natural tropolone hinokitiol is low-stimulus and non-carcinogenic in rat testing.

ingredients.name: Troxerutin
ingredients.note: Rutin-derived bioflavonoid used as an antioxidant and skin-soothing/capillary-strengthening agent. Non-comedogenic, benign.

ingredients.name: Tryptophan
ingredients.note: Tryptophan is an essential amino acid that occurs naturally in all living things; in cosmetics it is used as a skin- and hair-conditioning agent to help maintain moisture and smoothness. The CIR Expert Panel reviewed alpha-amino acids including tryptophan and concluded they are safe for use in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of α-Amino Acids as Used in Cosmetics – SAGE Journals
CIR Safety Assessment of α-Amino Acids as Used in Cosmetics (full report PDF)

ingredients.name: Tyrosine
ingredients.note: Amino acid used as a skin-conditioning agent; a precursor of melanin sometimes included in tanning/conditioning products. Benign standard ingredient.

ingredients.name: Ubiquinone
ingredients.note: Lipophilic cellular antioxidant supporting mitochondrial energy and reducing oxidative damage. Safe and non-irritating in skincare.

ingredients.name: Ulmus Davidiana Root Extract
ingredients.note: Root extract of David's elm used as a soothing/conditioning botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Ultramarines
ingredients.note: Synthetic sodium aluminum sulfosilicate pigments. Approved by FDA (not for lips) and EU; considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Undaria Pinnatifida Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed (wakame) extract used as a skin-conditioning, antioxidant-rich active. CIR found brown-algae ingredients safe; control arsenic via GMP, rare seaweed/iodine allergy.

ingredients.name: Undecane
ingredients.note: A volatile C11 hydrocarbon used as a light, fast-drying solvent/emollient, often paired with tridecane as a silicone alternative. Considered a benign cosmetic solvent.

ingredients.name: Undecylenoyl Glycine
ingredients.note: This is an amino acid derivative (glycine coupled to undecylenic acid) used in cosmetics for its antimicrobial, anti-acne, and skin-conditioning properties. It has a good tolerability profile and is sometimes used as part of a natural preservative blend.

ingredients.name: Urea
ingredients.note: Humectant at low levels and keratolytic at higher concentrations; safe and widely used, though high-percentage formulas can sting compromised skin.

ingredients.name: Uric Acid
ingredients.note: An endogenous purine metabolite used in cosmetics as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning agent. Benign at cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Angustifolium (Blueberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Blueberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant/conditioning botanical. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Macrocarpon (Cranberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Cranberry fruit extract rich in A-type proanthocyanidins; used as antioxidant, astringent and skin-conditioning agent.

sources.label: PubMed: Wound-healing properties of the oils of Vitis vinifera and Vaccinium macrocarpon
PMC: Polyphenol-Rich Cranberry Beverage Positively Affected Skin Health, Skin Lipids, Skin Microbiome
PMC: Vaccinium Species — Unexplored Sources of Active Constituents for Cosmeceuticals

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Macrocarpon (Cranberry) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Balanced omega-3/6 cranberry seed oil used as an antioxidant emollient; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Myrtillus Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Bilberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Vitis-Idaea (Lingonberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the fruit of lingonberries, native to boreal forests, rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and vitamin C. It is used in cosmetics as an antioxidant and skin-calming ingredient; a cosmetic cream prototype with 5% lingonberry extract showed good physicochemical properties and is well tolerated.

sources.label: doi.org — Development and Characterization of Oil-in-Water Cosmetic Creams Containing Vaccinium vitis-idaea Fruit Extract
PubMed — Lingonberry (Vaccinium vitis-idaea L.) Skin Extract antioxidant study

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Vitis-Idaea Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Lingonberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant/moisturizing skin-conditioning botanical. Generally considered safe; not a prohibited cosmetic substance.

sources.label: DOI: Development and Physicochemical Characterization of Oil-in-Water Cosmetic Creams Containing Vaccinium vitis-idaea (Lingonberry) Fruit Extract
PubMed: Antioxidant activity in lingonberries (Vaccinium vitis-idaea L.) and inhibitory effect on AP-1, NF-kappaB, and MAPK
PubMed: Exploring Vaccinium vitis-idaea L. as a potential source of therapeutic agents: antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities

ingredients.name: Valine
ingredients.note: Branched-chain amino acid used as a skin-conditioning and antistatic agent. Benign topical cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Tahitensis Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the cured seed pods of Vanilla tahitensis (Tahitian vanilla), used as a fragrant skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics; its key aromatic compounds include vanillin and anisaldehyde. The CIR Expert Panel concluded that vanilla-derived cosmetic ingredients are safe as used when formulated to be non-sensitizing.

sources.label: PubMed – Safety Assessment of Vanilla as Used in Cosmetics
CIR – Safety Assessment of Vanilla-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (SLR)

ingredients.name: Vanillyl Butyl Ether
ingredients.note: Oil-soluble warming/sensate agent acting on TRPV1 to induce vasodilation. Low irritation at recommended 0.1-0.5%; used in lip and body products.

sources.label: PubMed: RIFM fragrance ingredient safety assessment, vanillyl butyl ether, CAS 82654-98-6
MDPI Cosmetics: A Validated HPLC Method for the Determination of Vanillyl Butyl Ether in Cosmetic Preparations

ingredients.name: Vegetable Amino Acids
ingredients.note: A mixture of amino acids derived from plant proteins (such as soy or wheat hydrolysates) that function as skin and hair conditioning agents, attracting and retaining moisture in the skin. The CIR Expert Panel has assessed plant- and animal-derived amino acids as safe for use in cosmetics.

sources.label: PubMed 25323218 – Safety assessment of animal- and plant-derived amino acids as used in cosmetics
CIR Safety Assessment of α-Amino Acids as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Veronica Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Speedwell (Veronica) extract used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. A standard, low-hazard plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed: Phytochemical Characterization of Veronica officinalis L. and Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties
PMC: Phytochemical Characterization of Veronica officinalis — Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties
MDPI Molecules: Veronica Plants — Drifting from Farm to Traditional Healing, Food Application, and Phytopharmacology

ingredients.name: Vibrio Alginolyticus Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: A filtrate from fermenting a marine bacterium that produces a skin-conditioning exopolysaccharide (sold as Epidermist 4.0™); it helps strengthen the skin barrier, support epidermal renewal, and improve hydration. It is subject to EU cosmetic safety regulations and is considered safe as formulated.

sources.label: PMC — Structure of the Polysaccharide Secreted by Vibrio alginolyticus CNCM I-5035 (Epidermist 4.0)

ingredients.name: Vigna Angularis Seed Powder
ingredients.note: Finely ground adzuki bean powder used as a gentle physical exfoliant and skin-conditioning ingredient in cleansers and masks. It is food-safe, non-sensitizing, and well tolerated across all skin types.

sources.label: PubMed 29243610 – Azuki bean extract inhibits atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions in NC/Nga mice

ingredients.name: Vigna Radiata Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Mung bean seed extract used as an antioxidant, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Standard low-hazard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Vincetoxicum Atratum Extract
ingredients.note: Plant extract used as antioxidant skin-conditioning agent; standard botanical with no notable sensitization data.

sources.label: PubMed: Cynanchum atratum inhibits the development of atopic dermatitis in 2,4-dinitrochlorobenzene-induced mice

ingredients.name: Vinegar
ingredients.note: Dilute acetic acid solution used as an acidifier/astringent and pH adjuster. Benign food-grade ingredient.

ingredients.name: Vinyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone polymer used as a film former and texture/skin-conditioning agent. CIR considers dimethicone-type polymers safe as used.

ingredients.name: Vinyl Dimethicone/Methicone Silsesquioxane Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Crosslinked silicone elastomer used as a texturizer providing soft-focus, mattifying feel. Inert, standard silicone ingredient.

ingredients.name: Viola Mandshurica Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Manchurian violet flower extract with flavonoids/polyphenols used as antioxidant and astringent. Benign standard botanical.

sources.label: PubMed: Anti-inflammatory and anti-asthmatic effects of Viola mandshurica ethanolic extract in a mouse model of allergic asthma
PMC: Anti-obesity activity, acute toxicity, and chemical constituents of aqueous and ethanol Viola mandshurica extracts
PMC: Chloroform Extract from Fermented Viola mandshurica Regulates LPS-Induced Inflammation Response

ingredients.name: Viola Odorata Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Sweet violet leaf extract used as a soothing, skin-conditioning botanical. Standard cosmetic plant extract with low irritation potential.

sources.label: PubMed: A Critical Review on Phytochemistry, Pharmacology of Viola odorata L. and Related Multipotential Products
PubMed: Anti-melanogenic activity of Viola odorata different extracts on B16F10 murine melanoma cells
PMC: Commercially Available Viola odorata Oil — Chemical Variability and Antimicrobial Activity

ingredients.name: Viola Tricolor Extract
ingredients.note: Wild pansy/heartsease extract used as a soothing, emollient skin-conditioning botanical. Benign, though plant extracts may rarely irritate sensitive skin.

sources.label: PubMed: Analysis of rutin in the extract and gel of Viola tricolor
PMC: Immunosuppressive activity of an aqueous Viola tricolor herbal extract
PMC: A Systematic Review of Phytochemistry, Nutritional Composition, and Pharmacologic Application of Species of the Genus Viola

ingredients.name: Vitis Vinifera (Grape) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Grape fruit extract used as an antioxidant/conditioning botanical (polyphenols, resveratrol). Standard benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Vitis Vinifera (Grape) Juice Extract
ingredients.note: An antioxidant-rich extract from grape juice containing anthocyanins, tartaric acid, and resveratrol precursors that help protect skin from oxidative stress; the CIR Expert Panel assessed 24 grape-derived ingredients and found them safe as used in cosmetics.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Vitis vinifera (Grape)-Derived Ingredients (PDF)
PubMed: Safety assessment of Vitis vinifera (grape)-derived ingredients as used in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Vitis Vinifera (Grape) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: A polyphenol-rich extract from grape seeds with potent antioxidant activity — stronger than vitamins C and E in some studies — that protects skin from UV-induced damage and supports anti-aging. The CIR Expert Panel assessed 24 grape-derived cosmetic ingredients as safe.

sources.label: Safety assessment of Vitis vinifera grape-derived ingredients in cosmetics – PubMed
Nonclinical evaluation of Vitis vinifera extract as novel antiaging ingredient – PubMed

ingredients.name: Vitis Vinifera (Grape) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Linoleic-acid-rich grape seed oil used as a lightweight emollient and antioxidant. Standard low-hazard plant oil.

ingredients.name: Vitreoscilla Ferment
ingredients.note: A fermented extract of the non-pathogenic bacteria Vitreoscilla filiformis, originally found in thermal spa mud; it is used in skincare to support the skin's natural microbiome, antioxidant defenses, and barrier function. It is well-studied, safe, and biodegradable.

sources.label: PMC – Vitreoscilla filiformis Extract for Topical Skin Care: A Review
PMC – Complete Genome Sequence of Vitreoscilla filiformis Used as a Cosmetic Ingredient

ingredients.name: Volcanic Ash
ingredients.note: Volcanic ash is used in facial cleansers and masks as a natural mineral absorbent to draw out excess oil and impurities, and in masks for its mineral content. It is generally safe when used as intended in face masks, but coarser volcanic ash particles in scrubs should be avoided near the eyes as glass microsphere fragments can cause eye irritation.

sources.label: PubMed: Analysis of volcanic-ash-based insoluble ingredients of facial cleansers
PubMed: Efficacy and tolerance of a cosmetic mask containing Vichy volcanic mineralizing water

ingredients.name: VP/Eicosene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Oil-soluble film former used for pigment binding and wear in makeup and sunscreens; regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: VP/Hexadecene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic film-forming polymer used as a viscosity/texture and binding agent; large inert polymer, low concern.
