sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Vinylpyrrolidone Polymers as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: VP/VA Copolymer
ingredients.note: Film-forming copolymer for fixative and texturizing effects. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.note: Universal cosmetic solvent and base; entirely benign.

ingredients.name: Wheat Amino Acids
ingredients.note: Wheat Amino Acids are the peptide fragments and amino acids released when wheat protein is hydrolyzed; they condition and strengthen both skin and hair. The CIR has concluded that wheat-derived amino acids and hydrolysates are safe in cosmetics when the peptide lengths are kept within established limits.

sources.label: CIR – Safety Assessment of Wheat-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed – Safety assessment of animal- and plant-derived amino acids as used in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Whey Protein
ingredients.note: Milk-derived protein used as a skin-conditioning agent; benign topically but a milk allergen to avoid for those with IgE-mediated milk-protein allergy.

ingredients.name: Withania Somnifera Root Extract
ingredients.note: Ashwagandha root extract is a well-known Ayurvedic adaptogen that, when applied topically, delivers antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits to help soothe and firm aging skin. A randomized controlled trial of 8% ashwagandha lotion found significant improvement in photoaged skin with no significant safety concerns.

sources.label: Study of Efficacy and Safety of Ashwagandha Lotion on Facial Skin in Photoaged Adults – PMC

ingredients.name: Xanthan Gum
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide gum from fermentation used to thicken and stabilize water phases. Natural, food-grade and very well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Xylitol
ingredients.note: Sugar alcohol used as a humectant and moisture-binding agent. Benign, well-tolerated cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Xylitylglucoside
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived humectant that supports skin water reservoirs; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Xylose
ingredients.note: Plant-derived sugar used as a humectant to bind water and improve skin moisture/elasticity. Rarely irritating or sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Yeast Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: Fermentation-derived extract used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant active. No significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Yellow 5 Lake (CI 19140)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved, batch-certified tartrazine lake pigment; safe topically, with rare hives in sensitive individuals (<0.01%).

sources.label: FDA: Color Additives and Cosmetics Fact Sheet
PubMed: Hypersensitivity to tartrazine (FD&C Yellow No. 5) and other dyes
PMC: Toxicological Effects of Tartrazine Exposure: A Review

ingredients.name: Yellow 6 Lake (CI 15985)
ingredients.note: FDA/EU-approved azo colorant (Sunset Yellow FCF) as an aluminum lake; safe at GMP levels, with rare contact-allergy potential in sensitive users.

sources.label: PMC: Safety and efficacy of Sunset Yellow FCF as feed additive — EFSA assessment
PMC: Sunset Yellow protects against oxidative damage and exhibits chemoprevention in chemically induced skin cancer model

ingredients.name: Yellow Iron Oxide (CI 77492)
ingredients.note: This is a synthetic yellow iron oxide pigment (hydrated ferric oxide) used to give cosmetics warm yellow and earthy tones; it is exempt from FDA batch certification and permitted for use in cosmetics. It is an inert inorganic pigment with no significant safety concerns.

sources.label: FDA – Color Additives Permitted for Use in Cosmetics
PubChem – C.I. Pigment Yellow 42 / Ferric oxide Yellow (CID 23320441)
ECHA – Iron hydroxide oxide yellow Substance Information

ingredients.name: Zea Mays (Corn) Kernel Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from corn kernels used in cosmetics for antioxidant, soothing, and skin-regenerating effects. The CIR Expert Panel reviewed corn-derived cosmetic ingredients and determined they are safe for use in the concentrations described in the safety assessment.

sources.label: PubMed – Final report of the safety assessment of cosmetic ingredients derived from Zea mays (corn)

ingredients.name: Zea Mays (Corn) Starch
ingredients.note: Corn-derived starch used as absorbent, bulking and skin-conditioning agent (talc alternative). CIR concluded corn-derived ingredients safe.

ingredients.name: Zea Mays Oil
ingredients.note: Corn oil (from the Zea mays plant) is a lightweight emollient rich in linoleic acid that softens and moisturizes skin. It is a well-established cosmetic ingredient with a strong safety record, and is listed by the CIR as an evaluated cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: CIR: Zea mays (corn) oil – Cosmetic Ingredient Review

ingredients.name: Zinc Chloride
ingredients.note: Zinc salt used as astringent/oral-care/biocide agent; CIR safe when formulated to be non-irritating, but severely irritating at ~1%, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Zinc Citrate
ingredients.note: Zinc citrate is a zinc salt with mild antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, often used in skincare and oral care products to reduce bacteria and soothe irritation. It is well tolerated at cosmetic use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Zinc Gluconate
ingredients.note: Zinc salt of gluconic acid used as a sebum-regulating/soothing skin-conditioning agent; CIR found zinc salts safe when formulated to be non-irritating. GRAS as a nutrient.

ingredients.name: Zinc Hydrolyzed Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Zinc salt of hydrolyzed hyaluronic acid combining humectant moisture-binding with zinc's soothing/sebum-balancing action. Gentle and non-irritating at low use levels.

sources.label: PubMed: Zinc-hyaluronate — an original organotherapeutic compound of Gedeon Richter Ltd
CIR: Safety Assessment of Hyaluronates as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report PDF)

ingredients.name: Zinc Lactate
ingredients.note: A zinc salt of lactic acid used in cosmetics and oral care products for its antimicrobial and astringent properties (often in deodorants and toothpastes). The CIR assessed zinc salts as safe in cosmetics; the EU restricts zinc-containing products to a maximum of 1% zinc in ready-to-use preparations.

sources.label: CIR — Safety Assessment of Zinc Salts as Used in Cosmetics (Final Report)

ingredients.name: Zinc oxide
ingredients.note: Mineral sunscreen filter; the clean alternative to chemical UV filters. Safe.

ingredients.name: Zinc PCA
ingredients.note: Zinc salt of PCA used as a sebum-regulating, antimicrobial humectant active. Recognized as safe and well tolerated at typical 0.1-1% levels.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety Assessment of PCA (2-Pyrrolidone-5-Carboxylic Acid) and Its Salts as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed: Zinc l-pyrrolidone carboxylate inhibits UVA-induced matrix metalloproteinase-1 and enhances collagen synthesis
CIR: Safety Assessment of PCA and Its Salts as Used in Cosmetics (PDF)

ingredients.name: Zinc Ricinoleate
ingredients.note: The zinc salt of ricinoleic acid (from castor oil) used as an odor absorber in deodorants; it traps and neutralizes odor molecules rather than blocking sweat. It has been reviewed by the CIR and is considered safe at the concentrations used in cosmetic products.

ingredients.name: Zinc Stearate
ingredients.note: Metallic soap used as a binder and slip agent in powders; safe topically, though inhalation of loose powder forms should be avoided.

ingredients.name: Zingiber Officinale (Ginger) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Ginger root extract used as a skin-conditioning antioxidant botanical; CIR found ginger root/rhizome ingredients safe when formulated to be non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Zingiber Officinale Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from ginger root that delivers antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits to skin, helping to calm redness and fight free-radical damage. The CIR Expert Panel concluded 8 ginger-derived ingredients are safe in cosmetics when formulated to be non-sensitizing.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Zingiber officinale (Ginger)-Derived Ingredients (PDF)
doi.org – CIR Safety Assessment of Ginger-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics (2026)

ingredients.name: Zizyphus Jujuba Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Jujube fruit extract rich in flavonoids, polysaccharides and vitamin C used as an antioxidant, soothing, brightening botanical.

ingredients.name: Zymomonas Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from Zymomonas mobilis fermentation used as a humectant and skin conditioner. Listed in EU CosIng (ref 60329), generally safe topically.

ingredients.name: Silica Dimethyl Silylate
ingredients.note: Surface-treated (silanized) silica used as an anti-caking agent, thickener and texturizer in cosmetics. Inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Cross-linked silicone polymer used as a thickener and to give a smooth, velvety skin feel. Benign and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Triisostearyl Citrate
ingredients.note: Citric acid triester used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Inert synthetic ester, well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Methyl Methacrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Cross-linked acrylic polymer microspheres used as a soft-focus texturizer and bulking agent. Inert and non-reactive once polymerized.

ingredients.name: Neopentyl Glycol Dicaprylate/Dicaprate
ingredients.note: Light synthetic ester emollient that improves spreadability and softens skin. Non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Plumeria Rubra Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Frangipani flower extract used for fragrance and skin conditioning. Floral botanical with potential to contain sensitizing fragrance constituents.

ingredients.name: Myristoyl Pentapeptide-17
ingredients.note: Lipidated signal peptide marketed to support eyelash and keratin growth. Well tolerated cosmetic peptide.

ingredients.name: Salicornia Herbacea Extract
ingredients.note: Glasswort (sea succulent) extract used as a humectant and skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extract with potential sensitizing constituents.

ingredients.name: C20-24 Olefin
ingredients.note: Long-chain hydrocarbon used as an emollient and texturizing agent. Inert and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Shea Butter Ethyl Esters
ingredients.note: Ethyl esters derived from shea butter used as a lightweight emollient. Non-sensitizing skin conditioner.

ingredients.name: Marrubium Vulgare Extract
ingredients.note: White horehound plant extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extract with possible sensitizing constituents.

ingredients.name: C18-36 Acid Triglyceride
ingredients.note: Synthetic wax-like triglyceride used as an emollient and structuring agent. Inert and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Propylene Glycol Dibenzoate
ingredients.note: Benzoate diester used as an emollient and solubilizer. Non-sensitizing synthetic ester.

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice Powder
ingredients.note: Dried aloe vera leaf juice used as a soothing humectant and skin-conditioning agent. Widely used and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Lonicera Caprifolium (Honeysuckle) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Honeysuckle flower extract used as a skin conditioner and natural preservative booster. Floral botanical with potential sensitizing constituents.

ingredients.name: Camellia Oleifera (Green Tea) Leaf Extract
aliases: Camellia Oleifera Green Tea Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Green tea leaf extract used as an antioxidant and soothing botanical. Plant extract with possible sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Tridecapeptide-1
ingredients.note: Synthetic signal peptide used as a skin-conditioning and anti-aging active. Well tolerated cosmetic peptide.

ingredients.name: Sea Whip Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of Pseudopterogorgia (sea whip coral) used for its soothing, anti-irritant properties. Marine-derived extract with potential sensitizing constituents.

ingredients.name: Rubus Idaeus Leaf Cell Culture
ingredients.note: Raspberry leaf cell-culture extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical. Plant-derived material with possible sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Behenylcarbamoylpropyl Polysilsesquioxane
ingredients.note: Silicone resin (silsesquioxane) used as a film former and texturizing agent. Inert and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Quaternium-90 Bentonite
ingredients.note: Quaternary-ammonium-modified bentonite clay used as a suspending and gelling agent. Contains a quaternary ammonium moiety with potential sensitizing concern.

ingredients.name: Di-C12-13 Alkyl Tartrate
ingredients.note: Tartaric acid diester used as a light emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Non-sensitizing synthetic ester.

ingredients.name: Cassia Angustifolia Seed Polysaccharide
ingredients.note: Senna seed polysaccharide used as a film-forming humectant (botanical hyaluronic-acid analog). Well tolerated moisturizing agent.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Soybean Oil
ingredients.note: Hardened soybean oil used as an emollient and structuring agent. Non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Lindera Strychnifolia Root Extract
ingredients.note: Root extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extract with possible sensitizing constituents.

ingredients.name: Sucrose Tetrastearate Triacetate
ingredients.note: Sugar-based ester used as an emulsifier and emollient. Non-sensitizing and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Stearalkonium Bentonite
ingredients.note: Quaternary-ammonium-modified bentonite clay used as a suspending/gelling agent. Contains a quaternary ammonium moiety with potential sensitizing concern.

ingredients.name: Passiflora Edulis (Passion Fruit) Seed Oil
aliases: Passiflora Edulis Passion Fruit Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Passion fruit seed oil used as a lightweight emollient rich in fatty acids. Non-sensitizing plant oil.

ingredients.name: Brassica Alba Sprout Extract
ingredients.note: White mustard sprout extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Mustard-family plant extract with possible sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Tripeptide-29
ingredients.note: Synthetic peptide (collagen-related) used as a skin-conditioning anti-aging active. Well tolerated cosmetic peptide.

ingredients.name: Michelia Alba Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Aromatic leaf essential oil used for fragrance and skin conditioning. Essential oil with sensitizing fragrance potential.

ingredients.name: Xanthophylls
ingredients.note: Naturally derived yellow carotenoid pigments used as colorants and antioxidants. Generally benign.

ingredients.name: Camellia Oleifera (Camellia) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Camellia (tea) seed oil used as a rich emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Non-sensitizing plant oil.

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Hexacaprylate/Hexacaprate
