ingredients.name: Polyglucuronic Acid
ingredients.note: A polysaccharide used as a humectant and film-forming skin-conditioning agent. Benign standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polygonum Bistorta Root Extract
ingredients.note: A tannin-rich botanical extract from bistort root used as an astringent and antioxidant in skincare. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Sorbeth-230 Tetraoleate
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated sorbitol ester of oleic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Considered a benign standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Cocoyl Proline
ingredients.note: An amino-acid-derived (proline) coconut fatty acyl surfactant used as a mild cleansing and conditioning agent. Regarded as gentle and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Lactoperoxidase
ingredients.note: An enzyme used in cosmetics as part of antimicrobial enzyme systems and for skin-conditioning. Considered benign at use levels.

ingredients.name: Glucose Oxidase
ingredients.note: An enzyme used in cosmetics for antimicrobial and antioxidant preservation systems. Regarded as a benign functional ingredient.

ingredients.name: Sodium Laurylglucosides Hydroxypropylsulfonate
ingredients.note: A mild glucoside-derived anionic surfactant used as a gentle cleansing agent. Considered a low-irritation, benign cleanser.

ingredients.name: Glycol Stearate
ingredients.note: An ester of ethylene glycol and stearic acid used as an emulsifier, opacifier and pearlescent agent. Considered a benign standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Gigartina Stellata Extract
ingredients.note: A red algae (carrageenan-type seaweed) extract used as a film-former, hydrating and skin-conditioning agent. Generally regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Acetyl SH-Hexapeptide-5 Amide Acetate
ingredients.note: A synthetic acetylated hexapeptide used as a signal peptide skin-conditioning active. Peptides are considered benign cosmetic actives.

ingredients.name: Ceteth-20 Phosphate
ingredients.note: A phosphated ethoxylated cetyl alcohol used as a nonionic/anionic emulsifier and surfactant. Considered a benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Dicetyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: A phosphate diester of cetyl alcohol used as an emulsifier and co-emulsifier in creams. Regarded as a benign standard ingredient.

ingredients.name: Althaea Officinalis Root Extract
ingredients.note: A mucilage-rich marshmallow root extract used as a soothing, hydrating skin-conditioning agent. Botanical extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Myristamidopropyl PG-Dimonium Chloride Phosphate
ingredients.note: A quaternary ammonium phosphate compound used as a conditioning surfactant and antistatic agent. Quaternary ammonium compounds carry sensitizer/irritant potential.

ingredients.name: Zerumbone
ingredients.note: A sesquiterpene compound from wild ginger used as an antioxidant and conditioning botanical active. As a reactive terpene it carries sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Helianthus Annuus Sunflower Seed Extract
ingredients.note: A sunflower seed extract used as an emollient, antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, well-tolerated botanical emollient.

ingredients.name: Glycereth-7 Triacetate
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated glycerin triacetate used as an emollient and solvent. Considered a benign standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Behenate/Eicosadioate
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl ester used as a nonionic emulsifier and thickener in emulsions. Considered a benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa Kernel Extract
ingredients.note: An oat kernel extract used as a soothing, anti-irritant skin-conditioning agent. Oat is well tolerated and regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Epilobium Angustifolium Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: A willow herb (fireweed) extract used as a soothing, anti-inflammatory botanical active. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Myristoyl Hexapeptide-16
ingredients.note: A lipidated synthetic hexapeptide used as a skin-conditioning signal peptide active. Peptides are considered benign cosmetic actives.

ingredients.name: Azelaoyl Bis-Dipeptide-10
ingredients.note: An azelaic-acid-linked dipeptide used as a skin-conditioning and brightening peptide active. Peptides are considered benign cosmetic actives.

ingredients.name: C12-16 Pareth-9
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated fatty alcohol used as a nonionic surfactant and emulsifier. Considered a benign standard surfactant.

ingredients.name: Citrate Buffer
ingredients.note: A citric acid/citrate salt system used to buffer and stabilize formulation pH. Considered an inert, benign pH adjuster.

ingredients.name: Trimethylsiloxyamodimethicone
ingredients.note: An amino-functional silicone used as a conditioning agent and emollient. Silicones are considered benign cosmetic ingredients.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Pomegranate Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A pomegranate fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Dunaliella Salina Algae Extract
ingredients.note: A carotenoid-rich microalgae extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Generally regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Pongamia Glabra Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A plant seed oil (karanja) used as an emollient and skin-conditioning oil. Considered a benign emollient.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Ascorbate
ingredients.note: A glycerin ester of vitamin C used as a stabilized antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign vitamin derivative.

ingredients.name: Clitoria Ternatea Butterfly Pea Flower Extract
aliases: Clitoria Ternatea Butterfly Pea Flower Extract
ingredients.note: A butterfly pea flower extract used as an antioxidant, colorant and skin-conditioning botanical. Flower extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Bis-Phenylpropyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A phenyl-functional silicone used as an emollient and gloss/conditioning agent. Silicones are considered benign cosmetic ingredients.

ingredients.name: Laminaria Ochroleuca Extract
ingredients.note: A brown seaweed (Mediterranean kelp) extract used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning agent. Generally regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Pumpkin Seedcake
ingredients.note: A hydrolyzed pumpkin seed press-cake used as a conditioning and exfoliating/enzymatic skin agent. Considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Vaccinum Angustifolium Blueberry Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A blueberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Prunus Serotina Wild Cherry Fruit Extract
aliases: Prunus Serotina Wild Cherry Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A wild cherry fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign fruit botanical.

ingredients.name: Alpha-Tocopherol
ingredients.note: The principal form of vitamin E used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, beneficial vitamin.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Jojoba Protein
ingredients.note: A hydrolyzed jojoba-derived protein used as a film-forming, moisturizing and conditioning agent. Considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Tridecyl Stearate
ingredients.note: A synthetic fatty-alcohol ester used as an emollient and texture-enhancing agent. Considered a benign emollient ester.

ingredients.name: Decarboxy Carnosine HCl
ingredients.note: A carnosine derivative used as an antioxidant and anti-glycation skin-conditioning active. Considered a benign cosmetic active.

ingredients.name: Triacetin
ingredients.note: A glycerin triacetate used as a solvent, plasticizer and humectant. Considered a benign standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Vegetable Glycerides Citrate
ingredients.note: A citric-acid-esterified hydrogenated vegetable glyceride used as an emulsifier and antioxidant/chelating co-agent. Considered a benign ingredient.

ingredients.name: Peucedanum Graveolens Dill Extract
ingredients.note: A dill plant extract used as a skin-conditioning and firming botanical active. Aromatic herb extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Sambucus Nigra Black Elderberry Fruit Extract
aliases: Sambucus Nigra Black Elderberry Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A black elderberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Pomegranate Extract
ingredients.note: A pomegranate extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Lycium Barbarum Goji Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A goji berry fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign fruit botanical.

ingredients.name: Actinidia Chinensis Kiwi Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A kiwi fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign fruit botanical.

ingredients.name: Tetrapeptide-30
ingredients.note: A synthetic tetrapeptide used as a skin-conditioning and brightening peptide active. Peptides are considered benign cosmetic actives.

ingredients.name: Chamomilla Recutita Flower Extract
ingredients.note: A chamomile flower extract used as a soothing, anti-inflammatory skin-conditioning botanical. Chamomile (Asteraceae) is a known potential sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Peucedanum Graveolens Extract
ingredients.note: A dill plant extract used as a skin-conditioning and firming botanical active. Aromatic herb extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Sambucus Nigra Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An elderberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa Bran Extract
ingredients.note: An oat bran extract used as a soothing, anti-irritant skin-conditioning agent. Oat is well tolerated and regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A pomegranate fruit extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Picea Excelsa Wood Extract
ingredients.note: A spruce wood extract used as a skin-conditioning and astringent botanical. Coniferous/resinous extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: PPG-2-Deceth-30
ingredients.note: A propoxylated/ethoxylated decyl alcohol used as a nonionic surfactant and solubilizer. Considered a benign standard surfactant.

ingredients.name: C13-14 Alkane
ingredients.note: A plant-derived light hydrocarbon used as a lightweight emollient and solvent. Considered a benign emollient.

ingredients.name: Quaternium-18 Bentonite
ingredients.note: A quaternary-ammonium-modified bentonite clay used as a suspending and viscosity-controlling agent. As a bound clay it is considered an inert, benign texturizer.

ingredients.name: Bis-Diglyceryl Polyacyladipate-1
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl adipate ester used as a rich emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign emollient ester.

ingredients.name: Biotinoyl Tripeptide-1
ingredients.note: A biotin-conjugated tripeptide used as a skin- and hair-conditioning peptide active. Peptides are considered benign cosmetic actives.

ingredients.name: Glycyrrhiza Glabra Licorice Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: A licorice leaf extract used as a soothing, brightening and antioxidant botanical active. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Amorphophallus Konjac Root Extract
ingredients.note: A konjac (glucomannan) root extract used as a hydrating, film-forming and texturizing agent. Considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Pomegranate Peel Extract
ingredients.note: A pomegranate peel extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign, well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Pentaerythrityl Tetrabehenate
ingredients.note: A pentaerythritol ester of behenic acid used as an emollient, thickener and structuring wax. Considered a benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Tristearin
ingredients.note: A triglyceride of stearic acid used as an emollient, thickener and structuring agent. Considered a benign standard ingredient.

ingredients.name: Helianthus Annuus Seed Oil Unsaponifiables
ingredients.note: The unsaponifiable fraction of sunflower seed oil used as an emollient and soothing skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign emollient.

ingredients.name: Babassu Oil Glycereth-8 Esters
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated-glycerin ester of babassu oil used as an emollient and emulsifying conditioning agent. Considered a benign emollient.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose Stearoxy Ether
ingredients.note: A hydrophobically modified cellulose ether used as a thickener, film-former and stabilizer. Considered a benign film-forming polymer.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-5 Oleate
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl ester of oleic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Considered a benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Gleditsia Triacanthos Seed Extract
ingredients.note: A honey locust seed extract used as a film-forming, tightening and skin-conditioning agent. Generally regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Sodium Magnesium Fluorosilicate
ingredients.note: A synthetic fluorosilicate clay (hectorite-type) used as a viscosity-controlling and suspending agent. As an inert mineral it is considered benign.

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyacrylate Starch
ingredients.note: A starch-grafted sodium polyacrylate used as a thickener, gelling and absorbent agent. Considered a benign film-forming polymer.

ingredients.name: Achillea Millefolium Yarrow Extract
ingredients.note: A yarrow extract used as an astringent, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Yarrow (Asteraceae) is a known potential sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Sapindus Mukorossi Soapberry Peel Extract
ingredients.note: A saponin-rich soapberry (soapnut) peel extract used as a natural surfactant and cleansing agent. Saponin-rich botanical extracts can carry irritation/sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Punica Granatum Bark/Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A pomegranate bark and fruit extract used as an antioxidant and astringent skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Jojoba Oil
ingredients.note: A hardened jojoba ester wax used as an emollient, thickener and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign emollient/wax.

ingredients.name: Polyimide-1
ingredients.note: A synthetic polyimide polymer used as a film-forming and texture-enhancing agent. Considered a benign film-forming polymer.

ingredients.name: Butyl Stearate
ingredients.note: A butyl ester of stearic acid used as an emollient and lubricant. Considered a benign emollient ester.

ingredients.name: Gossypium Hirsutum Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A cottonseed oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning oil. Considered a benign plant emollient.

ingredients.name: Zea Mays Germ Oil
ingredients.note: A corn germ oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning oil. Considered a benign plant emollient.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa Germ Extract
ingredients.note: A rice germ extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Triisodecyl Trimellitate
ingredients.note: A trimellitate ester used as an emollient and conditioning film-former. Considered a benign emollient ester.

ingredients.name: Beeswax Cera Alba
ingredients.note: Natural beeswax used as a thickener, emollient and structuring wax. Considered a benign standard cosmetic wax.

ingredients.name: Capparis Spinosa Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: A caper fruit extract used as a soothing, anti-irritant skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Dimethylmethoxy Chromanol
ingredients.note: A vitamin-E-related chromanol used as a stable, broad-spectrum antioxidant. Considered a benign antioxidant ingredient.

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Lactate
ingredients.note: A lactic acid fatty ester used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign emollient ester.

ingredients.name: Daucus Carota Sativa Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A carrot seed essential oil used as an aromatic, antioxidant skin-conditioning agent. Essential oils carry fragrance-allergen and sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Dipentaerythrityl Pentaisostearate
ingredients.note: A dipentaerythritol ester of isostearic acid used as an emollient and thickening agent. Considered a benign emollient ester.

ingredients.name: Cichorium Intybus Root Extract
ingredients.note: A chicory (inulin-rich) root extract used as a hydrating, smoothing skin-conditioning agent. Generally regarded as benign.

ingredients.name: Sodium Potassium Aluminum Silicate
ingredients.note: An inert aluminosilicate mineral used as a bulking, anticaking and texturizing agent. Considered a benign inert mineral.

ingredients.name: Gentiana Lutea Root Extract
ingredients.note: A gentian root extract used as a soothing and skin-conditioning botanical active. Plant extracts can carry sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Zinc Laurate
ingredients.note: A zinc salt of lauric acid used as a viscosity-controlling, opacifying and anticaking agent. Considered a benign metallic soap.

ingredients.name: Enterococcus Faecalis
ingredients.note: An inactivated/lysate probiotic-derived ingredient used as a skin-conditioning and microbiome-supporting agent. Used in inactivated form it is considered benign.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyapatite
ingredients.note: A calcium phosphate mineral used as an abrasive, bulking and opacifying agent. Considered a benign inert mineral.

ingredients.name: Morinda Citrifolia Fruit Juice
ingredients.note: A noni fruit juice used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign fruit botanical.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Distearate
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl diester of stearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Considered a benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Glutamine
ingredients.note: A free amino acid used as a hydrating and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Olive Glycerides
ingredients.note: Mono-, di- and triglycerides derived from olive oil used as an emollient and emulsion stabilizer. Considered a benign emollient.

ingredients.name: Biotinoyl Hexapeptide-2 Amide
ingredients.note: A biotin-conjugated hexapeptide used as a skin- and hair-conditioning peptide active. Peptides are considered benign cosmetic actives.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Yeast Protein
ingredients.note: A hydrolyzed yeast-derived protein used as a moisturizing and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Lauryl Methacrylate/Glycol Dimethacrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic crosslinked acrylate polymer used as a film former, viscosity controller and absorbent in cosmetics. Inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Citrullus Vulgaris (Watermelon) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Watermelon fruit extract used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant botanical. As a plant extract it carries some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Silybum Marianum (Lady's Thistle) Extract
ingredients.note: Milk thistle (silymarin) extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts of the daisy family can be sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Olea Europaea (Olive) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Olive fruit extract used as an emollient and antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat) Bran Extract
ingredients.note: Wheat bran extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. May pose sensitizing or allergen concern for gluten/wheat-sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Saccharin
ingredients.note: A synthetic sweetener used as a flavoring/oral-care agent. Considered safe and non-sensitizing in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Potassium Sulfate
ingredients.note: A simple inorganic salt used as a viscosity-increasing and bulking agent. Inert and benign.

ingredients.name: Hypnea Musciformis (Algae) Extract
ingredients.note: Red algae extract used as a film-forming, moisturizing botanical. As a marine plant extract it carries some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Tripeptide-32
ingredients.note: A synthetic signal peptide used as a skin-conditioning and anti-aging active. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Poria Cocos Sclerotium Extract
ingredients.note: Fungal (hoelen) extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Plant/fungal extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Gelidiella Acerosa Extract
ingredients.note: Red algae extract used as a skin-conditioning and moisturizing botanical. As a marine plant extract it carries some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: PPG-5-Ceteth-20
ingredients.note: A polyoxyethylene/polyoxypropylene ether of cetyl alcohol used as a nonionic emulsifier and surfactant. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Lycium Chinense (Wolfberry) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Goji/wolfberry fruit extract used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Sapindus Mukorossi Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Soapnut fruit extract (saponin-rich) used as a natural cleansing and skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone Silylate
ingredients.note: A silicone derivative used as an emollient and film former. Inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Pantethine
ingredients.note: A pantothenic acid (vitamin B5) derivative used as a skin-conditioning agent. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyethylene Terephthalate
ingredients.note: An inert synthetic polyester used as a film former and for glitter/film substrates in cosmetics. Chemically stable and benign.

ingredients.name: Sodium RNA
ingredients.note: The sodium salt of ribonucleic acid used as a skin-conditioning active. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Narcissus Tazetta Bulb Extract
ingredients.note: Daffodil bulb extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Narcissus contains alkaloids and is a known contact sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Jojobate
ingredients.note: An isopropyl ester of jojoba acids used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Betula Alba (Birch) Extract
ingredients.note: White birch extract used as an astringent skin-conditioning botanical. Birch is a recognized contact allergen/sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Artemia Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of brine shrimp (Artemia) zooplankton used as a skin-conditioning and energizing active. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Aleurites Moluccana (Kukui) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Kukui (candlenut) seed oil used as an emollient skin-conditioning oil. Well tolerated as a plant emollient.

ingredients.name: Garcinia Mangostana Peel Extract
ingredients.note: Mangosteen peel extract (xanthone-rich) used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Salvia Hispanica Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Chia seed extract used as a moisturizing, omega-rich skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Myrtillus Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Bilberry seed oil used as an emollient, fatty-acid-rich skin-conditioning oil. Well tolerated as a plant emollient.

ingredients.name: Paeonia Suffruticosa (Peony) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Tree peony root extract used as a skin-conditioning and brightening botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Panax Ginseng (Ginseng) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Ginseng root extract used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Trisodium HEDTA
ingredients.note: A chelating agent that sequesters metal ions to stabilize formulations. Used at low levels and generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Methyldihydrojasmonate
ingredients.note: A synthetic jasmine-type fragrance ingredient. As a fragrance material it carries fragrance-allergen/sensitizer potential.

ingredients.name: Gentiana Lutea (Gentian) Root Extract
ingredients.note: Yellow gentian root extract used as a skin-conditioning and soothing botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: PEG-3 Distearate
ingredients.note: A PEG ester of stearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Myrica Cerifera (Bayberry) Fruit Wax
ingredients.note: Bayberry (myrtle) fruit wax used as an emollient and structuring wax. Well tolerated as a plant wax emollient.

ingredients.name: Sucrose Polybehenate
ingredients.note: A sucrose ester of behenic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyvinyl Acetate
ingredients.note: An inert synthetic film-forming polymer used in masks, mascara and adhesives. Chemically stable and benign.

ingredients.name: Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Extract
ingredients.note: Rosemary extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical. Rosemary is a recognized fragrance/contact sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Lanolin Oil
ingredients.note: The liquid fraction of lanolin (wool wax) used as an emollient and skin-conditioning oil. Generally well tolerated, though lanolin can occasionally sensitize.

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat) Germ Extract
ingredients.note: Wheat germ extract used as a vitamin-rich skin-conditioning botanical. May pose allergen/sensitizer concern for wheat-sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Hordeum Vulgare (Barley) Extract
ingredients.note: Barley extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. May pose allergen/sensitizer concern for grain-sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Tricaprylyl Citrate
ingredients.note: A citric acid ester of caprylyl alcohol used as a synthetic emollient. Well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Stearoyl Stearate
ingredients.note: A fatty-acid ester used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Orange 5 Lake
ingredients.note: An aluminum lake of the D&C Orange 5 dye used as a cosmetic colorant. Approved for use as an external colorant.

ingredients.name: Hypnea Musciformis Extract
ingredients.note: Red algae extract used as a film-forming, moisturizing botanical. As a marine plant extract it carries some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Magnolia Grandiflora Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Magnolia bark extract used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Plant extracts carry some sensitizing potential.

ingredients.name: Potassium Gluconate
ingredients.note: The potassium salt of gluconic acid used as a chelating and skin-conditioning agent. Inert and benign.

ingredients.name: Fish (Pisces) Collagen
ingredients.note: Collagen protein derived from fish used as a moisturizing, film-forming skin-conditioning agent. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Ricinoleate
ingredients.note: A cetyl ester of ricinoleic (castor) acid used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Moringa Oil/Hydrogenated Moringa Oil Esters
ingredients.note: Esters derived from moringa oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Well tolerated as a plant-derived emollient.

ingredients.name: Copper Powder
ingredients.note: Finely divided metallic copper (CI 77400), an approved cosmetic colorant/pigment used in color cosmetics. Considered safe at cosmetic use levels — treated like other mineral colorants (cf. Bronze Powder).

ingredients.name: Magnesium Salicylate
ingredients.note: A salicylate salt used in skincare as a conditioning/exfoliating BHA-type agent. Salicylates can mildly sensitize and are best limited in leave-on use (consistent with Sodium Salicylate).

ingredients.name: PEG-4 Dilaurate
ingredients.note: A PEG ester of lauric acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Lauryl Methyl Gluceth-10 Hydroxypropyldimonium Chloride
ingredients.note: A quaternary ammonium (quat) conditioning agent used for hair and skin substantivity. Quaternary ammonium compounds carry sensitizer/irritant concern.

ingredients.name: Dipotassium EDTA
ingredients.note: A chelating agent that sequesters metal ions to stabilize formulations. Used at low levels and generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: C12-15 Alkyl Octanoate
ingredients.note: An ester of octanoic acid with C12-15 alcohols used as a light synthetic emollient. Well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Panthenyl Triacetate
ingredients.note: An acetylated derivative of panthenol (provitamin B5) used as a skin- and hair-conditioning agent. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Farnesyl Acetate
ingredients.note: An acetate ester of farnesol used as a fragrance and skin-conditioning ingredient. As a farnesol-related fragrance material it carries fragrance-allergen potential.

ingredients.name: Phenyl Methicone
ingredients.note: A phenyl-substituted silicone used as an emollient and film former imparting slip and gloss. Inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Hydroxylated Lanolin
ingredients.note: A hydroxylated form of lanolin used as an emollient and emulsifier. Generally well tolerated, though lanolin can occasionally sensitize.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Sesquiisostearate
ingredients.note: A polyglycerol ester of isostearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Lanolin Wax
ingredients.note: The solid wax fraction of lanolin used as an emollient and structuring agent. Generally well tolerated, though lanolin can occasionally sensitize.

ingredients.name: Stearamide AMP
ingredients.note: A fatty amide of aminomethyl propanol used as an emulsion stabilizer and viscosity agent. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: PEG-20 Sorbitan Beeswax
ingredients.note: A PEG/sorbitan derivative of beeswax used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Arctostaphylos Uva Ursi (Bearberry) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of bearberry (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) leaves used as a skin-conditioning/brightening botanical; it contains arbutin (5-15%), a hydroquinone precursor used for skin-lightening, which raises a precautionary concern consistent with how arbutin is treated.

sources.label: PMC: Composition and antioxidant potential of A. uva-ursi leaf extracts

ingredients.name: Myrothamnus Flabellifolia (Resurrection Plant) Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of the desiccation-tolerant African 'resurrection plant', rich in trehalose and antioxidant polyphenols; used as a hydrating, barrier-protecting skin-conditioning botanical with no specific safety concern.

sources.label: PMC: Antioxidant, cytotoxicity, antibacterial effects of Myrothamnus flabellifolius leaves and stem

ingredients.name: Myristoyl Pentapeptide-8
ingredients.note: Synthetic lipopeptide (myristic acid conjugated to pentapeptide-8) used as a skin-conditioning anti-aging active to support dermal proteins; a benign cosmetic peptide with no specific safety concern.

sources.label: PMC: Effects of KTTKS matrikine peptide analogues on cytotoxicity and proteolytic activity

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-3
ingredients.note: Acetylated four-amino-acid peptide used as a skin/scalp-conditioning active that stimulates extracellular-matrix proteins (collagen, laminin); a benign cosmetic peptide with no specific safety concern.

sources.label: PMC: Topical Alternatives for Hair Loss (reviews acetyl tetrapeptide-3 / Capixyl)

ingredients.name: Trifolium Pratense (Clover) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of red clover flowers used as a botanical conditioning agent; it is rich in isoflavone phytoestrogens (biochanin A, formononetin) that bind estrogen receptors, raising an endocrine-disruption concern.

sources.label: PubMed: Estrogenic activity of red clover (Trifolium pratense) extracts in MCF-7 cells
PubMed: Red clover isoflavone biochanin A inhibits aromatase

ingredients.name: C13-14 Isoalkane
ingredients.note: Highly refined synthetic branched hydrocarbon used as a lightweight emollient and solvent; CIR reviewed isoparaffins/isoalkanes as safe with low irritation and sensitization potential.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Isoparaffins as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Malus Domestica Fruit Cell Culture Extract
aliases: Malus Domestica Fruit Cell Culture Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from cultured cells of a Swiss apple variety used as an anti-aging skin-conditioning active (plant cell-culture extract); a benign cosmetic ingredient with no specific safety concern.

sources.label: EU CosIng: Malus Domestica Fruit Cell Culture Extract (REF 86123)

ingredients.name: Olea Europaea (Olive) Callus Culture Lysate
aliases: Olea Europaea Olive Callus Culture Lysate
ingredients.note: Lysate of cultured olive (Olea europaea) callus/stem cells used as a skin-conditioning active; a benign plant cell-culture ingredient with no specific safety concern.

sources.label: PMC: Genetic stability in olive (Olea europaea) callus-induced shoots

ingredients.name: Shellac
ingredients.note: Natural resin secreted by the lac insect used as a film-forming/fixative agent in nail, hair and makeup products; the CIR safety assessment found cosmetic-grade shellac safe up to 6% with no measurable irritation or sensitization (rare contact-allergy reports exist but are not the cosmetic-grade norm).

sources.label: CIR: Final Report on the Safety Assessment of Shellac (1986)

ingredients.name: 2-Methylbutyric Acid
ingredients.note: A short-chain branched carboxylic acid used as a fragrance/flavor volatile in cosmetics. The RIFM fragrance ingredient safety assessment found no skin-sensitization or systemic-toxicity concern at fragrance-use levels.

ingredients.name: Acetylated Lanolin
ingredients.note: An acetylated derivative of lanolin used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. The CIR Expert Panel concluded lanolin and its derivatives are safe as used in cosmetic products.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Lanolin and derivatives

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Dimethylaminoethyl Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic acrylate copolymer used as a film former, hair fixative and viscosity/texture agent in cosmetics. The CIR has reviewed acrylates copolymers as safe as used in cosmetic products.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers

ingredients.name: Acrylic Acid/Isobutyl Acrylate/Isobornyl Acrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: A high-molecular-weight acrylate copolymer used as a film former and binder providing wear and texture in cosmetics. As a cured polymer it is non-absorbing and CIR-reviewed acrylate copolymers are considered safe as used.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers

ingredients.name: Acrylonitrile/Methyl Methacrylate/Vinylidene Chloride Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic copolymer used as the shell of expandable microspheres that provide bulking, opacifying and texture in cosmetics. The fully polymerized material is inert and used as a bulking/film agent without residual-monomer concern at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Alcohol
ingredients.note: Denatured or ethyl alcohol (ethanol) used as a solvent, astringent and volatile carrier in cosmetics. Ethanol is well characterized and considered safe as used as a cosmetic solvent.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alcohol Denat.

ingredients.name: Aletris Farinosa Root Extract
ingredients.note: A botanical extract from the roots of Aletris farinosa (true unicorn) used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant agent in cosmetics. Used at low levels as a botanical extract with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Allyl Stearate/VA Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic copolymer of allyl stearate and vinyl acetate used as a film former and viscosity/texture agent in cosmetics. As a polymeric film former it is considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Aniba Rosaeodora (Rosewood) Wood Oil
ingredients.note: An essential oil distilled from the wood of Aniba rosaeodora (rosewood), rich in linalool, used as a fragrance and skin-conditioning agent. Used within IFRA fragrance limits, it is considered safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Barosma Betulina Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: An essential oil steam-distilled from the leaves of Barosma (Agathosma) betulina, or buchu, used as a fragrance and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. Used at low fragrance levels within IFRA standards.

sources.label: EFSA evaluation of buchu leaf oil (PMC8902667)

ingredients.name: Bellis Perennis (Daisy) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: A botanical extract of common daisy (Bellis perennis) flowers used as a skin-conditioning and brightening agent in cosmetics. Used at low levels as a botanical extract with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Benzaldehyde
ingredients.note: An aromatic aldehyde used as a fragrance and flavor volatile in cosmetics. The CIR Expert Panel concluded Benzaldehyde is safe as used, with sensitization concern mitigated when used within IFRA concentration standards.

ingredients.name: Boswellia Serrata Resin Extract
ingredients.note: A resin extract from Boswellia serrata (Indian frankincense) used as a skin-conditioning and soothing agent in cosmetics. Used at low levels as a botanical extract with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Brassica Campestris/Aleurites Fordi Oil Copolymer
ingredients.note: A copolymer derived from rapeseed (Brassica campestris) and tung (Aleurites fordii) oils used as a film former and emollient/texture agent in cosmetics. As a plant-oil-derived polymer it is considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Nut Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of shea (Butyrospermum parkii) nuts used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. Shea-derived ingredients are CIR-reviewed and considered safe as used.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea)-derived ingredients

ingredients.name: C30-45 Alkyldimethylsilyl Polypropylsilsesquioxane
ingredients.note: A silicone resin/wax used as a film former and emollient that improves water resistance and texture in cosmetics. As an inert high-molecular-weight silicone it is considered safe as used.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Silsesquioxane silicones

ingredients.name: C9-11 Pareth-6
ingredients.note: A polyethylene-glycol ether of C9-11 fatty alcohols used as a nonionic surfactant and emulsifier in cosmetics. The CIR has reviewed alkyl PEG ethers (pareths) as safe as used in cosmetic products.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG Ethers

ingredients.name: Cetyl Behenate
ingredients.note: The ester of cetyl alcohol and behenic acid used as an emollient and viscosity-increasing wax in cosmetics. As a fatty-acid ester it is CIR-reviewed and considered safe as used.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters

ingredients.name: Cetyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: A phosphoric acid ester of cetyl alcohol used as an anionic emulsifier and emulsion stabilizer in cosmetics. The CIR has reviewed cetyl phosphate as safe as used in cosmetic products.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Cetyl Phosphate

ingredients.name: Cetyl Stearate
ingredients.note: The ester of cetyl alcohol and stearic acid used as an emollient and thickening wax in cosmetics. As a fatty-acid ester it is CIR-reviewed and considered safe as used.

sources.label: CIR Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters

ingredients.name: Convallaria Majalis Bulb/Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the bulb and root of the lily-of-the-valley plant, used as a skin-conditioning botanical in cosmetics. Used at low cosmetic levels in topical formulations with no notable safety concern flagged.

ingredients.name: Diatomaceous Earth
ingredients.note: A naturally occurring siliceous sedimentary mineral composed of fossilized diatom skeletons, used in cosmetics as an absorbent, bulking, and abrasive/texture agent. Considered safe for topical cosmetic use; inhalation of dry crystalline silica forms is the only handling concern.

ingredients.name: Dibutyl Ethylhexanol Glutamide
ingredients.note: An amino-acid (glutamic acid) derived oil-gelling/thickening agent used to structure anhydrous and oil phases in cosmetics. Used as a texturizer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Diethylhexyl 2,6-Naphthalate
ingredients.note: A naphthalene dicarboxylic acid diester used as an emollient, solvent, and UV-absorber/photostabilizer (notably to stabilize avobenzone) in sunscreens and makeup. Used as a non-active emollient with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Diisostearyl Dimer Dilinoleate
ingredients.note: A high-molecular-weight ester of dimerized linoleic acid and isostearyl alcohol used as an emollient and viscosity/texture agent that imparts a cushiony skin feel. Used as a benign emollient with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Dilinoleic Acid/Butanediol Copolymer
ingredients.note: A polyester copolymer of dimer (dilinoleic) acid and butanediol used as an emollient, film former, and thickening/texture agent in cosmetics. Used as a benign emollient/texturizer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone/Bis-Isobutyl PPG-20 Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A crosslinked silicone-polyether elastomer used as a thickening, texturizing, and sensory (silky slip) agent in cosmetics. Used as a benign silicone texturizer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone/Polyglycerin-3 Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A crosslinked silicone elastomer with polyglycerin groups used as a thickener, emulsion stabilizer, and texture/sensory agent in cosmetics. Used as a benign silicone texturizer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone/Vinyl Dimethicone Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A crosslinked silicone elastomer used as a thickening, film-forming, and texturizing agent that provides a smooth powdery skin feel. The CIR has reviewed dimethicone crosspolymers and considers them safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Dimethylhydroxy Furanone
ingredients.note: A volatile furanone (furaneol) with a sweet caramel/strawberry odor used as a fragrance/flavor component in cosmetics. Used at low levels as a fragrance ingredient with no notable safety concern at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Ethylene/VA Copolymer
ingredients.note: An ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer used as a binder, film former, and viscosity/texture agent in cosmetics such as sticks and gels. Used as an inert polymer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Ethylenediamine/Stearyl Dimer Dilinoleate Copolymer
ingredients.note: A polyamide copolymer of dimer (dilinoleic) acid derivatives used as a gelling, thickening, and structuring agent for oil phases in cosmetics. Used as a benign texturizer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Gamma-Undecalactone
ingredients.note: A volatile lactone with a peach-like odor used as a fragrance/flavor ingredient in cosmetics. Used at low levels as a fragrance component with no notable safety concern at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Griffonia Simplicifolia Seed Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of the seeds of the West African Griffonia simplicifolia plant, naturally rich in 5-hydroxytryptophan, used as a skin-conditioning/antioxidant botanical in cosmetics. Used at low topical levels with no notable safety concern flagged.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Polydicyclopentadiene
ingredients.note: A saturated hydrocarbon resin used as a film former, gloss agent, and emollient/texture agent in lip and makeup products. Used as an inert hydrocarbon polymer with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Inulin Lauryl Carbamate
ingredients.note: A modified polysaccharide (inulin) with grafted lauryl carbamate groups used as an emulsion stabilizer and o/w emulsifier in cosmetics. Used as a benign emulsifier with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus/Arundinaria Gigantea Leaf Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: A filtrate produced by Lactobacillus fermentation of giant cane (Arundinaria gigantea) leaf, used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant active in cosmetics. Used at low topical levels with no notable safety concern flagged.

ingredients.name: Lauryl Lactate
ingredients.note: The lauryl alcohol ester of lactic acid used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent that imparts a light, dry skin feel. The CIR has reviewed alkyl lactates and considers lauryl lactate safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Lavandula Hybrida Grosso Herb Oil
ingredients.note: An essential oil distilled from lavandin (Lavandula hybrida 'Grosso'), used as a fragrance and aromatic botanical in cosmetics. Generally considered safe at cosmetic levels, though it contains naturally occurring fragrance allergens (e.g. linalool) that may sensitize susceptible individuals.

ingredients.name: Lilium Candidum Flower Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of the flowers of the Madonna lily (Lilium candidum), used as a skin-conditioning and soothing botanical in cosmetics. Used at low topical levels with no notable safety concern flagged.

ingredients.name: Limonium Gerberi Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of the whole Limonium gerberi plant (Plumbaginaceae) used as a skin-protecting and hair-conditioning agent in cosmetics. Considered a benign botanical extract with no documented safety concerns at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Magnolia Liliflora Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Botanical extract from the flowers of Magnolia liliflora used as a skin-conditioning and soothing agent in cosmetic formulations. Regarded as a benign botanical with no documented safety concerns at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Mahonia Aquifolium Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Botanical extract from the flowers, leaves and stems of Mahonia aquifolium (Oregon grape) used as a skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. Considered a benign botanical extract with no significant documented safety concerns at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Mauritia Flexuosa Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Fixed oil expressed from the fruit of Mauritia flexuosa (buriti palm), rich in carotenoids and oleic acid, used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Regarded as a benign plant oil with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Meadowfoam Estolide
ingredients.note: Oligomeric ester (estolide) derived from meadowfoam (Limnanthes alba) fatty acids, used as an emollient and skin/hair-conditioning agent. Considered a benign plant-derived emollient with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Morinda Citrifolia Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from the fruit of Morinda citrifolia (noni) used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. Regarded as a benign botanical extract with no documented safety concerns at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Nylon-6/12
ingredients.note: Synthetic copolyamide film-forming/texturizing polymer used as a bulking and viscosity-controlling agent in cosmetics. Reviewed as safe by the CIR; high molecular weight and not skin-absorbed.

ingredients.name: Nylon-611/Dimethicone Copolymer
ingredients.note: Silicone-modified polyamide copolymer used as a film-former and texturizing/viscosity agent in cosmetics. Considered a benign high-molecular-weight polymer with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Citrate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Crosslinked ester polymer of citric acid and octyldodecanol used as an emollient gelling and texturizing agent in cosmetics. Regarded as a benign polymeric ingredient with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil Polyglyceryl-6 Esters
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol esters derived from olive oil fatty acids used as a non-ionic emulsifier and emollient in cosmetics. Considered a benign, well-tolerated emulsifier with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa Cera
ingredients.note: Rice bran wax obtained from Oryza sativa, used as a thickener, emollient and structuring wax in cosmetics. Regarded as a benign plant-derived wax with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: PEG-20
ingredients.note: Polyethylene glycol of average 20 ethylene-oxide units used as a humectant, solvent and emulsion-stabilizing agent in cosmetics. CIR-reviewed PEGs are considered safe in cosmetics as used.

ingredients.name: PEG-200 Glyceryl Stearate
ingredients.note: PEGylated glyceryl stearate ester used as a non-ionic emulsifier and surfactant in cosmetic formulations. PEG glyceryl stearate esters are CIR-reviewed and considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: PEG-30 Glyceryl Stearate
ingredients.note: PEGylated glyceryl stearate ester used as a non-ionic emulsifier and emollient in cosmetics. CIR-reviewed PEG glyceryl stearate esters are considered safe in cosmetics as used.

ingredients.name: PEG/PPG-14/4 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Polyether-modified silicone (dimethicone copolyol) used as a non-ionic emulsifier and surfactant in cosmetics. Considered a benign silicone surfactant with no documented safety concerns at use levels.

ingredients.name: Passiflora Incarnata Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Extract from the fruit of Passiflora incarnata (passionflower) used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant agent in cosmetics. Regarded as a benign botanical extract with no documented safety concerns at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Pentaclethra Macroloba Oil
ingredients.note: Pracaxi oil from the seed of Pentaclethra macroloba, rich in behenic and oleic acids, used as an emollient and skin/hair-conditioning agent. Considered a benign plant oil with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Perlite
ingredients.note: Expanded amorphous volcanic glass (aluminosilicate) used as an absorbent, bulking and abrasive/opacifying agent in cosmetics. Considered a benign inert mineral; not bioavailable through skin.

ingredients.name: Phenethyl Benzoate
ingredients.note: Benzoic acid ester of phenethyl alcohol used as an emollient and fragrance/solvent component in cosmetics. Regarded as a benign emollient ester with no significant documented safety concerns at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Polybutylene Terephthalate
ingredients.note: Synthetic thermoplastic polyester used as a film-forming and texturizing/structuring polymer, often in mascaras and color cosmetics. Considered a benign high-molecular-weight polymer with no documented safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Polyester-25
ingredients.note: A synthetic polyester polymer used in cosmetics as a film former and viscosity/texture agent, typically in color cosmetics and long-wear formulas. Considered a benign, non-reactive structural polymer.

ingredients.name: Polymethylsilsesquioxane/Trimethylsiloxysilicate
ingredients.note: A silicone-based film-forming resin blend used as a texturizer and to impart wear/transfer resistance and a soft-focus finish in cosmetics. Silicone resins of this type are regarded as inert and well tolerated on skin.

ingredients.name: Polyvinyl Laurate
ingredients.note: The polymerized lauric-acid vinyl ester used in cosmetics as a film former and binding/texture agent. It is a non-reactive polymer considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Quaternium-22
ingredients.note: A quaternary ammonium compound used as an antistatic and conditioning agent in hair and skin products, also providing humectant-like film conditioning. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Raphanus Sativus (Radish) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A plant oil expressed from radish (Raphanus sativus) seeds, used in cosmetics as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Plant seed oils of this type are considered benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Rayon
ingredients.note: Regenerated cellulose fiber used in cosmetics (notably mascaras) as a bulking/fiber agent to add length and volume. As an inert cellulose-based fiber it is regarded as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Rosa Centifolia Flower Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of cabbage rose (Rosa centifolia) flowers used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning and fragrance/botanical agent. Generally considered safe, though the inherent fragrance constituents may contribute allergen content.

ingredients.name: Rubus Fruticosus (Blackberry) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: A plant oil from blackberry (Rubus fruticosus) seeds, rich in essential fatty acids, used in cosmetics as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Considered a benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Sargassum Pacificum Thallus Extract
ingredients.note: An extract from the thallus of the brown seaweed Sargassum pacificum used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning/humectant botanical agent. Marine algae extracts of this type are regarded as benign cosmetic ingredients.

ingredients.name: Schizandra Chinensis Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of the fruit of Schisandra chinensis (variant spelling Schizandra), used in cosmetics as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical agent. Considered a benign plant extract for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Sodium Saccharin
ingredients.note: The sodium salt of saccharin, an artificial sweetener used in cosmetics chiefly as a flavoring agent in lip products and toothpastes. It is widely used and considered safe at cosmetic-use levels.

ingredients.name: Stearoyl Glutamic Acid
ingredients.note: An amino-acid-derived (glutamic acid acylated with stearic acid) surfactant used as an emulsifier and surface-conditioning agent in cosmetics. Acyl glutamate ingredients are mild and considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: A wax ester of stearyl alcohol and palmitic acid used in cosmetics as an emollient and viscosity/texture agent. CIR reviewed alkyl esters of this type and found them safe as used.

ingredients.name: Trans-2-Hexenal
ingredients.note: An unsaturated aldehyde (leaf-aldehyde) used as a fragrance/flavor ingredient in cosmetics. It is permitted under SCCS review subject to restriction in EU Annex III, with IFRA recommending low concentration limits.

ingredients.name: Triisopropanolamine
ingredients.note: An alkanolamine used in cosmetics as a pH adjuster/neutralizer and emulsion stabilizer. The CIR Expert Panel concluded it is safe in the present practices of use and concentration.

ingredients.name: Tris-BHT Mesitylene
ingredients.note: A hindered-phenol (BHT-derived) antioxidant used in cosmetics to prevent oxidation and rancidity of formulas and to stabilize active ingredients. Used at low levels and considered a benign formulation stabilizer.

ingredients.name: VP/DMAPA Acrylates Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic copolymer of vinylpyrrolidone, dimethylaminopropylamine and acrylates used in cosmetics as a film former and hair/skin fixative. As a high-molecular-weight polymer it is considered safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Vaccinium Macrocarpon (Cranberry) Fruit Juice
ingredients.note: The juice of cranberry (Vaccinium macrocarpon) fruit, used in cosmetics as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning botanical agent. Considered a benign plant-derived ingredient.

ingredients.name: Zinc Sulfate
ingredients.note: A mineral zinc salt used in cosmetics as an astringent and skin-conditioning agent. The CIR Expert Panel reviewed zinc salts and found zinc sulfate safe as used in cosmetic products.

ingredients.name: 1,3-Bis(N-2-(Hydroxyethyl) Palmitoylamino)-2-Hydroxy Propane
ingredients.note: A synthetic pseudo-ceramide (ceramide-mimetic lipid) used as a skin-conditioning emollient that reinforces the stratum corneum lipid barrier. Considered a benign, well-tolerated ingredient at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Acacia Decurrens Flower Cera
ingredients.note: A natural floral wax (cera) obtained from Acacia decurrens flowers, used as a structuring and emollient agent in balms and stick formulations. Regarded as a benign botanical wax with no notable safety concerns at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Carnitine HCl
ingredients.note: The acetylated derivative of the amino-acid-related compound carnitine, used as a skin-conditioning and energizing active in cosmetics. Considered safe and well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Adenosine Phosphate
ingredients.note: A nucleotide (adenosine monophosphate) used as a skin-conditioning and smoothing active that supports cellular energy pathways. Recognized as safe and non-irritating at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of the aloe vera plant used as a soothing, humectant skin-conditioning agent. The CIR Expert Panel concluded Aloe barbadensis-derived ingredients are safe in cosmetics at current use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Polysaccharides
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide fraction (e.g., acemannan) isolated from aloe vera leaf, used as a film-forming, soothing, and moisturizing skin-conditioning agent. Considered safe and non-irritating at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Betula Alba (Birch) Bark Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of white birch (Betula alba) bark, rich in betulin/betulinic acid, used as a soothing and skin-conditioning botanical. Regarded as safe and well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Bromelain
ingredients.note: A proteolytic enzyme complex derived from pineapple (Ananas comosus), used as a gentle enzymatic exfoliant and skin-conditioning active. Considered safe at cosmetic concentrations when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea Butter)
ingredients.note: A fatty plant butter expressed from the nuts of the shea tree (Butyrospermum parkii), used as a rich occlusive emollient and skin-conditioning agent. The CIR Expert Panel found shea butter ingredients safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: C10-30 Cholesterol/Lanosterol Esters
ingredients.note: A wool-wax (lanolin)-derived complex of cholesterol and lanosterol esterified with C10-30 fatty acids, used as a barrier-replenishing emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Because it is a lanolin/wool-derivative, it carries a recognized potential for contact sensitization in susceptible individuals.

ingredients.name: Cellulose Acetate Butyrate
ingredients.note: A mixed cellulose ester used as a film-forming and viscosity-controlling polymer in nail products and color cosmetics. Considered an inert, benign polymer with no notable safety concerns at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Lactate
ingredients.note: The cetyl alcohol ester of lactic acid, used as a lightweight, non-greasy emollient and skin-conditioning agent. The CIR has reviewed lactate esters and found them safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone Crosspolymer-3
ingredients.note: A crosslinked silicone elastomer used as a texture-modifying, mattifying, and skin-conditioning agent that provides a smooth velvety feel. Considered inert and safe at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone/PEG-10 Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A PEG-modified crosslinked silicone elastomer used as an emulsifier and texturizing agent that thickens and stabilizes water-in-silicone emulsions. Regarded as inert and safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone/PEG-10/15 Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: A PEG-modified crosslinked silicone elastomer used as an emulsifier and texture-enhancing agent in emulsions and color cosmetics. Considered inert and safe at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Ethylene/Acrylic Acid Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic copolymer of ethylene and acrylic acid used as a film former, viscosity builder, and stabilizer in cosmetics. The CIR has reviewed ethylene/acrylic acid copolymers and found them safe as used.

ingredients.name: Ethylene/Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: A synthetic ethylene/methacrylate copolymer used as a film former and bulking/texturizing agent in cosmetics. Considered an inert, benign polymer with no notable safety concerns at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Ext. Violet 2
ingredients.note: An anthraquinone violet dye (CI 60730 / Acid Violet 43) approved as an FDA-certified colorant for external cosmetic use only. Reviewed by FDA and the SCCS and considered safe for external application at permitted levels.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Behenate
ingredients.note: The glycerol ester of behenic acid, used as an emollient, emulsion stabilizer, and viscosity/texture-controlling agent. The CIR found glyceryl esters of this type safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Distearate
ingredients.note: The diester of glycerin and stearic acid, used as an emulsifier, emollient, and opacifying/thickening agent in creams and lotions. The CIR reviewed glyceryl stearate esters and found them safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Ethylhexanoate/Stearate/Adipate
ingredients.note: A mixed glyceryl ester of ethylhexanoic, stearic, and adipic acids, used as an emollient and skin-conditioning/texturizing agent. Considered a benign ester emollient with no notable safety concerns at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Glyceryl Linoleate
ingredients.note: A monoester of glycerin and linoleic (omega-6) fatty acid used as an emollient, skin-conditioning agent, and nonionic emulsifier. CIR reviewed glyceryl monoesters and concluded they are safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Glycine
ingredients.note: The simplest amino acid, used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent, buffer, and humectant. CIR assessed glycine and its salts as safe as used in cosmetic formulations.

ingredients.name: Hamamelis Virginiana (Witch Hazel)
ingredients.note: An extract/distillate of the witch hazel shrub used as an astringent, skin-conditioning, and soothing agent owing to its tannin content. CIR concluded Hamamelis virginiana-derived ingredients are safe in the present practices of use and concentration.

ingredients.name: Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seedcake
ingredients.note: The solid residue (cake) remaining after expression of oil from sunflower seeds, used as an emollient, abrasive, and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found Helianthus annuus (sunflower)-derived ingredients safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Caesalpinia Spinosa Gum
ingredients.note: A hydrolysate of tara gum (a galactomannan polysaccharide from the tara tree seed) used as a film-forming, skin-conditioning, and viscosity-controlling agent. Plant-polysaccharide derivative regarded as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Rice Extract
ingredients.note: A hydrolysate of Oryza sativa (rice) yielding peptides, amino acids, and sugars used as a skin- and hair-conditioning and film-forming agent. CIR reviewed rice-derived ingredients and concluded they are safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyethylpiperazine Ethane Sulfonic Acid
ingredients.note: HEPES, a zwitterionic sulfonic-acid buffering agent used in cosmetics to control pH and as a mild keratolytic/exfoliant supporting cell renewal. Used at low levels as a buffer and considered safe in cosmetic formulation.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Tetrahydropyrantriol
ingredients.note: A synthetic xylose-derived sugar (Pro-Xylane) used as a skin-conditioning anti-aging active to support glycosaminoglycan synthesis. Considered a benign, well-tolerated cosmetic active.

ingredients.name: Isoeicosane
ingredients.note: A branched synthetic C20 hydrocarbon (isoparaffin) used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent giving a light, non-greasy slip. CIR found synthetic isoparaffin hydrocarbons safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Laminaria Saccharina Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of sugar kelp (a brown seaweed) used as a skin-conditioning and soothing agent rich in polysaccharides and minerals. Marine botanical extract regarded as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Lanolin Alcohol
ingredients.note: A fraction of lanolin (wool wax alcohols, including sterols) used as an emollient, emulsifier, and skin-conditioning agent. CIR and dermatology literature recognize wool/lanolin alcohols as documented contact allergens capable of causing allergic contact dermatitis, especially on damaged skin.

ingredients.name: Lauryl PEG-9 Polymethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A silicone-polyether (PEG-modified dimethicone) surfactant used as a water-in-silicone emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Silicone polymer emulsifier considered safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Methoxy Amodimethicone/Silsesquioxane Copolymer
ingredients.note: An amino-functional silicone copolymer used as a hair- and skin-conditioning and film-forming agent providing smoothness and slip. Silicone polymer regarded as safe in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Methyl Perfluorobutyl Ether
ingredients.note: A volatile hydrofluoroether (perfluoro ether) used as a fast-evaporating, non-residue carrier solvent in cosmetics. Used as an inert volatile solvent considered safe at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Methyl Perfluoroisobutyl Ether
ingredients.note: A volatile hydrofluoroether isomer (perfluoro ether) used as a fast-drying, low-residue carrier solvent in cosmetic formulations. Inert volatile solvent regarded as safe at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Morus Bombycis (Mulberry) Root Extract
ingredients.note: An extract of mulberry root used as a skin-conditioning and brightening agent, valued for tyrosinase-inhibiting flavonoids. Botanical extract regarded as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Octadecenedioic Acid
ingredients.note: A monounsaturated C18 dicarboxylic acid (biofermented from oleic acid) used as a skin-brightening and sebum-regulating active that lowers tyrosinase expression via PPAR binding. Reported non-cytotoxic and non-photosensitizing at cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Oleth-3 Phosphate
ingredients.note: A phosphate ester of ethoxylated oleyl alcohol used as an anionic emulsifier and surfactant. CIR reviewed PEG alkyl-ether phosphates and found them safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Oleyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: An unsaturated C18 fatty alcohol used as an emollient, emulsifier, and skin-conditioning agent providing slip and spreadability. CIR concluded oleyl alcohol is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: PEG-5 Glyceryl Triisostearate
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated glyceryl triester of isostearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier and emollient/skin-conditioning agent. PEG glyceryl fatty-ester reviewed by CIR as safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: PEG-7 Trimethylolpropane Coconut Ether
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated trimethylolpropane ether of coconut-derived fatty alcohols used as a nonionic emulsifier and refatting/skin-conditioning agent. PEG ether considered safe as used in cosmetic formulations.

ingredients.name: PEG-9
ingredients.note: PEG-9 is polyethylene glycol with an average of 9 ethylene oxide units, used in cosmetics as a humectant, solvent and surfactant/emulsifying agent. The CIR has reviewed PEGs and found them safe for use in cosmetics at the concentrations used.

ingredients.name: PPG-15 Stearyl Ether
ingredients.note: PPG-15 Stearyl Ether is a polypropylene glycol ether of stearyl alcohol used as an emollient, skin-conditioning agent and emulsifier in cosmetics. The CIR Expert Panel concluded PPG stearyl ethers are safe as used in cosmetic formulations.

ingredients.name: PVP/Hexadecene Copolymer
ingredients.note: PVP/Hexadecene Copolymer is a film-forming polymer of vinylpyrrolidone and hexadecene used as a binder, viscosity agent and water-resistance/film former in cosmetics. The CIR reviewed PVP and its copolymers and found them safe as used.

ingredients.name: Padina Pavonica Thallus Extract
ingredients.note: Padina Pavonica Thallus Extract is an extract of the Mediterranean brown alga Padina pavonica, rich in polyphenols and polysaccharides, used as a skin-conditioning agent. It is a benign botanical active with no significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Perfluorononyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Perfluorononyl Dimethicone is a fluorinated silicone polymer used as a skin-conditioning agent and emollient that imparts water/oil resistance and smooth feel in cosmetics. It is considered a benign, inert film-forming silicone.

ingredients.name: Phloretin
ingredients.note: Phloretin is a dihydrochalcone polyphenol found in apple tree leaves and bark, used in cosmetics as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. It is regarded as a benign antioxidant active with no notable safety concerns at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Piptadenia Colubrina Peel Extract
ingredients.note: Piptadenia Colubrina Peel Extract is a botanical extract from the bark/peel of the Piptadenia colubrina tree, used as a skin-conditioning and barrier-supporting agent that promotes hydration. It is a benign botanical active with no significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Polyphosphorylcholine Glycol Acrylate
ingredients.note: Polyphosphorylcholine Glycol Acrylate is a biocompatible phosphorylcholine-bearing acrylate polymer used as a film former and moisturizing/conditioning agent in cosmetics. It is a benign, inert polymer with high skin compatibility.

ingredients.name: Propylene Glycol Dicaprate
ingredients.note: Propylene Glycol Dicaprate is the diester of propylene glycol and capric acid used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. The CIR reviewed propylene glycol diesters and found them safe as used.

ingredients.name: Pyridoxine Dipalmitate
ingredients.note: Pyridoxine Dipalmitate is a fat-soluble dipalmitate ester derivative of pyridoxine (vitamin B6) used as a skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. The CIR reviewed pyridoxine and its derivatives and found them safe as used.

ingredients.name: Saccharomyces Lysate Extract
ingredients.note: Saccharomyces Lysate Extract is a yeast-derived ferment lysate rich in amino acids, peptides and minerals, used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant active in cosmetics. It is a benign ferment ingredient with no significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Sigesbeckia Orientalis (St. Paul's Wort) Extract
ingredients.note: Sigesbeckia Orientalis Extract is a botanical extract of the St. Paul's wort herb used as a soothing skin-conditioning agent in cosmetics. It is a benign botanical active with no notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Sodium Cocamidopropyl PG-Dimonium Chloride Phosphate
ingredients.note: Sodium Cocamidopropyl PG-Dimonium Chloride Phosphate is a coconut-derived amphoteric/zwitterionic surfactant used as a mild cleansing, conditioning and antistatic agent in cosmetics. It is considered a benign mild surfactant.

ingredients.name: Sodium Myristoyl Sarcosinate
ingredients.note: Sodium Myristoyl Sarcosinate is the sodium salt of the myristic acid amide of sarcosine, used as a mild anionic surfactant, cleansing and foaming agent. The CIR reviewed sarcosinate surfactants and found them safe as used when nitrosating agents are absent.

ingredients.name: Sodium Polyaspartate
ingredients.note: Sodium Polyaspartate is the sodium salt of polyaspartic acid, a biodegradable amino-acid polymer used as a humectant, hydrating and skin-conditioning/chelating agent in cosmetics. It is a benign, biodegradable ingredient with no significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Steareth-10
ingredients.note: Steareth-10 is a polyethylene glycol ether of stearyl alcohol (10 EO units) used as a nonionic surfactant and emulsifier in cosmetics. The CIR reviewed the steareth series and found them safe as used in cosmetic formulations.

ingredients.name: Taurine
ingredients.note: Taurine is a naturally occurring sulfur-containing amino acid used in cosmetics as an antioxidant, humectant and skin-conditioning agent. It is a benign, well-tolerated ingredient with no notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat Bran) Extract
ingredients.note: Triticum Vulgare (Wheat Bran) Extract is a botanical extract from wheat bran used as a skin-conditioning, soothing and antioxidant agent in cosmetics. It is a benign botanical, though products are not gluten-free for those with relevant sensitivities.

ingredients.name: Yellow 10 Lake
ingredients.note: Yellow 10 Lake is the aluminum lake of D&C Yellow No. 10 (CI 47005), an FDA-listed color additive permitted for use in drugs and cosmetics subject to batch certification. It is a permitted cosmetic colorant with eye-area-use restrictions.
